author: suboohi
title: SLC29-W6// "Chicken With Rice"
text:
![1000398010.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWDR1XKyAeqZLpSoJAQnRCaidRHEDrwHj8oWTqjPnp1LZ/1000398010.jpg)

Greetings to everyone!!!

Welcome to last week of Steemit Learning Challenge Season 29. I would like to thank @steemitblog for giving me this opportunity. In this course we will explore the different chicken recipes every week, their ingredients, their cooking style, and a food recipe that is the most popular.
Introduction
Chicken is one of the most versatile and widely consumed proteins globally, offering endless possibilities for culinary creativity; from spicy curries and smoky barbecues to tender roasts and zesty stir-fries, chicken adapts effortlessly to diverse flavors and cooking techniques, making it a staple in kitchens worldwide, where methods like grilling, braising, frying, and slow-cooking transform simple ingredients into mouthwatering dishes that cater to every palate and occasion. In this season we'll explore different cooking techniques to make different delicious recipe.
Chicken With Rice
There are a lot of recipes where chicken is cooked with rice. In every cuisine there are many varieties of cooking chicken and rice. Rice is served with many recipes as a side, but today in this week we only consider the recipe in which chicken is cooked with rice. In Pakistani culture, biryani is popular; on the Arab side, bukhari; in Afghanistan and Iran, polao; and in African cuisine, jollof rice and arroz chino in Venezuela are popular recipes with chicken. This week you are again free to make any chicken and rice recipe that you like.
Chicken Biryani
Ingredients :
![1000334091.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXzuaiNDc9JWZi6X34KfrrmaJa1f7fvQa17F6ivsbvLSU/1000334091.jpg)| -|
Chicken 1kg| -| Rice 850g| Garlic ginger paste 2tbsp| Oil 5-6tbsp| Chilli powder 1.5 tbsp | Coriander powder 2tbsp| Chilli flakes 1tsp| Salt as per taste | Cumin seeds 1tsp | Turmeric powder 1tsp| Garam masala | Fried onion 4-5tbsp| Tomatoes 5 medium | Yougart | Food color |
Making :
Biryani is done in three steps ,first chicken masala is made then rice is boiled then mixing of chicken masala is done to make Biryani. ***Step 1:*** * Take oil in a pan and fry chicken in it until its colour changes then add garlic ginger paste in it and fry it for 1-2 minutes. Slit green chillies into 4 pieces and fry it with chicken. Then add all dry spices in pan and sotey it until good aroma of spices come out. ![1000334094.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSzzqNszBdCWFakZcimp5obq7cbkd57Boph5LMsr76Py7/1000334094.jpg)|![1000334095.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbSQVjs5JmxJ7ftsPjYobHdVYHiyUr3ZfAauQFvXaBoDD/1000334095.jpg)|![1000334096.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSY6TMRgyXbPVBjdpXkpPsnXBYbk5nQGZF4HhdryaXepp/1000334096.jpg) -|-|- * Add tomatoes, yougart and fried onions in it and cook until chicken and all soten and oil comes out. Now chicken masala is ready to mixed with rice. ![1000334099.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbmMwr1HMA7fjzaDtoaGj4hKocUgqhSYhAG7pNnKGKU18/1000334099.jpg)|![1000334100.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSvLiFb2KkjucFX7q63EyQrhEs2o83TxAxQ1S1xgPaDXi/1000334100.jpg)|![1000334102.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmaCag4NtJZ37DvanVL4EhXwttz6M41CjcsGFU6QbQwiVn/1000334102.jpg) -|-|- ***Step:*** * Rice is soaked in water for 30 minutes before boiling. Add cardamom, cinnamon and clove in water and keep heating it. After 7-8 minutes add soaked rice in it and boil it. When rice 95% cooked strain it in a strainer. ![1000334103.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQXGVLEW5eh4oNkLf8N6PEjuUJRsWF2W6LZS2B6wxtLnM/1000334103.jpg)|![1000334118.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTV14dL3aGoZGpigMeEQ85SbD6nosnpGxf1xmpin5642V/1000334118.jpg)|![1000334138.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSqieXEg4KSRo5ppHzQmF99XGGpnpmZbPvpQPTK3MWgXm/1000334138.jpg) -|-|- ***Step3:*** * In a pan add 1tbsp of oil then add a base layer of boiled rice, then spread chicken masala over rice . Then make another layer of chicken masala over it then spread rice over it . Then add food color at the top of lyer. Add 1-2 tbsp of oil on it. Place lid over pan and cook it for 7-8 mins until rice completely cooked. ![1000334139.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcR3pgMyqZoT7uzyFBVTHuBjX2afrzCVuF95gYGuSvHdW/1000334139.jpg)|![1000334140.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTnMNBGX75hhEW3LEh2zSp9CVzjxWkGvvmCRWvP6v6bhR/1000334140.jpg)|![1000334141.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmeUi1UCcTigXwsnURLqkXvqdedJAqNhps7MvDwp4mRsGr/1000334141.jpg)|![1000334144.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQMuL59atPEobHQtudorCqRPeLL92bFZ1jx5wA2EpVbzZ/1000334144.jpg) -|-|-|- Mixed chicken masala with rice . Delicious chicken Biryani is ready to serve. ![1000391230.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZmHqeJrv6ZR8JfHk2h4Ti6qqRcA4ZewPj4DqYdFx5TYF/1000391230.jpg)| |- ![1000391233.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmY5TxGgmnCJ93jbsmUXgLnhhDNyqYz8AbDzT7ckuqkowi/1000391233.jpg)| -|
For students task
1. Have you made chicken with rice before? What make it different from other recipes. (1 Mark) 2. Make the chicken recipes that is shared in the course or anyone that you like , with a detailed step-by-step procedure along with images. (6 Marks) 3. List the prices of the ingredients you buy for making the recipe. (prices in your local currency and in STEEM).(2 Mark) 4. Make a good Presentation of your recipe. (1 Mark) ##
Rules and criteria:
✓ The post can be written on your blog or any community. ✓ The title of your post must be: SLC29-W6// "Chicken With Rice" ✓ Use your own images, plagiarism and use of AI are prohibited, the publication must contain a minimum of 300 words. ✓ Make sure to use the tags #worldofchicken-s29w6 , #learnwhitsteem #steemexclusive #food and your country tag. (example, #pakistan) ✓ Include a handwritten identification sign next to your food, containing the user name and date . ✓ Invite at least three friends to participate: @lirvic , @dasudi , @sualeha . ✓ Leave the link to your post in the comments section. ✓ Any language is accepted. ✓ Promote on social networks like X. ✓ This contest will be open for 7 days starting at 00:00 UTC on Monday, February 16, 2026, 00:00 UTC to Sunday, February 22, 2026, 23:59 UTC.
Rewards:
SC01/SC02 will evaluate posts across all participating teaching teams. Upvotes are not guaranteed for all articles. ***At the end of the week, top 6 users will be nominated based on content quality and presentation not on comment count.*** Best regards @suboohi

author: kiwiscanfly
title: Big Storm⛈️💨 Grid outage 🪫🔋
text:
We have had a major storm for the past 18 odd hours with 100 kilometer winds and plenty of rain, last night as the winds got up i checked the Powerwall battery level only to see it was at 5%🪫.

That was after a cloudy rainy day yesterday, so i decided to charge it up while i could nd by 2am we were back to 100%🔋.
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcxZf3r8eujCN6nnpmwtvM3c99CZrYv8ToroX87GXzdbJ/image.png)

We lost power around 4am this morning and the battery has kept us going nicely, now that the storm has passed mostly we have a grey cloudy day but we are producing power from the solar panels.
![Screenshot_20260216_131234_Tesla.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXNBzTf1t4D3Uvdk4wKFttFPViuNNWZQ7BHi6KKQfQAUf/Screenshot_20260216_131234_Tesla.jpg)
The weather forecast for the upcoming week is looking far nicer.


-----------

In other event i brought a couple of new Mountain bike tires for my ride, the current tire's are almost bald and needing replacing.


I decided to go top of the line and pay $230 each for a couple of MAXXIS 26x4.0 Minion FBF 26x4.0 / FBR 26x4.0.
![s-l1600.webp](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWX73Z17zJbmYHU8jPKay1te5E7sRvhZSKUwmsWRcEN6P/s-l1600.webp)
These tires are similar but one is designed for the front tyre to help with steering etc, the one for the back has a grid pattern that focuses on traction.

These should be here within a week then they will be replacing the current ones.


Right now its stopped raining i feel a walk is in order.


Have a good Monday :)

*all pics are mine*
*smudge approves this post*

comments.author: ubongudofot
comments.body:
>....i checked the Powerwall battery level only to see it was at 5%🪫.

That's poor! 

As at this 2am that the battery was fully charged..., were you using a public utility to charge it...?


Also, if the power was 100% full at 2am and by 4am it discharges at that rate....it could be that  your loads were much heavier or perhaps, the battery is getting weak. Do you experience this often even on days with much sun?

I am doing a research/program on **Tesla** product

replies.author: kiwiscanfly
replies.body:
Maybe i didn't explain it properly LOL

> As at this 2am that the battery was fully charged..., were you using a public utility to charge it...?

yep just the normal power grid to charge it


Powerwall was FULL by 2am and would have sat idle until the grid went down at 4am, then the 100% battery took over and ran the house for the next 15 hours.

> ....i checked the Powerwall battery level only to see it was at 5%🪫.

That's poor!

If we had a bad day for solar generation then the powerwall level will suffer, we had a bad day yesterday for generation

author: companions
title: My Foody Day at Home:  Coconut Rice and delicious Soup
text:
There’s a quiet magic to the rhythm of a day spent in the kitchen, especially when the menu is a comforting bowl of coconut rice paired with a hearty pot of Afang soup. For me, this duo isn’t just a meal; it’s a ritual that ties together family memories, cultural pride, and the simple joy of creating something delicious from scratch.

5:30 am – The First Light

The day begins before the sun has fully risen. I love this early hour because the house is still, the only sound being the soft hum of the fridge. I head straight to the pantry, pull out the bag of long‑grain rice, and give it a quick rinse. As the water runs clear, I can already smell the faint, nutty aroma of the grains—a promise of the creamy coconut flavor that’s about to unfold.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTMmuf3fZyEhDapaqRu4VqyNtJwEMUBj2WGLxyW5kP2vC/image.png)

6:00 am – Coconut Prep

A fresh coconut is the star of today’s rice. I crack it open with a sturdy hammer, careful not to waste any of the tender white flesh. Once the meat is scraped out, I blend it with a splash of warm water, then strain it through a fine muslin cloth. The resulting milky liquid pools into my pot, where I’ll toast the rice.

I heat a generous knob of palm oil, letting it shimmer before adding the rinsed rice. The grains sizzle, coating themselves in oil, and the kitchen fills with a faint, caramelized scent. I pour the coconut milk in, add a pinch of salt, and let the mixture come to a gentle boil. Then the lid goes on, and the rice steams away, turning fluffy and fragrant.

7:30 am – The Soul of Afang Soup

While the rice is doing its quiet work, I turn my attention to Afang soup—a West African classic that never fails to warm both body and spirit. The base is a blend of palm oil, smoked fish, and assorted meats (usually beef and shaki). I start by sautéing diced onions and minced garlic in a generous splash of palm oil until they turn translucent and sweet.

Next, I add the smoked fish and meat, letting them brown and release their smoky essence. A spoonful of ground crayfish follows, intensifying the umami depth. Then comes the star ingredient: Afang leaves (also known as Okazi). I wash them meticulously, chop them roughly, and stir them into the pot, watching the bright green mingle with the rich, reddish broth.

To balance the flavors, I toss in diced tomatoes, a splash of fermented locust beans (iru), and a pinch of ground pepper. The soup simmers gently for about twenty minutes, allowing the leaves to soften and the flavors to meld. A final sprinkle of chopped garden eggs and a dash of salt bring everything into harmony.

8:15 am – The Grand Finale

The rice is perfectly cooked—each grain separated, glossy with coconut milk, and subtly sweet. I fluff it with a fork, then spoon a generous serving onto a plate, nestling a ladleful of Afang soup beside it. The steam rises, carrying the tropical scent of coconut and the earthy fragrance of the leaves.

I sit at my kitchen table, a steaming bowl before me, and take a moment to appreciate the simple pleasure of a homemade meal. The coconut rice provides a creamy, slightly sweet base that tempers the robust, peppery notes of the soup. Together, they tell a story of tradition, love, and the quiet satisfaction of a day well spent.

Why This Meal Matters

Cooking coconut rice and Afang soup isn’t just about feeding the body; it’s a daily affirmation of my roots and the people who taught me these recipes. Each stir, each pinch of spice, is a conversation with the past, a celebration of the present, and a hope for future generations to enjoy the same comforting flavors.

So, the next time you’re looking for a “normal” day that feels a little extraordinary, try your hand at this duo. Let the aroma fill your home, and let the rhythm of the kitchen remind you that sometimes the simplest meals are the most profound. Bon appétit!

***

title: "Recipe of the Day, Week No. 97: (Aromatic Bhindi Chicken Delight  )"
text:
## 💐In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful💐 ![Marble Photographic Spice Recipes Instagram Post_20260216_193943_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmY4xPyYAADgnyibHZhuFSRLXfJg56oxj7yZmWEpABpbBS/Marble_Photographic_Spice_Recipes_Instagram_Post_20260216_193943_0000.png) ***Edited by Canva*** ![20250901_113508_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVYagcJLEwteuohPxY78tBXHxgtiSafU5uY2fXPE8btLB/20250901_113508_0000.png) |
Greatings to all Steemit iron-chef !
| |-| |
Hi to our vibrant community and supportive moderators
| |-| ***I am fine. I hope that all my friends are also fine and from the bottom of my heart I pray for all of you that you all are always happy, prosperous and live a very enjoyable life with your family members. Guys we should always pray for everyone. Today I am participating in this contest with a delicious Bhindi chicken recipe. Trust you will like my recipe انشاءاللہ***. ![Untitled design_20250901_113723_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNmgusPLG5r4qCPfzsuTx9qKbdEQjuquLNJEdCYNuqQYm/Untitled_design_20250901_113723_0000.png) |
***Here are the ingredients***:👇
| |-| |Sr|Ingredients| |--|--| |1|Bhindi| |2|Chicken| |3|Onion 🧅| |4|Tomato 🍅| |5|Green coriander| |6|Turmeric| |7|Red chilli| |8|Chilli flakes| |9|Salt 🧂| ![Untitled design_20250901_113723_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNmgusPLG5r4qCPfzsuTx9qKbdEQjuquLNJEdCYNuqQYm/Untitled_design_20250901_113723_0000.png) ## ***Bismillaherahmanuraheem***! > I have brought very tasty and delicious recipe. It is made with very simple spice. But this is really very tasty and delicious. So let me tell you how I have made curry of okra and chicken in a simple way. ![Untitled design_20250901_113723_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNmgusPLG5r4qCPfzsuTx9qKbdEQjuquLNJEdCYNuqQYm/Untitled_design_20250901_113723_0000.png) - As always we first place the pan on the stove. And then add the ghee to it. I did the same and when it started to heat up. I added the chicken to it. I had already washed the chicken.
|![IMG_20260215_135249_226.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPMLYcj8VG4mCfEb6Esc55h8pTvk5Uoq9DWkn3Bfojuga/IMG_20260215_135249_226.jpg)| | - |
|![IMG_20260215_135310_889.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfHnzBnZpJnEbDYikxddzuQa5AQmJmhr9iMEuYBmtLucj/IMG_20260215_135310_889.jpg)| | - |
![IMG_20260215_135619_261.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfB7wcuMTsThTvxjB8LzAPBGudJci6UJh2LATz8cxHo7d/IMG_20260215_135619_261.jpg) ![Untitled design_20250901_113723_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNmgusPLG5r4qCPfzsuTx9qKbdEQjuquLNJEdCYNuqQYm/Untitled_design_20250901_113723_0000.png) - I started frying the chicken thoroughly. I fried the chicken on a low heat so that it was well browned and fried. The chicken was well browned and fried. So I took it out on a plate.
|![IMG_20260215_140342_680.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmchazmv1DmEGn8yavqV6JZW7R9vCezoohKAzSsBu2rLFW/IMG_20260215_140342_680.jpg)| | - |
|![IMG_20260215_140901_174.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTaerA9a32su7h6nDNRizTfzR3SMNbQTBnmUXXT47ShCE/IMG_20260215_140901_174.jpg)| | - |
![Untitled design_20250901_113723_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNmgusPLG5r4qCPfzsuTx9qKbdEQjuquLNJEdCYNuqQYm/Untitled_design_20250901_113723_0000.png) - I had already chopped the onions and I had also chopped the okra. Everything was well washed and clean. I took out the chicken separately. Then I added bhindi to the ghee and started frying them well. I first fry okra because it has lace in it. Therefore we have to finish it off by frying it well. So that our curry can be delicious. I friy it well on low flame. I fried it for about five minutes. After frying it for five minutes the lace inside was completely gone.
|![IMG_20260215_140930_790.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUHbSUntFqYgKB3JjTW64duWUwyJJvbWMBoUm5yiEhNGN/IMG_20260215_140930_790.jpg)| | - |
|![IMG_20260215_141054_178.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSmLPBrYJYJmMeq5uwG2tEFvYqYZK8xiRyWhfYWwNy2Jg/IMG_20260215_141054_178.jpg)| | - |
|![IMG_20260215_141338_143.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUJy66B5N2Egr6vi9fzoXP8BdgcZUP6f3kGqnJqwHpbcy/IMG_20260215_141338_143.jpg)| | - |
|![IMG_20260215_141402_350.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbwPJRVKag7FBsLZYLY3dr8n6f6XUgxZJMbfogDzaC21J/IMG_20260215_141402_350.jpg)| | - |
![Untitled design_20250901_113723_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNmgusPLG5r4qCPfzsuTx9qKbdEQjuquLNJEdCYNuqQYm/Untitled_design_20250901_113723_0000.png) - Well, there are some people here who fry the okra and take it out separately. But I don't cook it that way. After I fry the okra. I add the onions to it. I cook it my way so I like this method better. And then after frying the okra. I added the onions well in it. and fried the onions well for about five to six minutes. The color of the onions also started to change slightly.
|![IMG_20260215_141421_814.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmazpcstR28NPNLYMY9hdF9hz9Z67Vh2BrhtuS9yTCzGhy/IMG_20260215_141421_814.jpg)| | - |
|![IMG_20260215_141457_269.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQfjvTqwZDEvoptBRAcpnnqewr7QF43hGCMrzL5bb4o9t/IMG_20260215_141457_269.jpg)| | - |
![IMG_20260215_141848_519.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNpjy2Zj5wW7vv5PqnNEEyRwKTaGdrnnQBtFj4tup5Y1x/IMG_20260215_141848_519.jpg) ![Untitled design_20250901_113723_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNmgusPLG5r4qCPfzsuTx9qKbdEQjuquLNJEdCYNuqQYm/Untitled_design_20250901_113723_0000.png) - Then I turned the flame down and added the tomatoes cut into small pieces. Then I added spices to it. The spices include red chili powder two chili flakes turmeric and salt. Then I added the fried chicken to it. Mixed it well and left it to cook on low heat. After about 10 minutes my delicious spicy and very simply cooked okra and chicken curry was ready.
|![IMG_20260215_142049_360.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZzq6rGCF8veTe4GjEzrkesu6atQXTxQKLU4tiTKuXA5Z/IMG_20260215_142049_360.jpg)| | - |

author: alexanderpeace
title: Steemit Challenge pitch season 30: Meals and Cultures of the World.
text:
<div class="text-justify">
<div class="pull-left">
</div

I want to thank the Steemit team for the opportunity to apply for this season’s challenge pitch, and this season, my pitch is what I titled **“Meals and Cultures of the World” (Expressing culture through taste)”**.


![IMG_2791.jpeg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUVtqMPR8CBSXa8mK9BR6SdJ6LQzXbmwJEYtuevDRN4cw/IMG_2791.jpeg)


## About me

I joined the platform in 2021 and have served in different capacities in the system and still look forward to serving more and give my best, I am presently a moderator in steem fashion and style community, ladies universe and traveling steem community and also acted as a community curator on different occasions.

## My statistic

Items|statistics
----|---
Reputation | 75.206
CSI| 8.3
Steem power | 10,246.70
Country | Nigeria 

### Challenge Concept

Food is more than nourishment , it is our identity, history, memory, heritage, and culture served on a plate.

This challenge is a global cultural journey that will help us explores how meals are powerful expressions of our human identity.

Across civilizations, cultures, and continents, food has always been a language,  a way we express who we are, where we come from, what we value, and how we live.

**Meals and Cultures of the World is not a cooking competition. It is a cultural documentation challenge that uses food as a lens to explore our;**

* Traditions
* Beliefs
* History
* Migration
* Identity
* Community
* Family
* Heritage
* Lifestyle and 
* Unity

**Each week, the participants will explore one culture and one meal, telling the story behind the food, not just how it is prepared, but what it represents. This challenge connects our tastes to tradition, flavor to faith, meals to memory, and our recipes to our roots.**

Participants are encouraged to express culture through:

* Storytelling
* Photography
* Videography
* Personal experiences
* Research
* Documentation
* Cultural narratives

### The objectives of this challenge 
This challenge is designed to:

* Help  to Promote global cultural awareness and appreciation.
* Encourage cultural storytelling through food.
* Makes for  Preservation and documentation of traditional meals and heritage recipes.
* Will  educate participants on the cultural meanings behind meals.
* Build empathy and understanding between cultures
* Will  Encourage quality content creation
* Strengthen our Steemit ecosystem through creative engagement with other cultures.
* Support the use of Steem Atlas and Steem Video
* Encourage originality, depth, and cultural research
* Transform food posts into cultural archives

### Weekly Structure (6 Weeks)

Each week, we will focuses on ONE culture plus ONE meal, explored deeply through storytelling, cultural explanation, and personal reflection.

**Participants are expected to:**
* Cook the meal
* Eat the meal
* Document the meal
* Research the meal
* Experience it locally
* Explore it digitally
* Present it through video
* Narrate it through writing
Our goal is cultural expression, not perfection.


### Weekly Themes

🖋️ **Week 1: Roots & Identity**
**Theme: Food as Cultural Identity**

**Task: Participants will explore a meal that represents their own culture, tribe, ethnic group, or heritage. This is a personal cultural reflection week.
Participants should tell the story behind the meal:**

* Why is this food important to your people?
* When is it traditionally eaten? Is it festive, ceremonial, daily, or symbolic?
* What does it represent culturally?
* How does it reflect the lifestyle, values, and history of your people?
* What memories do you personally associate with it?

*This week focuses on identity, roots, belonging, and personal heritage.*


🖋️ **Week 2: Across Borders**
**Theme: Discovering Another Culture**

**Task: Participants will explore a culture different from their own and focus on one traditional meal from that culture.**

They should share:

* The origin of the meal.
* The cultural background. The people who eat it.
* The social meaning of the food.
* Ingredients and preparation style.
* What the meal says about that culture’s lifestyle and environment.

*This week encourages cross-cultural learning, curiosity, respect, and global exploration.*

🖋️ **Week 3: Week 3: Festive Foods**
**Theme: Celebration Meals**

**Task: Participants will explore foods eaten during:**

* Cultural festivals
* Religious celebrations
* Weddings
* Naming ceremonies
* National holidays
* Cultural rituals
* Traditional rites

They should explain:

* Why this meal is sacred or special
* The event it is connected to
* The symbolism behind it
* The emotions attached to it
* The traditions surrounding its preparation and sharing.

*This week focuses on food as a carrier of joy, spirituality, celebration, and sacred meaning.*

🖋️ **Week 4: Week 4: Street Food Culture**
**Theme: Everyday Cultural Meals**

**Task: Participants will explore street foods or everyday meals that define the daily life of a people.**

They should document:

* The environment where the food is sold
* The people who prepare it. The community around it
* The social interaction around the meal. (The lifestyle it represents)
* The affordability and accessibility
* The cultural importance of this everyday food

*This week reflects real-life culture, not luxury culture.*


🖋️ **Week 5: Week 5: Home & Heritage**
**Theme: Family Meals and Cultural Transmission**

**Task: Participants will explore meals passed down through:**

* Families
* Generations
* Lineage
* Ancestral traditions
* Oral history
* Cultural inheritance

They should share:

* Who taught them the recipe
* The family history behind it
* The cultural significance
* Why it is preserved
* How food becomes a tool for cultural continuity.

*This week focuses on legacy, memory, inheritance, and preservation.*


🖋️ **Week 6: Unity Through Food**
**Theme: Food as Global Connection**

**Task: Participants will explore:**

* Meals shared across multiple cultures
* Fusion meals
* Migrated foods
* Adapted cultural meals
* Globalized dishes

They should write on:

* Which Cultures the food connects (at least three cultural
Connections)
* Explain the ingredients they share in common
* Describe the Shared traditions
* What are the  Cultural blending of the food
* Explain how the food unites cultures. 

*This week focuses on unity, connection, globalization, and shared humanity.*

### Participation Format

Each entry must include:
* One clearly identified meal
*  One clearly identified culture
* Cultural background explanation
* Historical or traditional context
* Personal reflection
* Original photos or videos
* Proper storytelling structure
*  Clear headings and formatting
* Authentic narration
* Original content only
* Respect for cultural representation

### Steem Ecosystem Integration

Participants are encouraged to actively use:

* Steem Atlas Location pinning: cultural mapping, community representation
* Steem Video :Food preparation, storytelling, cultural narration, visual documentation

Participants may share their stories through:
* Written posts
* Steem Video
* Or a combination of both

### Challenge Summary 

**“Meals and Cultures of the World” is a cultural journey, not a cooking contest. It is a global storytelling experience where our tastes becomes language, meals become memory, and our food becomes identity.**


## Grading
The tasks would be graded thus;
Items|Grades
----|---
Ai check|✅
Plagiarism | ✅
Cultural depth and understanding (Authenticity/Storytelling quality) | 3
Originality and creativity(Educational value) | 3
Presentation/ Adherence to instructions|2
Community Engagement |1
Steem atlas/ steem video| 1
Total| 10

Thanks for this opportunity again. I do hope to bring in my best in this challenge season if granted.

Telegram- sculpturedips
Discord- alexanderpeace88

author: arshani
title: Daily Menu Delicacies Week No.110. date (14-02-2026) // Yummy day with loved ones
text:
*Hello My Dear friends 😍,* You all are welcome to my blog. Today I am going to share with you the food I had on February 14th. Saturday, February 14th is Valentine's Day. Although it is a special day for lovers, my day went by as usual. Since various programs were organized on television channels throughout the day, I spent the day entertaining my family. ![IMG_5171.jpeg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZBkaoyo75RZcGpPotf9RxNbmFW8LXZBN5ZEcxuNQ1sV1/IMG_5171.jpeg) However, it is a pleasure to see such delicious food that people all over the world eat throughout the day through this food menu and through it we can see the names of various foods and foods that we have never seen before. So we should thank the @steemit-ironchef community to organizng such an amazing contest for us.
![0e6271d9-0712-4f2d-8317-5477768932cb.jpeg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTvt3eZVr1xhrTWkwgKfueygguA27msFe2CitfKbxYALT/0e6271d9-0712-4f2d-8317-5477768932cb.jpeg)
I had chickpeas for breakfast. Chickpeas were made by sauteing them with onion slices, green chilies. My mother had prepared them with spices. That morning was a day when I had pharmacy duty. I ate some chickpeas before going to work. It was really delicious. This kind of taste and spicy food is good for getting rid of morning boredom. Chickpeas are a very nutritious grain. Most of the time, in our house, this is prepared boiled, or tempered with onion slices and chilli pods with spices. I love to eat them both ways.
![IMG_5121.jpeg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmW776AWJtGbvPuJafP3mid7rpAScCDLLs7FrKETAoBMN3/IMG_5121.jpeg)
I was supposed to be off duty at 12 noon. When I went home, it was around 1.30 pm. By then, my mother had already prepared lunch. Because of Valentine's Day, everyone in my house was gathered in the living room and watching television programs. I also quickly freshened up and joined them. After a while, my mother said that lunch was ready, so we each put food on our lunch plates. I really like spending such days with my family.
![IMG_5151.jpeg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVVnZeQzU6n5VTfMjn7B6KhadVvqA2HvjEbQyogCrxjXn/IMG_5151.jpeg)
We were sitting in front of the television all day, but in the afternoon, we took a break from the television because it was raining. Because it was raining with lightning, my father said that there would be a power outage. However, after a while, the rain stopped and the environment became fresh. At that time, my mother was preparing roti for dinner. In a few minutes, the dinner table was set and we had dinner while watching television. We really like to eat food like roti during rainy season. Because I like to have a warm roti and a cup of tea with a cold wind. A cup of tea mixed with ginger and sugar is even better. That's how the day was spent beautifully with food. I am preparing another menu delicacies for the day. I will share that with you soon. Finally I invite @kyrie1234, @roohiasif99, @aviral123 to take part in this wonderful contest. ####
Thank you for giving a time!

author: lunasilver
title: Chicken and potato croquettes | chicken recipe slc29-w5
text:
![Recipe Boiled Chicken_20260212_075630_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYUbL9W8nc5iEDG9rxW9CA6kpmYdSTvmcKgAA84gChzj2/Recipe_Boiled_Chicken_20260212_075630_0000.png)
###
Boiled Chicken Recipe
Boiled chicken is a valuable source of protein in my family's diet. We prepare several delicious recipes with boiled or poached chicken. Among them are "Russian salad" and chicken. shredded and sautéed. Kabah is another way to enjoy boiled chicken. So, preparing this recipe seems appealing to me. Although there are some of its ingredients that I don't have available and in some process I will reduce them due to a lack of ingredients. Let's begin with the first steps of this recipe, namely: ###
The ingredients
For this recipe I bought the protein and vegetables: | Chicken | |--------| |Potato| |Carrot| |Onion| |Bell pepper| |Chili pepper| |Garlic| |1 egg | |1 cup of wheat flour | |Oil for frying | |Salt and paprika|
![IMG_20260209_192245.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQ9sLbKUqxrrJQVeg4RbEJ56BqG6CD6xZ4c886oc3BX5s/IMG_20260209_192245.jpg)
###
Cooking process
For this recipe, I started by boiling the chicken, potatoes, and carrots. Then, checking the recipe instructions, I realized the carrots were supposed to be grated... But what could I do? Oh well, I just went ahead with the recipe.
![IMG_20260209_192139.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYXtTuyjy4HL3AszpznF73xdnNhNTWQFE8aKzhDF5zhtn/IMG_20260209_192139.jpg)
Next, I chopped the chili peppers, onion, and bell pepper along with the garlic.
![IMG_20260209_192330.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmb8ywJiCDZRbN3pywJZHvdBdbBJJT2fu7gB8PU3QBbXpR/IMG_20260209_192330.jpg)
When the chicken, carrot, and potato were ready, I shredded the chicken and peeled the skin off the potato and carrot. I then started crushing these ingredients and added the chopped seasonings. I also added salt and paprika. I mixed everything until I got a dough.
![IMG_20260210_024722.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUEk7Jz67eW3tcpGYheKZtNk7YSPv73mK4nz8CTsMQKdS/IMG_20260210_024722.jpg)
To this dough I added an egg and wheat flour and kneaded it again. I already had a dough ready to make the croquettes. I shaped them into circles and fried them.
![IMG_20260210_024839.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQw7Wh5qad5mwHpxYiGTS2NNA8AxzPL3E9jEgXpGztoeS/IMG_20260210_024839.jpg) ![IMG_20260210_024940.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWJEzvqnh5LuL2Tw73UTiguAhncXQ1k2S5WVDAxX3nLJ5/IMG_20260210_024940.jpg)
###
The cost of making this recipe
|Product| Local price| Steem price| |--------|--------|--------| |Chicken |1000 bs|48.17| |Carrot |70 bs|3.37| |Potato |80 bs|3.85| |Chili pepper |50 bs|2.40| |Bell pepper |50 bs|2.40| |Garlic |130 bs|6.26| |Seasoning (salt, paprika)|50|2.40| |Total investment |1430 bs|68.88| ###
The experience of preparing this recipe
The flavor was magnificent. Dear @subooni, thank you for sharing this delicious chicken recipe in this challenge. This recipe is very filling. I could eat it on its own without any side dish, or perhaps with a fresh salad of tomatoes, onions, and avocado. Now I want to make this recipe with all the ingredients raw, make the batter, and then fry it, to see if the flavor changes. Or if the texture changes. I'm going to get creative 🤣🤣🤣🤣. What is certain is that during these 5 weeks of the challenge I've learned to eat chicken in different ways. I want to invite @eglis, @pandora2010, and @eudisfiaz to take advantage of these last few weeks of the challenge. Here's the invitation: https://steemit.com/worldofchickens29w5/@suboohi/slc29-w5-boiled-chicken-recipes-t7hm8e ```The cover was created in Canva. The images are my own, and some were edited with a Xiaomi Redmi 9A phone. ```
![Gracias por leer dos idiomas ](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXh1Tz62QDUUh3NKNQ3F5QSWszvSW7XomPQZ6x5toYMsY/Leer_hace_bien_20251017_115222_0000.png) @Lunasilver| jueves 12 de febrero de 2026 |[ Logro 1](https://steemit.com/hive-172186/@lunasilver/my-introduction-to-the-steem-greeters-community-achievement-1)

author: samhunnahar
title: Spicy fried chickpeas recipe
text:
![IMG_20260212_185210.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWg7UkyjwducKAV6dmw97dXQya5mQpvpa4mqGnY3rBfHa/IMG_20260212_185210.jpg)
# Assalamualaikum, How are you all, friends? I hope you are all well. Alhamdulillah, I am also well and healthy. I have not been able to work for two days because my mobile phone broke. I gave it to be repaired so that I can do the work properly if I get it in my hand. But now I am working with a laptop, and it is very troublesome. But what else can I do? I really like doing the work constantly. So today I am back with you to share a new video blog. Which I had captured on my mobile before. Since I have Google, I took it from my laptop and shared it with you. Today I will share with you a cooking recipe in which I fried chickpeas. I really like eating fried chickpeas. It is as delicious to eat as it is beneficial for health. However, those who have allergies cannot eat it either. I like it very much, but I don't want to eat it all the time; I like to eat it occasionally. I made the recipe a few days ago, and it was very good to eat. It is very good to eat it when cooked like this. So I break the potatoes and cook it like this. If you cook it with a little more salt, it is amazing to eat. I am here to share that recipe with you today. I hope you enjoy watching the video. I am sharing the processes of how I cooked it with you in the video. Thank you all so much for taking your valuable time to watch. May everyone be well and healthy, may Allah Hafez bless you. [IPFS Video](https://gateway.pinata.cloud/ipfs/bafybeiej3iycpy3i3udw52xk5prinpizkunckt2eiksztnoo4d6b3r5y2i)

author: gondalbiya
title: Daily Menu Delicacies Week No.109. date (11-02-2026) // (Tasty Delights from Morning to Night) 🤤🍽️
text:
|
Greetings to all friends
| | - | ![Minimalist Summer Photo Collage Instagram Post_20260212_225349_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYrZX8zyLodRdaJYDyNeZBGi31Wdv7any4yYMJH4pgSMh/Minimalist_Summer_Photo_Collage_Instagram_Post_20260212_225349_0000.png)
Designed By Canva
|
Hi Everybody
| | - | **Assalamualaikum!** How are you all? I hope everyone is living a healthy and happy life. I feel truly thankful for each new day and sincerely thank Allah for this beautiful blessing. Today, I am excited to participate in the wonderful contest **“The Daily Menu: Delicacies Week #109”** 🎉. I am very grateful to the Steem Iron Chef community for arranging such an engaging and meaningful contest. It gives us a great platform to share our favorite dishes and cooking experiences. I’m happy to present my daily menu and hope you enjoy these dishes as much as I enjoyed preparing them.
https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPjMtXoLDsYpxnyJ51A7R9MH8pZpP9wAMwxL4dBoKrAD1/IMG_7098.jpeg
In the morning, I wanted something tasty for breakfast, so I decided to make some non-gluten biscuits for myself. I often bake them at home to enjoy with milk tea in the morning. Since the previous batch was already finished, I took some time to make a fresh batch. I also made some Naan Khatai biscuits, which are soft, crumbly, and melt in your mouth. This picture shows the biscuits before baking they looked so yummy and perfectly shaped. Once baked, they turned out soft, delicious, and perfect for a cozy breakfast with a cup of tea.
https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfEHeZi7oYQ6SVVgv7swucDNouHuqPXkXbBXp7AMPibc9/IMG_7030.jpeg
In the afternoon, I cooked this roast with my mother. We had marinated it well the night before and added plenty of seasoning so it would be flavorful the next day. We chose to steam it because the steaming makes it more tender and delicious. The longer it stays to be marinated, the more flavorful and spicy it becomes, giving it a rich and tasty taste. https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQF7wm1wtsA8nDVMPCmzjnukXBJSkRqYVHvmoyxnEHoDk/IMG_7091.jpeg In the evening, some guests came to our house and brought this cake with them. It was really delicious. The top was white and green, and inside it was filled with rich chocolate flavor. The cake was very soft, and we enjoyed it with our evening tea. Many people often bring cakes, but this one stood out because of its white color and tasty chocolate filling. The red cherry on top was fresh and added a nice touch, making the cake look even more appealing.
https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTVqa8J5joEJHGjZVYk5J7atz3rGaSusFf5p5XHNc45h2/IMG_7031.jpeg
Then at night, we were all craving some tasty, spicy home-cooked food. I hadn’t made macaroni at home for a long time, so everyone asked me to prepare it. I like making macaroni tastier by using just the right amount of spices it makes the flavor better. I had prepared all the ingredients in advance and then cooked the macaroni slowly, adding extra vegetables. This makes it even more delicious, and it’s a great way for kids to eat vegetables without noticing, while the overall taste becomes richer and more flavorful. |
I will invite my Friends@ahsansharif @sualeha @max-pro @m-fdo
| | - | |
Thanks for reading my post
| | - |
**Best Regards** **@gondalbiya** ![png-transparent-steemit-logo-blockchain-cryptocurrency-facebook-post-text-logo-bitcoin-fotor-bg-remover-20240108184243.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdtGsjPeZCeedByXgRFkK1tH6ELZuCMiWpgMAgfsBYTUa/png-transparent-steemit-logo-blockchain-cryptocurrency-facebook-post-text-logo-bitcoin-fotor-bg-remover-20240108184243.png)

author: polash123
title: Allah's gift is a storehouse of eggs."Zirabo"
text:
**** ****
|![1000463165.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSCJnqsGxnHENpKQjk2k4xpgjYSBBMHxjaByB1ei1kfR3/1000463165.jpg)| |--|
Store name
Assalamu Alaikum, how are you all? I hope you are all doing well. So today I have come to you to discuss the Allah Dan Dim Aarta shop. This shop is located at Jirabo Bus Stand. To come here, you will have to take a bus from Abdullahpur to Jirabo for 10 taka and get off and you will see this shop on the side of the road. Because it is next to where you will be dropped off, but this shop is located next to the bus stand. Anyway, you can come by following this location. Name|photo |--|--| Duck eggs|![1000463166.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmezYU7TnSCFf1qm3g7yKwdXVnRsUmzLBaaExTBSb5sYyQ/1000463166.jpg) Duck eggs|![1000463167.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVAtjDoKZKP8esssdQ8K7WhgWqiuAuxgmAvc8MofkgKST/1000463167.jpg) So the shop is small, but this shop not only sells wholesale but also sells boiled eggs. If you go here, you can only buy wholesale and there are different types of eggs available here. But you have to tell them in advance. Anyway, I went to this egg shop yesterday and I was very happy to see the egg shop on the side of the road. And I ate a boiled egg from his shop. Photo|name| |--|--| ![1000463169.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmS1DxC7AhPKcMmX6suGJiGBdEKSSuBKGQ52jKKz4TAhoD/1000463169.jpg)|Duck eggs ![1000463172.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXNHncCCK81TDByZWZAiKCZEVTzEYQXHFfTCHnXDkZPBy/1000463172.jpg)|Tray If you look at the photo, you can see that a young boy is running the shop. But running this shop at such a young age is a very courageous act. Actually, I think that if you want to achieve success in life, it is possible to move forward with patience and courage. But another thing is that it is never right to look down on someone's work. Whether it is small or big, work is work. Similarly, this is also a very big act for them. They do this work but they manage their family. Name|photo| |--|--| Boiler chicken eggs|![1000463175.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVdGLQ2keK9aTWJvoxKhX9QTuqZXyThpmoh6Zdc2LjXNL/1000463175.jpg) Quail eggs|![1000463176.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbQwQQYBim6VuPCkST15qAjLqiJzhxBugGaGm6cgYy6Yb/1000463176.jpg) Duck eggs|![1000463177.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfAq11WCEx4fJGEDKCoLZbdgLZxuJZLtoYvE99YzqzXyN/1000463177.jpg) Duck eggs|![1000463179.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRxnWz1Rqcg6RKj1unQ1DxdFKKASq3xJnRRLdEJjZZK9L/1000463179.jpg) But anyway, I liked seeing his courage. And if every decision in life is difficult, then it is possible to achieve success. Any work requires patience and hard work, that hard work never goes in vain. So I say that we should all stick to that hard work, no matter what we do. Because hard work never goes in vain, hard work shows the face of success one day or another. |![1000463185.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmT3t2nkbNc5hTtrdnRtTHWQk2pjnnPkW14m2ajKibpX7E/1000463185.jpg)| |--|
Whole store
|![1000463184.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQfBA4a29AjnWibudkhABi4chr1J2UnUh4d3a8oufU9CF/1000463184.jpg)| |--|
Boiled egg
Anyway, the environment around this shop is very good. But I would say personally, it's a shop on the side of the road, so you need to be a little careful about the environment. But this is for a while. Because once the road is fixed, their shop will be very popular. Anyway, overall, I found the area around the shop to be clean and tidy, I liked everything. So, I said goodbye here today. **** ****

author: lamiyahaque
title: Chicken Biryani Recipe.
text:
![IMG20260213141851.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmeMAse12XbNzLif7TKSTx1SwaZDY9fnfDMgt4f6SGSESz/IMG20260213141851.jpg) ### Assalamualaikum ❣️ I'll start by wishing everyone a good afternoon. How are you all? I hope you are all well by the grace of God. I am also doing well with my family, Alhamdulillah. Today I am here with a recipe post for you. I hope you all enjoy it, Insha Allah. Wishing everyone good health. So let's get started. ---
🧑‍🍳 Cooking ingredients 🧑‍🍳
--- |![IMG20260213113604.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNsdbhQeeeCLjtLbkw1jtNVb2Sb6Hq87VYnk2xxyBkNKC/IMG20260213113604.jpg)|Chicken|2 kg| |-|-|-| |![IMG20260213113456.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNinLdyNQz5VmK6QHYLr35nnKBHAzw5qqKmfZhd2VUw8p/IMG20260213113456.jpg)|Rice |1.5 kg| |![IMG20260213113728.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTrZKy2apMZvqQmyzFdekn9QvDHdHB23DcCGwnZA7LcAb/IMG20260213113728.jpg)|Onion |Two cups| |![IMG20260213113435.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdHousv9bT9KCSh2zCZxyGTqyZXPp1iKL5zEcRin2Vid2/IMG20260213113435.jpg)|Ginger paste |Four teaspoons| |![IMG20260213113444.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYpSgepDcT4mkBHrQ3qjNAethcbBcn6h9YFa29qc6JYJD/IMG20260213113444.jpg)|Garlic paste |Four teaspoons | |![IMG20260213115657.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVkMGYLg1VTaefrxuysj8bpJjrpQPAdUhruq5Myv8CBSw/IMG20260213115657.jpg)|Biryani spices |One packet| |![IMG20260213120026.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcMk8cjdCjuebRWLVtXuujKNCT52RqTAzxPDHSB4yVDb9/IMG20260213120026.jpg)|Sour yogurt |Four teaspoons | |![IMG20260213120241.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTcyvqDc5CVBHYjq398gfLsbx7uUYBPwhEkXt9rAteKaT/IMG20260213120241.jpg)|Tomato sauce |1 cup | |![IMG20260213115523.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmP6s7i5mg8aUW8z1fWoe7WuFbfQsFTysakbWn13srNm8N/IMG20260213115523.jpg)|Milk powder | Four teaspoons | |![1000102486.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTu4wNxfpdnjx94Lgktt4b8aG5oEBUtvn2zp3Apc9GcTR/1000102486.jpg)|Salt |A lot .| ---
🥧 Cooking steps 🥧
--- |![IMG20260213114936.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXN9a4nBdw4WAXuDxzhFtgMeYk41EV7dMRYd8xS2Qq1dD/IMG20260213114936.jpg)|![IMG20260213115554.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQnUte5Gh4PCFezjALCC8fDVeF2ZDQGXWWpMVytEx6Xi1/IMG20260213115554.jpg)| |-|-| ![IMG20260213115849.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmenwiKwd1Sp7RUq8ehhDhitLT84VmAAtFMJX3T2NPGMiK/IMG20260213115849.jpg)|![IMG20260213115953.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbimA1mPDCuEeRHgdokA6oggVvf4jui8SVMAVQcVm9Wrb/IMG20260213115953.jpg)| First, I lit the stove and put the pan on. After that, I heated the oil and fried the chopped onion. After that, I added the ginger paste and garlic paste and mixed it well. After a while, I added the chicken. After that, I mixed everything well. --- |![IMG20260213120531.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbcApnM4K1nQ3pLiLGHTVFFv9GMk713JBWs3DQN72vR7a/IMG20260213120531.jpg)|![IMG20260213120805.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXqtYrfKWRqk5K4LkvGKk8D7ZTB5FwoBbg1sdtnUHdAqa/IMG20260213120805.jpg)| |-|-| |![IMG20260213120836.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbxDdAEgdRhvd1RkbvE61G4AdKAvB16N6dA9Y1piXwfhJ/IMG20260213120836.jpg)|![IMG20260213123307.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRhcXG4Tn8LbQ4HTLcQTatwWp11pys5aHrs76MohPtaKe/IMG20260213123307.jpg)| Now I mixed it with biryani masala. After a while, I added all the masala ingredients and mixed everything well. This time I shredded the chicken well. --- |![IMG20260213123344.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUUr7MHpZPpn6w1TpcC5MMsum2rKKo1jGD18TDBGPL9SC/IMG20260213123344.jpg)|![IMG20260213123501.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmf9r26VMcneCZP27TVcotBdHuedsWFabt7VSyWWYuL5Jp/IMG20260213123501.jpg)| |-|-| |![IMG20260213123834.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmc4Fso5fpuFfq33MyjZC9WWEYjUWjCbJYMt3eJfXLVi7x/IMG20260213123834.jpg)|![IMG20260213124213.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZLmigdq2ZmrCxuv3udUmt4um9smTqpyRwfHdfsdayoDe/IMG20260213124213.jpg)| Now I cleaned the rice and then poured it in. Now I mixed everything well. I cooked it for a while and then added the required amount of water. --- |![IMG20260213124213.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZLmigdq2ZmrCxuv3udUmt4um9smTqpyRwfHdfsdayoDe/IMG20260213124213.jpg)|![IMG20260213124913.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQ3guDPzpEsrprw7fApfzjp5BSvWHZGBMu87FeMUmKTvT/IMG20260213124913.jpg)| |-|-| ![IMG20260213124918.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmecomN1p6hViAe3CXPD5hyiKJiAXtMzR9D2aBLSZHyJzE/IMG20260213124918.jpg)|![IMG20260213130335.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZSZLKSFCFcNDwo5SjS26pVS4cRUr53JxeWd5Z2rJ497j/IMG20260213130335.jpg)| Now I covered it with a lid. After a while, I opened the lid and shook it well. I covered it with a lid again. After a while, I saw that the rice was cooked, so I drained the water. After that, I finished cooking here. ---
🧑‍🍳 Presentation 🧑‍🍳
--- |![IMG20260213140753.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmeMGDWF332JRDeQ82wwi4PEyW8vWewm2e8iNey7cKz7Wj/IMG20260213140753.jpg)|![IMG20260213141851.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmeMAse12XbNzLif7TKSTx1SwaZDY9fnfDMgt4f6SGSESz/IMG20260213141851.jpg)| |-|-| Chicken biryani is cooked. Now I arranged the food on the plate to serve. Chicken biryani was very delicious to eat and we all ate it together. Don't forget to comment and let me know how the recipe post went. I look forward to your cooperation. I bid farewell here today. I wish everyone to be well and healthy always. |

author: roohiasif99
title: Recipe of the day week#97 Left over rice|| making Caremal kheer|| Delicious desert at home
text:
![1000152147.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUh7LvVSWvobgrvFY3MXnn3YvVL8LiRHNUVABmiAdAvpt/1000152147.jpg)
" ##
Greeting friends, Welcome to my belog,how are you all .I am @ roohiasif99 from.pakistan .I hope everybody fine too.Todsy here I am participating this wonderful contest Recipe of the day week #97 .This interesting contest organized by @ lirvic .I am Thank ful for @ lirvic and whole steem Iron- chef community.to giving me a chance to bring my abilities .Today I am share you left over rice||making Caramel kheer|| Recipe.Its very different taste and unique texture .In our house, we often have leftover rice at night, which we think is stale, so how can we eat it? I am sharing a new recipe with you, I hope you will like it too. Lets move towards and starts ##
Things are need Ingridents|Quality -|- Milk|2 glass Sugar|1 cup Cream |2 tsp Chocolate rusk|4 Sesames seeds|2 tsp Left over rice|1 cup Shrews|2 for garnishing ![1000151945.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdW77KzZS75a8iqcTY4SCMJNQQJNBMUbv9WF9upLrScNV/1000151945.jpg) ##
Procedure with step by step First I boiled the milk. When the milk boiled, I set it aside to cool. ![1000151942.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUmJzGmAgkEqGMUPCA3qPgsoXox5Q2VNU8egKW2jFAaTb/1000151942.jpg) I broke the Rusk into smaller pieces.take a blender jug add in milk,left over Rice,seasoms seeds,then grind it.then making a fine paste . ![1000151950.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmeenom81b4WuHVMSXJbBChygVRULTMa6fNtVcty5U4TyR/1000151950.jpg)|![1000151951.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdTKU8jqPqyC1eFhyMh8L4msxQLff1HTewFeaCZ1h8C5B/1000151951.jpg) -|- ![1000151956.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTquuDRzvPwfyuwxPH8tQ1mQyp29cB9fXUPcAEiYkmDXT/1000151956.jpg)|![1000151957.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmR3uUgeHMjXjAMuQ7jjHNa8u8Ub88BE5qKbEzHmY6JpUR/1000151957.jpg) -|- Take a pen add all the milk mixture then turn on the flame .Then I cooked it in the milk mixture until it thickened. ![1000151963.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmaiB9LaFVMDpzbbwwkFH9FmTPrc5SbApp9PBDirmu8FAK/1000151963.jpg)|![1000151961.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUpQmVn2Su9DDZ6aQ7XN68anMEhKyuu1gvYeprkx3J7Bd/1000151961.jpg) -|- On the other hands take a pen add in sugar in medium flame .untill the sugar is caramelized.becausevi want to be a cream kheer.I added our sugar, which was nicely caramelized, to add in kheer. ![1000151948.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSEhgY4ohMiMMEGhMr9UY2FBDGjzfkYDLDuYM9Qi1bjMg/1000151948.jpg)|![1000151949.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPQ7AL63UabCiGJNozHRbp6YRkpkr5qVBzwN6VHLmcWqx/1000151949.jpg) -|- Mixed all the ingridents very well.When we add the melted sugar to the milk mixture, it will be quite hot, so we will add it with some care so that it does not splash.The consistency of the kheer is up to you. How thin it is and how thick it should be depends on your preference. ![1000151964.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNgz82jdUMt96Xzp2t6PTBtBTwa2GFSdvJj9mTWre3V43/1000151964.jpg)|![1000151977.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQi6118oVffcNUWhaNjPPvyiKyi5n6LATDQDFyEMcSi45/1000151977.jpg) -|- I like medium, not too much thick not too thin.when the kheer is cooler add in serving bowl .After pouring the pudding into a bowl, I crushed a piece of shrews in cheese grater and put it on top. ##
final presentation of my delicious Caremal kheer ![1000151987.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcVHCNvyBjgF3nnzdFtgXTfzEq26gfPgN2y4YF9qSM4Em/1000151987.jpg) ![1000151979.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSDa7hCfbuRchH2zn7kwMn1y8UDPQUXL9rmCYJP9DWeS1/1000151979.jpg) A very delicious type of delicious Caremal kheer is ready. You will definitely like it if you try it.The taste is amazing .smooth texture.The very important thing is that it's make very short time .quickly made recipe.I hope you must try at home . ![1000151990.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcmo6UBDcZaiJ1uYAaXQ5q2eUuF9Cy32AjAFJsaoqeCGP/1000151990.jpg)|![1000151980.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmf4RhB6kesyr6fyEUuKJ4XRy73TATFCvVg3v7i9zo2GJo/1000151980.jpg) -|- I hope you like my recipe and give me a valuable feed back .before I am going I would like to invite my lovely steemit friends @ suboohi @crismenia @lirvic to invite my friends. Best regards, @roohiasif99 ##
Thanks for Reading and support

author: mariyaafrin
title: SLC29-W5 // "Boiled Chicken Recipes".
text:
**Hello..!! My Dear Friends, I am @mariyaafrin from #Bangladesh 🇧🇩**
***
Hello my dear steemit friends. How are you all? I hope everyone is well by the grace of Allah. Alhamdulillah, I am also well with your prayers and love. I am here with a new post again, welcome to my new post, I hope you will like my post. Today I am here to participate in Steemit Learning Challenge Session 29 Week 5.The main theme of the fifth week's engagement challenge is the ***boiled chicken recipes***.Thank you @suboohi ma'am for organizing such a great competition.Today I made Chicken Vegetable Kebab as part of this competition.So let's share the step-by-step method of making Chicken Vegetable Kebab. so let's start........
|![1_20260214_212022_0000.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdVJfcEkrypgUczWLH6oNpKCRnC6GmMDLrcxbb5DVM898/1_20260214_212022_0000.png) |-|
Photo Designed by Canva
https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfAkUqGw5uy1jNmTcb68ECgENHtdXjHrr83YSaNXr9hjW/17303825412526700774889818796594.png >>Have you tried boiled chicken recipes?? What recipe your family likes?.
Yes, of course I have tried the boiled chicken recipe at home many times.Almost every member of my family loves boiled chicken recipes.Because it does not use extra spices or extra oil, it is very healthy for people of all ages.I especially serve the boiled chicken recipe for afternoon snacks or tea parties, and even for lunch because it goes well with everything.I love to cook, so I often make new recipes at home and feed them to my family members.Similarly, my family members also praise me a lot for eating various foods made with chicken.
 >>Make the chicken recipe that is shared in the course / or any one with boiled chicken that you like.with a detailed step-by-step procedure along with images.
Today, to participate in this course, I made an amazing Chicken Vegetable Kebab with some vegetables and boiled chicken that I had in the kitchen. Below is a step-by-step guide to making Chicken Vegetable Kebabs with ingredients:
 |
Making ingredients:
| |-| |![IMG_20260214_185122.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPbN5Ev7wPcVWU3BAmwbZkhrzn3QUoYrF1R11vkgKLPPQ/IMG_20260214_185122.jpg) |-| https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfAkUqGw5uy1jNmTcb68ECgENHtdXjHrr83YSaNXr9hjW/17303825412526700774889818796594.png |
Ingredients
|
Quantity
|----|---- |
boiled chicken
|
1 cup
|
boiled potato
|
1 cup
|
Carrots, grated
|
4 tablespoons
|
Coriander leaves, chopped
|
2 tablespoons
|
Chopped onion
|
2 tablespoons
|
Chopped green chili
|
1 tablespoons
|
Chili flakes
|
1 Teaspoon
|
Turmeric powder
|
half teaspoon
|
Cumin powder
|
half teaspoon
|
Salt
|
1 tablespoons (to taste)
|
egg
|
One
|
Bread powder
|
1 cup
|
Making process:
| |-| ####
***Step - 1***
*** ![IMG_20260214_185456.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXKh5B5HRem9Xznyn7x2PrUVxiQVrWjer9jxaLKTtgrHS/IMG_20260214_185456.jpg)|![IMG_20260214_185509.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYYuvKibf4b3dtE2vpVo3q7AvzxfkD6XEV9drhHC6wi7S/IMG_20260214_185509.jpg) -|-
First, I boiled the chicken and potatoes well for about 10 to 15 minutes. Then I drained the water and cooled the chicken and potatoes for some time.Then I took pre-boiled potatoes and chicken in a bowl.I put pre-grated carrots inside it.
####
***Step - 2***
*** ![IMG_20260214_185548.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTE5LQMHqxg25j3C9FuKUvfoTusBehuJiRE5cZNJxW1j1/IMG_20260214_185548.jpg)|![IMG_20260214_185559.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQEVfKx8ZvYYpt3PLEyfjuymmQ62ihSoqjCjBMwZ8hafV/IMG_20260214_185559.jpg) -|- |![IMG_20260214_190021.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZrQmxggPcnzT1BBYMtxjPZgkwGmWhUPQEbezRsGmNzwm/IMG_20260214_190021.jpg) |-|
Then, I added all the spices, chopped coriander leaves, chopped green chilies, chopped onion and salt to the boiled chicken one by one.Then I made a kebab dough by mashing boiled potatoes, boiled chicken, and all the spices well.
 ####
***Step - 3***
*** ![IMG_20260214_190210.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNnU1jrkDNWi2smydT1Ex2YDntqkriHRZVTQvmFzUyr2M/IMG_20260214_190210.jpg)|![IMG_20260214_190218.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUWoq8Cm6vGrp8xNUR9tDDxdidHAfhmwMCm1GYctf4vZi/IMG_20260214_190218.jpg) -|- |![IMG_20260214_190810.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTfpib9BNypg5rP6UeDJncfEUYkRkvY6MLX2Dgm28SbQa/IMG_20260214_190810.jpg) |-|
Once the kebab dough is ready, I first roll it into a round shape with my hands and then flatten it into a flat kebab shape.In the same way, I made all the kebab shapes one by one.
 ####
***Step - 4***
*** |![IMG_20260214_191227.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRmUFufMGxKE4CZvBQxoRt7kr2CsCv5yAAAfsLwuLkHTZ/IMG_20260214_191227.jpg) |-| ![IMG_20260214_191329.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdBriF7JRVyEJbLYHTanHgsYJYkdH1XMurPyPrQSyVZxX/IMG_20260214_191329.jpg)|![IMG_20260214_191438.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmR8AJeTgqRfHEhiviYvKnCFAzwRL22FVpZHPZk2D6n2mD/IMG_20260214_191438.jpg)|![IMG_20260214_191530.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmd97Qst8bubao9UpuFafHb6icbq2Q51gdyxcqfNW5cPEa/IMG_20260214_191530.jpg) -|-|-
Then I mixed flour, breadcrumbs, and an egg well to coat the kebabs.Then I shaped it into a kebab, first mixing it with flour, then with the egg mixture, and finally with breadcrumbs.I coated all of them in the same way in the shape of kebabs.
 ####
***Step - 5***
*** ![IMG_20260214_192305.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdkJaU2CunBvPhgB5awhX5c4mfdc8ym6PHeWpqxmt9bwm/IMG_20260214_192305.jpg)|![IMG_20260214_192632.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmeY7ZBcp2p2hiSzZ6sSxAZuQQvNTC3hswYGC14VeMT6NS/IMG_20260214_192632.jpg) -|- ![IMG_20260214_193017.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRVuzx6bm2KNErSca5QGYq2mMCG4nAqBDBfM73N34mVSP/IMG_20260214_193017.jpg)|![IMG_20260214_194014.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVYFEZeEfT1ZKqzeVP2SgCzbdtgw6BYJ7W5zqmfghUkQ7/IMG_20260214_194014.jpg) -|-
After coating all the kebabs in the shape of a kebab, I placed a pan on the stove and added a cup of soybean oil.When the oil was hot enough, I shaped the kebabs into it and fried them until they were well browned on medium heat.Once the kebabs were well fried, I drained them from the oil using a strainer and placed them in a bowl.Finally, today's chicken vegetable kebab is ready.
 >>List the prices of the ingredients you buy for making the recipe. (prices in your local currency and in STEEM).
The local price and steam price of the ingredients used to make today's Chicken Vegetable Kebab are listed below:

***(1 Steem=6.30bdt)*** >>Make a good Presentation of your recipe. |
Final presentation
| |-| |![IMG_20260214_195301.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQme9tAMCbr7udRnBNLY4UFtWsvkfPBWJKsnfSCKuUWq9gY/IMG_20260214_195301.jpg) |-| |![IMG_20260214_194901.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUv6YYL21NpivfkRLkuof8Nnm17i3iQXYgtruSBncSBA5/IMG_20260214_194901.jpg) |-| |![IMG_20260214_194901.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUv6YYL21NpivfkRLkuof8Nnm17i3iQXYgtruSBncSBA5/IMG_20260214_194901.jpg) |-| ![IMG_20260214_195516.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQ3WrKeuR6m3WZvhLHptUpJVusKgbkXSXyo9jQW2qXpnL/IMG_20260214_195516.jpg)|Selfie with my recipe -|- >>I hope everyone likes the Chicken Vegetable Kebab recipe I made today and let me know how you all liked it in the comments.See you in a new post. Until then, stay well, stay healthy, and stay safe. |
I am inviting my friends @sojib1996,@uzma4882 and @sualeha to participate in such a great course.

author: kolkamkwan
title: The Boat Noodle Odyssey: A Braised Pork Masterpiece!
text:
![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmWLzfXcHvBcEMDAAoNAvcKNSR55THnd7q8ZTAuJGpvHB2/image.png) ## The Boat Noodle Odyssey: A Braised Pork Masterpiece! ### 🇬🇧 English Review by Sakol ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmUQyEJMY1gia4yUvapnVYndf3SAK5bezvhzhDDuHxcs16/image.png) **Happy Holiday, my fellow flavor hunters!** Sakol is here to turn your lazy weekend into a **high-octane culinary adventure!** Today, we are not just eating lunch; we are setting sail on a river of rich, dark, and intense flavors with the legendary **Boat Noodles (Kuay Teow Rua)!** Let’s dive into this dark gold: ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmNqvnguqQodZwBnQDUVeLcMq9s5c9wMpENzVAjmguVAKR/image.png) * **The Foundation (Braised Pork):** Oh, sweet mercy! This pork has been stewed until it surrenders. It is so tender, it doesn't just melt in your mouth—it vanishes like a beautiful dream! Infused with Chinese herbs and cinnamon, every bite is a warm hug for your soul. * **The Noodle Duo (Sen Lek & Egg Noodles):** ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmXbynj8xyN9nxapK7WVC3HyAZm6vgpYQRBoTpJd5ZRAT2/image.png) * **Thin Rice Noodles (Sen Lek):** Slurping these is an art form. They are perfectly chewy, elastic, and soak up that spicy, blood-thickened broth like a sponge. ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmPxWP3vXPRPDaxEqxhy46YFqJTXGBmRLveuScxPgAScYD/image.png) * **Egg Noodles (Bamee):** Springy, yellow, and vibrant! They offer a delightful "bite" that dances on your palate. ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmdv9wLRGDS6AuBC3LgcyHiT1bXNsTbsLjMqMFBpv1KLPC/image.png) ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmUdSwX6KxW8xdvmmxjE2MSiXbCJh4r3qqzimuk9GnFC1B/image.png) ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmVaHcSMGWrXTWoprLVb4ewjKCRgvNegTWzYQZ53rRbyHj/image.png) * **The Sakol Special (Gao Lao + Rice):** Now, for the main event—**MY choice!** I skipped the noodles to get straight to the business: **Gao Lao (Soup & Meat Only)!** Paired with steaming hot jasmine rice, this is the champion's breakfast. Spooning that rich, concentrated spicy broth over the rice... it is absolute magic. ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmbtt5PyAuGM8ZMbf4s3f1YA2obpGjuYRAXyyFPKsguqe5/image.png) * **Blanched Morning Glory:** To cut through the richness, we have the extra **Morning Glory.** **CRUNCH!** It’s fresh, it’s snappy, and it adds that necessary texture to balance the tender pork. **The Verdict:** A holiday meal that hits you with spice, savory depth, and herbal aroma. Whether you are a noodle lover or a rice team player like me, this meal is a **12/10 flavor explosion!** ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmNg7m792LY2cQuvuScZBfFQHhKaD2s1Lk5XooDkFUSxaF/image.png) --- ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmS9drDnuZDcZfUbdYueFHVqb7ihwvzoySZwZC9WSeiVa2/image.png) ## ล่องเรือหารรัก(ษ์)ความอร่อย! พิชิตก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือหมูตุ๋นระดับตำนาน! ### 🇹🇭 รีวิวโดย พี่สากล ชวนชิม **สวัสดีวันหยุดสุดขีดครับพี่น้องครับ!** วันนี้พี่สากลคนดีคนเดิม จะพามาปลุกพลังกายพลังใจให้กระชุ่มกระชวย ด้วยเมนูที่บอกเลยว่า **"เข้มข้นจนต้องร้องขอชีวิต!"** กับก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือน้ำตกหมูตุ๋น ที่รสชาติลึกซึ้งบาดใจยิ่งกว่าซีรีส์เกาหลี! ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmRjJY9RjtSywi7kWPuGjNMeLh61do3oR6AhT3vztDjwbt/image.png) มาดูกันครับว่าความฟินระดับพรีเมียมวันนี้มีอะไรบ้าง: * **หมูตุ๋นจักรพรรดิ:** บอกเลยว่า "ตุ๋นจนลืมเคี้ยว!" เนื้อหมูนุ่มละลายแทรกซึมไปด้วยเครื่องเทศยาจีน หอมตลบอบอวล ยิ่งเคี้ยว (แทบไม่ต้องเคี้ยว) ยิ่งฟิน! เอ็นแก้วกรุบๆ นิดๆ คือสวรรค์รำไร! * **คู่หูเส้นสะท้านโลกันตร์:** ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmZAEaxDxzjBH7KqkQ9zCF64KsMQ4fkoMoeAVhsoqqPAKR/image.png) * **เส้นเล็ก:** เหนียวนุ่มหนึบหนับ! ดูดน้ำซุปเรือข้นคลั่กเข้าไปทุกอณู ซู้ดทีเดียว รสชาติพุ่งพล่านเต็มปาก! ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmY22P8UcWWf3aF3vPfqsTvzhdGkJxDxrXc5uFYDxvgBnF/image.png) ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmbAue9U1DVvPih1YzNEFHAE8r7CMb7HbrN7th136jZxtv/image.png) * **บะหมี่:** เส้นเหลืองหอมไข่ ลวกมาแบบเด้งสู้ฟัน เคี้ยวสนุก กรุบกริบเพลินใจ! * **สูตรลับฉบับพี่สากล (เกาเหลา + ข้าวเปล่า):** ไฮไลท์ของผมอยู่ตรงนี้! **"เกาเหลาแห้งชามน้ำชาม"** (หยอกๆ) เกาเหลาล้วนๆ ไม่เน้นเส้น เน้นเนื้อกับผัก! ราดน้ำซุปขลุกขลิกลงบนข้าวสวยร้อนๆ ควันฉุยๆ... โอ้โห! ข้าวเม็ดสวยดูดน้ำแกงเข้าไป คำนั้นคือ **นิพพาน!** * **ผักบุ้งลวก (เสริมทัพ):** สั่งเพิ่มมาเลยครับ! ผักบุ้งลวกเขียวสด กรอบดัง **"กร๊อบ!"** ช่วยตัดเลี่ยนและเพิ่ม Texture ให้การเคี้ยวหมูตุ๋นนุ่มๆ มีลูกเล่นมากขึ้น จิ้มกับน้ำส้มพริกดองสูตรเด็ดนะ... จบข่าว! **บทสรุป:** วันหยุดนี้ไม่มีเหงาครับ! เพราะรสชาติเผ็ด ร้อน หอม นัว ของก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือมื้อนี้ มันเติมเต็มหัวใจพี่สากลจนล้นปรี่! **ยกนิ้วโป้งให้ 3 ผ่านไปเลยครับลูกพี่!** ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmb78LxjALKEvRtLf8APKrxGG9uBr2KyPqJB6Stnyv2RFD/image.png) ---
![](https://images.ecency.com/DQma9gs9Xx5WxAnFttJsvFSen8gPixBfkHn8TKZSSEAXekJ/image.png) - ชุด | smartphone -----|----- 📱 Model | Xiaomi Mi 11 Pro & Sony ZV1M2 Type | Android & Compact Photographer | @kolkamkwan Language ability | Thai Location | Samutsakorn, Thailand
**ขอบคุณสำหรับการสนับสนุน โหวตให้กำลังใจจากคุณ** ***
Thank you for following And vote for encouragement. 🙏 🙏 🙏TH ![](https://images.ecency.com/DQmdQGxZkwPdmgRR9KAwfKa3e8mixr3JvzJz4Xz5aFMwT5x/sakol_thankyou.png) ***ขอบคุณที่ติดตาม และโหวตให้กำลังใจ***
*** **_SSUDJAI_** **_SSUDJAI_** **_SSUDJAI_** **_SSUDJAI_** **_SSUDJAI_** #thai #thailand #ssudjai #lifestyle คำเตือน *สินทรัพย์ดิจิทัลมีความเสี่ยง โปรดศึกษาและลงทุนให้เหมาะสมกับระดับความเสี่ยงที่ยอมรับได้ **คริปโทเคอร์เรนซีมีความเสี่ยงสูง คุณอาจสูญเสียเงินลงทุนได้ทั้งจํานวน *** ผลตอบแทนของสินทรัพย์ดิจิทัลในอดีตหรือผลการดําเนินงานในอดีต มิได้เป็นสิ่งยืนยันถึงผลตอบแทนของสินทรัพย์ดิจิทัลหรือผลการดําเนินงานในอนาคต ***

author: sualeha
title: Weekly contest|| Daily menu delicacies week of 109
text:
|![1000072460.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmecW3YWgP7UR5Ypp8FpZZZyR28pRwhwKX7ibPTBRXFyh6/1000072460.jpg) ----| ___ ##
***Greetings to all my friends***
___
Assalam o alikum friends ,I hope you all are well, today I like to share my participation post in weekly contest which were ***Daily menu delicacies week no :108*** .Well guys cooking for my family it's all but sharing love and care through food 🍴. It's a way to show you put thought into making something special just for them. Whether it's a simple meal or a fancy dish, the effort you put in speaks volumes 💕. Plus, it's a great way to create memories around the dinner table! [Contest post link share here](https://steemit.com/hive-180301/@steemit-ironchef/delicias-del-menu-diario-semana-109-daily-menu-delicacies-109) ##
Breakfast
For today's break fast I cooked omlate of three eggs in which chopped onion ,salt and green chillies.We also made my milk tea ☕ with naan 🫓.My hubby helped me in kitchen and love to fried any thing in kitchen hehehe. |![1000080758.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNPFxQaHwqfuTuVtLUHbX4ZW5TLJfe82WNGi46A6uP8JY/1000080758.jpg) ----| ___ ##
Lunch time
Afternoon time I did my home chores,when house cleaned with immagiatly started my lunch preparations infact my kids back to home and felt very hungry.Its true lentils are big source of protein in our daily menu so tried to cooked balance food for my love once. ![1000080766.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSB93BnU7EgorJBnb2DnpuHLjAQAYMh4Djqf4LH6M7QZV/1000080766.jpg) | ![1000080767.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmf4aLfsFar5UdWXMJgEwZDEnYpLLMEftpYqHdxgjDCFXD/1000080767.jpg) -|- ___ I cooked black lentils with cumin rice on my daughter's demand ___
![1000080768.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRmRkep7ncxmx5UR6Bi3rSphkbFBgD9kLDPUK9VkSGqVA/1000080768.jpg)
I followed my mother in low recipe for cooking black lentils ,I soaked for 15 minutes before cooking,I added chopped onion and tomatoes first after boiled then.added powder spices and tarkka on top and cooked cumin rice on separate Pan. ![1000080813.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNrEYchmvyhcukHzXpY7jaBzcb8SDAydbGBcWPgWskEPm/1000080813.jpg) | ![1000080816.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmURKBwT68s73RLZvy9yzNi1RyTcbigoCiVvNBuLkrZ7uF/1000080816.jpg) -|- ___
Super quick and delicious lunch ready to eat...
___ ##
Evening time
![1000080814.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmeCkvL1GH6p176SJEigfgH3XVsK6fieCsBQfjdpKACwfy/1000080814.jpg)
Evening time always tea ☕ time at my place ,my hubby were not back to home so I made only one cup tea for my self infact tea is not a drink it's more then me after took it I thought perfect,felt stressed free and it'smtly mood booster.My sons did there home assignments given by the school I helped them clean they needed. ##
Dinner time 😋
![1000080815.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVYccDDeyYAPyxPN5TZQNMZ1Sf4St9uYkXgwnMZMredQD/1000080815.jpg)
I never cooked anything in dinner today ,last night I made chicken Karahi it's leftover so I enjoyed it on this time with bread.My sons not at home they went out his friends birthday party . Chicken Karahi one of my family favourite food. |![1000070813.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRyLPB6NvTbYj7BoNoHhztTYXgWkXZ8KwymosPoo5qdHG/1000070813.png) ----| ___ So guy is it's all about from my today's food diary post to finish I would like to invite my friends @suboohi ,@aviral123 and @dasudi to participate and share daily menu of the day in SIC community. See you soon until next time tell them take care Allah hafiz ❣️ Regards @sualeha

author: denver23
title: Egg noodles
text:
**Hello steemit family,** how are you all, I am also fine with your prayers, Alhamdulillah, I wish peace to all.  In daily life we ​​do different things and also we do photography and memories. That's why I share with you some photography everyday.  Hope you like these photographs.


![1000062191.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcE6H67sBPofoazuNgodP1K6X2h3bhktUXBtxTTUaSEMW/1000062191.jpg)

![1000062188.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmb3fTDJCMXGeoXSXkpUfH9t1ZTHTeoFRkT8WZVqwkrdBn/1000062188.jpg)

![1000062204.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQS528g9KdrdqarC72fzxm4ZHMpaSZBL71mRdAXMCMhM8/1000062204.jpg)

![1000062205.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTYm38Xo9fzSBhRdfSArjsxrFgpf2huYg3trAFoArR53Y/1000062205.jpg)




 Egg noodles have been cooked with chili and onions and mixed very nicely. The noodles prepared here are very good. Cooking noodles with eggs makes them tasty and enjoyable. First, you need to boil the noodles. Then, in a pan, break the eggs and cut the vegetables before mixing them.


 This way, the dish becomes delicious and can be prepared in a short time. If you take it slowly, it will take longer, but by preparing it this way, it can be done quickly. When eating it, it will be tasty and enjoyable, and you will like it very much.


## <center>TAKE LOVE @DENVER23</center>

author: jyoti-thelight
title: Application Steemit Challenge Season 30 || Traditional Steam Cooking
text:
Greetings @steemitblog

According to the [ Announcement of SC SEASON 30 invitation](https://steemit.com/steemitchallenge/@steemitblog/steemit-update-february-8th-2026-the-steemit-challenge-applications-invited-for-season-30), the Steemit Challenge Season 30 will begin, and I consider it is a great opertunity to share my culinary prowess with my fellow Steemains this season. Numerous teams have been used, and I'm hoping that this new season will be more successful and efficient. I will present the "Traditional Steam" cooking techniques that are both the simplest and healthiest, while also being traditional. Participation in this topic is simple, and anyone can do it without assistance from artificial intelligence.
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVc413yoXPVWSTocZByt22qXc1WMrTavJPXt8mvZWS48a/image.png)
[source](https://www.canva.com/)
https://steemitimages.com/640x0/https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmca9Bt9r6sXsWNDjX5Z1bvJarpE5Jb91eHm2NM7x3xAur/image.png

The subject to be covered

Through this course, I have tried to re-teach the art of cooking that our ancestors practiced. In this course, I am going to teach you how to cook by steaming ingredients. Everyone around the world knows the art of cooking with steam. Especially in India, steaming is a popular method today, and I have come forward to teach it to our members because it is very tasty and healthy. I am going to teach you how to steam cook different foods each week for the next six weeks so that you can create and serve a healthy meal to your family. Six various kinds of steam cooking recipes from around the globe will be covered this season, including ### Week- 1: Idlis
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmc39qwFALZ1Df1UBeNCATC69NwqB3CH5gAHvXJqxR381N/image.png) [source](https://www.pexels.com/photo/meal-and-soup-20422128/)
I am going to teach you how to make Idli with its recipe for the first week. Idli is a popular South Indian steam cooked dish made by grinding rice and urad dal, fermenting it, and steaming it. It is flat, white, and fluffy. It is eaten for breakfast with chutney and sambar, and is a nutritious and easily digestible dish.
https://steemitimages.com/640x0/https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmca9Bt9r6sXsWNDjX5Z1bvJarpE5Jb91eHm2NM7x3xAur/image.png
### Week- 2: Momos
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVvXvbAF5CpnqVFUaDRJ7R3Q2EkdLTFPiXKpYQMhh2GeA/image.png) [source](https://www.pexels.com/photo/nepalese-momos-with-spicy-dipping-sauce-on-marble-28445593/)
I am going to teach you how to make Momos with its recipe for the 2nd week. Momos are a delicious steamed snack dish originating in Tibet and Nepal, and now popular in many countries, including India. They are made from maida or wheat flour, filled with chopped vegetables, paneer or meat (chicken/mutton), and served with chutney.
https://steemitimages.com/640x0/https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmca9Bt9r6sXsWNDjX5Z1bvJarpE5Jb91eHm2NM7x3xAur/image.png
### Week- 3 : Puttu
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmR7sNDqJ3m85EBTW5SSkob8cN89woFyGbL4f9Jm4prBRg/image.png) [source](https://www.pexels.com/photo/puttu-served-on-leaf-17104944/)
I am going to teach you how to make Rice Puttu with its recipe for the 3rd week. Rice Puttu (Pudding) is a popular South Indian, mainly Kerala and Tamil Nadu, traditional breakfast dish made by steaming rice flour, grated coconut, and water.
https://steemitimages.com/640x0/https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmca9Bt9r6sXsWNDjX5Z1bvJarpE5Jb91eHm2NM7x3xAur/image.png
### Week- 4 : Raagi Puttu
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfPJtHUAkgaJjehhXeWgkZnQpaJCoxev2HfZPwyyPuHKA/image.png) [source](https://www.kamalascorner.com/sweets/ragi-sweet-puttu.html)
I am going to teach you how to make Ragi Puttu with its recipe for the 4th week. Ragi Puttu (Ragi Pudding) is a traditional, nutritious and healthy South Indian dish made by steaming ragi flour, grated coconut, water and salt.
https://steemitimages.com/640x0/https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmca9Bt9r6sXsWNDjX5Z1bvJarpE5Jb91eHm2NM7x3xAur/image.png
### Week- 5 : Idiyappam
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNTiPo9GESvFgGYA7mWxuoytqqkHhrVYGcdsmxDgJfaPn/image.png) [source](https://pixabay.com/photos/indiappa-food-cuisine-idiyappam-6303127/)
I am going to teach you how to make Idiyappam with its recipe for the 5th week. Idiyappam is a traditional South Indian and Sri Lankan dish made by steaming rice flour. The dough is pressed into an Idiyappa mold and steamed in a plate-like shape.
https://steemitimages.com/640x0/https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmca9Bt9r6sXsWNDjX5Z1bvJarpE5Jb91eHm2NM7x3xAur/image.png
### Week- 6: Khaman Dhokla
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdRFMiEc466qxC3xycDABPVANLoBN1UHqCoynxkmsAo45/image.png) [source](https://pixabay.com/illustrations/khaman-dhokala-india-gujrat-nature-9906996/)
Khaman Dhokla is a very soft, fluffy and delicious Indian (Gujarati) snack made with chickpea flour and steamed. It is served with a sweet, sour and spicy taste, accompanied by mustard and curry leaves.
https://steemitimages.com/640x0/https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmca9Bt9r6sXsWNDjX5Z1bvJarpE5Jb91eHm2NM7x3xAur/image.png

Verification & Winner selection

During these six weeks, participants will be screened for plagiarism and AI content, while participants will be given scores based on how well they understood the concept and how they demonstrated their talent. The top four contestants will be announced as the best for that week. Every week the top 4 best of the week will be selected, and the results will be announced and rewarded, and they will also be nominated for SC01 support. ##### Verification Message:

We always appreciate and support "quality content," and we also respect your hard work. Best of luck.
