author: rojsa
title: Dahi – The Taste of Tradition and Health
text:
🥣 Dahi – The Taste of Tradition and Health

Hello Steemit family! 👋

Today I want to share something simple, delicious, and deeply connected to our daily lives — Dahi (curd/yogurt).

🌿 What is Dahi?

Dahi is a traditional fermented milk product made by adding a small amount of culture to warm milk and letting it rest for a few hours. The natural bacteria transform the milk into thick, creamy, slightly tangy goodness.

It is one of the most common foods in South Asian households, and almost every kitchen has its own way of setting perfect dahi.

⸻

💪 Health Benefits of Dahi

Dahi is not just tasty — it’s packed with nutrition:

✔️ Rich in probiotics – improves digestion
✔️ Strengthens immunity
✔️ Good source of calcium
✔️ Helps cool the body
✔️ Supports gut health

During hot weather, a bowl of chilled dahi or a glass of lassi feels like pure relief!

⸻

🍚 How We Enjoy Dahi

There are so many ways to enjoy it:
	•	With rice (curd rice)
	•	As sweet dahi with sugar or honey
	•	In lassi (sweet or salty)
	•	In raita with vegetables
	•	As an ingredient in curries

Personally, I love eating fresh homemade dahi with a little sugar after meals. It feels light and satisfying.

⸻

🏡 Homemade vs Store-Bought

Homemade dahi has a special taste and texture. It feels more natural and fresh. All you need is:
	1.	Fresh milk
	2.	A spoon of previous dahi (starter)
	3.	A warm place

After 6–8 hours, your dahi is ready!

⸻

🌎 Cultural Importance

In many cultures, dahi is considered pure and auspicious. It is used in festivals, religious ceremonies, and special occasions. In some places, people eat dahi and sugar before starting something important as a symbol of good luck.

⸻

Dahi may be simple, but it carries tradition, health, and comfort in every spoon.

Do you prefer sweet dahi or salty? Let me know in the comments! 😊

#food #health #traditionalfood #dahi #homee
:::

author: flowertron
title: Frozen Arepas - 10 February 2026
text:
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNaiCw1j6EZ3q4jbdqfdP2kaeQNXpE84s5RStjMpj9Bpq/image.png)
Some time ago I wrote about my attempts at preparing some frozen [tequeños](https://steemit.com/food/@flowertron/frozen-tequenos-16-september-2025) I bought at a supermarket. I also bought a bag of arepas that time; I think they were around £4 pounds at the time (or 80+ Steem) :
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmaNj6HL8Dy3qviudhidSt89yRnea8W1A1hDXK5U8f2et2/image.png) I wonder whether this is a well-known brand.
As you can see in the bag above there were 5 ready-made arepas that I just needed to either fry or put in the oven. I opted for the oven method because I thought it was faster and cleaner.
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVWG7YRApj7aDJjsXWcYw8xbMzTHfNMEmtZemUKbq5yZW/image.png)
It was a very straightforward process, but I just couldn’t think what to have on mine ! While I was spreading some butter and my friend cut me a bit of her cheese for me to try, I couldn’t resist taking a photo of the face that appeared on it :
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmaXv1YM5pWCKVnphQykhtt4nRVV3z2GJusvbGjrV7KwMm/image.png) My arepa didn’t look very impressed !
I ended up adding some slices of cucumber on mine as you can see in the first photo to make it refreshing and crunchy. I was actually surprised that after 10 minutes in the oven the ‘dough’ was still soft enough to fold in half. Anyway I was just wondering what you have on your arepas. Do you mainly put cheese and then melt the cheese using the arepa as a pizza base ? Do you have arepas instead of bread ? I still have another box of frozen tequeños, but this time I’ll just fry them, as @marito74 suggested, in a bit of oil to see if it works for me. Thank you for reading !

comments.author: marito74
comments.body:
Yum, delicious arepas, always looking for the creative side, ha ha ha. Arepas can have many fillings, from cheese to meat and more. I hope you try them. They're not the same as the ones I would make for you here in Margarita, and you'll enjoy them here on the beach, but I'm glad you're trying them.

My husband likes them crispy, I don't, but talking about arepas has a long history, my friend, it would require many conversations and arepas. Oh, my friend, how I would love for you to visit Margarita Island 🏖️ 😎 

Fried tequeños, also very tasty when roasted in the oven, with a cup of coffee, have a Venezuelan charm.

replies.author: flowertron
replies.body:
Thank you for all that inspiration @marito74  :   food, travel and a good chat !  

    🧶 🏖️ 🌴🐚 🍹

replies.author: steemcurator09

comments.author: sammy1109
comments.body:
Hello! Arepas are delicious, and it's great that you're trying new foods, but I don't know if it's the angle of the photo, but they look very thin😥😥. I think the best option is to buy corn flour and try making your own arepas. It's more fun, and believe me, they'll be much better😌. You should fill the arepa well with butter and cheese or your favorite filling. I recommend the reina pepiada😋

Arepas are more like hamburgers than pizza bases!!!!!!, but you can fill them with cheese and pepperoni if you want

replies.author: flowertron
replies.body: Thanks for the comment @sammy1109 !   I did think the arepas I bought looked a bit thin in comparison to the ones here on Steemit 😁

replies.author: sammy1109
replies.body: Yup, it's better if you make them, I hope to see the tequeños😋😋😋

comments.author: blessedlife
comments.body:
I don't know the brand since it's Colombian, and besides, we rarely eat frozen arepas; we make them directly with cornmeal. 

What I can say is that they look quite thin compared to what we're used to in most of Venezuela. 

We usually open them and fill them with whatever we want, whether it's protein, combinations of protein and fresh or cooked vegetables, or even plain. 

Next time, you could grill them in a pan; they might puff up a bit, and then you can fill them.

Here some ways we prepare and eat arepas in Venezuela, but there are other ways:
https://steemit.com/hive-108800/@blessedlife/asi-hago-arepas-horneadas
https://steemit.com/hive-108800/@blessedlife/arepitas-viudas-para-sopa
https://steemit.com/hive-108800/@blessedlife/arepas-a-lo-indio-y-a-lo-mantuano

title: Delicious Chicken Jalfrezi
text:
Today, we decided to cook chicken jalfrezi. I went to the market and purchased 1.5 kg of chicken, along with a good amount of onions and potatoes to store for the upcoming Ramadan. I also bought some other ingredients and returned home.

![1000470617.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUy11eJPLBYmeQk99KUhDQXA4D4LdFSBSwjs8x5aq72UH/1000470617.jpg)

My wife chopped all the vegetables, washed the chicken, and started cooking. I went to the masjid to say Asar prayer, and when I returned, the chicken jalfrezi was almost ready. I decided to bring fresh naan, so I went to a restaurant and bought 8 fresh naans.

author: kiwiscanfly
title: Big Storm⛈️💨 Grid outage 🪫🔋
text:
We have had a major storm for the past 18 odd hours with 100 kilometer winds and plenty of rain, last night as the winds got up i checked the Powerwall battery level only to see it was at 5%🪫.

That was after a cloudy rainy day yesterday, so i decided to charge it up while i could nd by 2am we were back to 100%🔋.
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcxZf3r8eujCN6nnpmwtvM3c99CZrYv8ToroX87GXzdbJ/image.png)

We lost power around 4am this morning and the battery has kept us going nicely, now that the storm has passed mostly we have a grey cloudy day but we are producing power from the solar panels.
![Screenshot_20260216_131234_Tesla.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXNBzTf1t4D3Uvdk4wKFttFPViuNNWZQ7BHi6KKQfQAUf/Screenshot_20260216_131234_Tesla.jpg)
The weather forecast for the upcoming week is looking far nicer.


-----------

In other event i brought a couple of new Mountain bike tires for my ride, the current tire's are almost bald and needing replacing.


I decided to go top of the line and pay $230 each for a couple of MAXXIS 26x4.0 Minion FBF 26x4.0 / FBR 26x4.0.
![s-l1600.webp](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWX73Z17zJbmYHU8jPKay1te5E7sRvhZSKUwmsWRcEN6P/s-l1600.webp)
These tires are similar but one is designed for the front tyre to help with steering etc, the one for the back has a grid pattern that focuses on traction.

These should be here within a week then they will be replacing the current ones.


Right now its stopped raining i feel a walk is in order.


Have a good Monday :)

*all pics are mine*
*smudge approves this post*

comments.body:
>....i checked the Powerwall battery level only to see it was at 5%🪫.

That's poor! 

As at this 2am that the battery was fully charged..., were you using a public utility to charge it...?


Also, if the power was 100% full at 2am and by 4am it discharges at that rate....it could be that  your loads were much heavier or perhaps, the battery is getting weak. Do you experience this often even on days with much sun?

I am doing a research/program on **Tesla** product

Maybe i didn't explain it properly LOL

> As at this 2am that the battery was fully charged..., were you using a public utility to charge it...?

yep just the normal power grid to charge it


Powerwall was FULL by 2am and would have sat idle until the grid went down at 4am, then the 100% battery took over and ran the house for the next 15 hours.

> ....i checked the Powerwall battery level only to see it was at 5%🪫.

That's poor!

If we had a bad day for solar generation then the powerwall level will suffer, we had a bad day yesterday for generation

author: live.log
title: Michigan's Global Hustle: Big G Goes European!
text:
Ever wonder what your state governor does when they're not, you know, governing right here at home? Well, if you're in Michigan, Governor Whitmer might just be on a whirlwind European tour, shaking hands and making deals for the good old Mitten State!

[SOURCE](https://www.detroitnews.com/gcdn/authoring/authoring-images/2026/02/09/PDTN/88593221007-20260209-dmme-reading-money-1064.jpg?crop=2105,1184,x0,y615&width=2105&height=1184&format=pjpg&auto=webp)

Think of it like this: she's basically Michigan's super-salesperson, showing off all the cool stuff we make and the awesome talent we have. Her mission? To pump up Michigan's economy, snag some sweet new investments, boost our exports, and create more jobs for folks like us. We're talking about everything from advanced manufacturing (hello, cars!) to clean energy, life sciences, and all things mobility. It's all about making sure Michigan isn't just strong, but stronger!

First stop: Italy! Picture ancient ruins mixed with cutting-edge tech. Governor Whitmer hit up cities like Turin, Milan, and Rome, not for a pizza tour (well, maybe a little, we hope!), but to meet with business bigwigs and government folks. She was basically telling everyone, "Hey, Michigan is open for business, and we're a fantastic place to invest!" She even chatted with the U.S. Ambassador to Italy, making sure Michigan's voice was heard loud and clear on the international stage.

Then it was off to Munich, Germany, for something a bit more serious: the Munich Security Conference. This isn't your average trade show; it's where world leaders talk about, you know, world stuff. Our Governor was right there in the mix, discussing global security with allies and even meeting face-to-face with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. It shows that Michigan isn't just focused on what's happening within our borders, but how we fit into the bigger global picture, supporting democracy and stability wherever we can.

So, what's the big takeaway from this international adventure? Michigan isn't just sitting pretty by the Great Lakes. We're out there, making friends, making deals, and making sure our state stays strong and grows. It's all about bringing home the bacon (or the pasta, in Italy's case!) for Michigan families and ensuring a bright, prosperous future. Go, Michigan, go!

[Original Article](https://www.detroitnews.com/story/news/politics/2026/02/16/michigan-gretchen-whitmer-italy-trade-mission-munich-germany-ukraine/88701545007/)

author: steem-for-future
title: Wonderful experience traveling on holiday with my friend
text:
Assalamualaikum. Greetings Sri Lankan community. How are you all? I hope that by the grace of Allah, you are all doing well. Alhamdulillah, by the grace of Allah, I am also doing well. I pray to Allah that we are always well. Today I want to share with you some of the activities I did with my friend on a weekend afternoon. Basically, I always love to travel. I love traveling and I feel even happier when I get to share my travel stories with you. Last Friday was a weekend. My friend works a little in an office and my friend's office was closed on Friday. Since it was a holiday, we had a lot of fun together in the afternoon and I enjoyed the entire afternoon's activities very much. ![PSX_20260216_134947.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXcyiQnDEFHPCPnHDq1BDqH2toVhE5v9YC5okExW2JTML/PSX_20260216_134947.jpg) In the afternoon, we both went out for a bike ride. After going out, we were very happy. We saw the crops in the empty fields and the natural beauty impressed us a lot. Since I live in the village, the beauty of the village constantly impresses me. In the afternoon, I enjoyed the natural beauty of the village, including the scene of collecting date juice and the crops around. Then we went to a garment shop in the afternoon. ![PSX_20260216_135149.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPz8wCbpRqbVdfDKFJUotP9NsUWyN3R3Nj6K3z8A4TBCg/PSX_20260216_135149.jpg) In the afternoon we went to a clothing store and we chose a Barcelona jersey. Finally we bought a master copy jersey for 350 taka. We actually saw several other products in that store. We bought the jerseys from a reliable store that sells and sells very high quality sports boots and jerseys and all kinds of jerseys. Then we went to a Honda garage. ![PSX_20260216_135021.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYnPEmoUtX4aEEbtyomBWj1RsDgZSB2xgK8MzMkUucEmw/PSX_20260216_135021.jpg) ![PSX_20260216_135100.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPJi5Cjc2e3ERsExSgNeVot7J3yEjhaXz8RdndZn6WpnY/PSX_20260216_135100.jpg) We reached a Honda servicing point. We got our car's carburetor serviced there. The sound of the car was a bit weak. The sound of the car was fixed and some gear work was done. Then our car's headlights and some other servicing were needed. We left the car at the servicing center and went to an old tea shop for tea. ![PSX_20260216_134909.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmaaz842owDAEgmwz7BwsQMJaZPYEBj2VzYPQMQkYPMmqJ/PSX_20260216_134909.jpg) The tea shop is a very old shop. Only colored tea was sold there. Each cup of tea cost only 5 taka. We really enjoyed sitting in that tea shop. I have never seen such an old tea shop before. Most of the people there were old and old people were drinking it and watching Bengali movies on TV. Such a simple scene of the village people always fascinates me. Finally, we finished drinking tea in the afternoon and went to the Honda garage. After going there, we paid the warehouse bill, that is, paid the service charge. After paying the service charge, we left for home. After leaving for home, we saw a market. Going to that market, I bought one kilogram of potatoes and 500 grams of sweet pumpkin. After that, I had another cup of tea. After drinking it, we left for our village market. We reached the market at seven in the evening and after reaching the market, I said goodbye to my friend and left for home. Basically, I enjoyed the whole day's activities very much. I felt very good after spending the afternoon with my friend and my mind felt light. Finally, if you want, you can also share with us the moments of your small trips with your friends. Thank you very much for enjoying today's small travel story.
# `ᏒᎬᎶᎪᏒᎠs ` ## [Achievement 1](https://steemit.com/hive-172186/@steem-for-future/achievement1-or-or-or-bangladesh-or-or-or-introduction) ![21PRtjKRXPQybj4WUXScWv5QPLninWRxfbcWNsx7SenD7FrhkCa1FNh95pCuKfX2zFADCawA8xHb1weasemMPCdBKdwpZjwnxZgGuS1bfpFGdAzMmJ7B4T7gHvKaPnXPkdS7x6z4uy79QYvGDMeBES9.gif](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXbhkt6S45FJ2EBoPRLrVYsoUMTnWHQ9UeUqEcvhxzYde/21PRtjKRXPQybj4WUXScWv5QPLninWRxfbcWNsx7SenD7FrhkCa1FNh95pCuKfX2zFADCawA8xHb1weasemMPCdBKdwpZjwnxZgGuS1bfpFGdAzMmJ7B4T7gHvKaPnXPkdS7x6z4uy79QYvGDMeBES9.gif) @STEEM-FOR-FUTURE

title: Pupha Mahanatee Garden at Queen Sirikit Park, Bangkok
text:
https://i.postimg.cc/85cQLYqN/Pupha-Mahanatee-Garden18.jpg

Queen Sirikit Park has a very large area which is divided into many sections.  One of the interesting sections is “Pupha Mahanatee Garden”.  (Pupha means mountain or hill, Mahanatee means river or lake or pond)  

Pupha Mahanatee Garden provides a beautiful landscape with a relaxing spot for walking, jogging or admiring gorgeous flowers with various species.

author: thevpnboss
title: Upcoming Events in Belfast: What’s Happening in the City
text:
Belfast is gearing up for a lively late spring and early summer in 2026. From major concerts to large-scale sporting events and open-air festivals, the city is once again proving why it’s one of the most exciting destinations on the island of Ireland. If you’re planning a visit — or you’re lucky enough to live here — now is the perfect time to check out what’s coming up.

For a full overview of concerts, festivals, and cultural happenings, you can always visit the official **[What’s Happening in Belfast guide](https://www.belfastguide.org/whats-happening/)** and stay up to date.


![belfastevents.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQEmYXTTJDL8KvXip8GQ4VnBwPLFVfZjo7XfaFDagJLLS/belfastevents.png)




## May 2026 Highlights

### 🎤 Alex Warren Concert – May 2, 2026  
The SSE Arena Belfast will host Alex Warren for what promises to be an energetic night of live music. The SSE Arena is one of the city’s premier indoor venues, known for attracting international artists and delivering unforgettable performances. Expect a packed crowd and plenty of atmosphere.

### 🏃 Belfast Marathon 2026 – May 3, 2026  
Just one day later, the city streets will be transformed for the **Belfast Marathon 2026**. This iconic event brings thousands of runners and spectators together. Whether you’re participating or cheering from the sidelines, the marathon creates an incredible buzz throughout the city. Hotels, cafés, and pubs fill up quickly, so planning ahead is essential.

## June 2026: Festival Season Begins

### 🎶 Belsonic Festival – June 12 – July 1, 2026  
Ormeau Park will once again become the beating heart of Belfast’s music scene during **Belsonic Festival 2026**. Running from mid-June into early July, this outdoor festival brings a diverse lineup of international and local artists. The park setting gives the event a relaxed yet electric summer vibe — perfect for warm evenings and big crowds.

### 🤠 Zach Bryan Concert – June 23–24, 2026  
Country star Zach Bryan will take the stage at **Boucher Road Playing Fields** for two nights. Outdoor concerts here typically draw large audiences, creating a festival-like atmosphere. If you’re a fan of Americana and heartfelt lyrics, this is one of the standout events of the season.

### 🎉 Pitbull Concert – June 30, 2026  
To round off the month, Pitbull is scheduled to perform in Belfast. Expect high-energy hits, crowd participation, and a party atmosphere. It’s shaping up to be one of the biggest pop events of early summer.

---

## Why Visit Belfast This Spring and Summer?

What makes Belfast special isn’t just the events — it’s the way the whole city gets involved. Restaurants extend their hours, pubs host after-parties, and the streets feel alive with visitors from across the UK and beyond.

With major concerts at the SSE Arena, open-air performances in Ormeau Park, large-scale shows at Boucher Road, and the annual marathon taking over the streets, the coming months offer something for every taste.

If you’re planning a trip, it’s wise to secure accommodation early and keep an eye on the official event listings. You can explore more details and discover additional events on the **[Belfast events calendar](https://www.belfastguide.org/whats-happening/)**.

Belfast 2026 is shaping up to be loud, lively, and unforgettable.

author: dove11
title: A Trip to Passport Office and Back Home
text:
![Image cover](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbPaPB9cBBpuJfqhtqfDruyYiWnwQskkQuDb4UkQHwQwt/20260211_160626.jpg)
On 11th February I visited passport office for renewable of my passport. I went by bus but returned by RapiD Rail. On my way back, I boarded the sleek Namo Bharat train at Sahibabad station, the doors whoosh shut behind me as we glide over smoothly on elevated tracks. I saw zipping pass the 6 intermediate stations between Sahibabad and Meerut South on the Delhi-Meerut RRTS (Rapid Rail) The route Ghaziabad, Guldhar, Duhai, Murad Nagar, Modi Nagar South, and Modi Nagar North.
![20260211_162746.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdBUP6d88SaMaZkhVqbr1obqhbGPm5HcxURADhd4K7kFH/20260211_162746.jpg)
The journey covers about 39 km and takes approximately 30-31 minutes. The train kicks in at about 100 km/h within seconds. The Ghaziabad skyline fades quickly, then the urban scene gives way to open views of highways and fields below. We zip past Guldhar, a quick blur of platforms and commuters, then Duhai where the depot hums with maintenance activity. As I cross Murad Nagar, spotting local markets and rivers glinting in the sun. The ride is whisper-quiet, seats comfy in economy class. I saw Modi Nagar South's industrial zones roll by.​ Modi Nagar North is next, a brief stop amid green patches and distant factories. Speed builds again, viaducts soaring over NH-34 traffic jams I'd otherwise endure for hours. Meerut's outskirts emerge, Shatabdi Nagar whizzes past non-stop on this route, then I decelerate into Meerut South.
![20260211_163542.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPiGAPamDGyQMHiXEMDUjFRkMeuLbLeDxLAos6sYjLTp3/20260211_163542.jpg)
Exactly 31 minutes later, doors open at the modern terminal. Three platforms, massive parking for my scooter pickup. What took me 2 hours by road while going in the morning, this seamless high-speed thrill, revolutionized my commute home to Modipuram. No worries but pure efficiency!
[IPFS Video](https://gateway.pinata.cloud/ipfs/bafybeicp2csdue6x542fnclf5fx6ffnh6drdv65xow5kw2nmaazhven6ke)
Posted with [Speem](https://speem.watch/)

author: rayhan111
title: A Field Where Community Comes Alive
text:
|
Assalamu Alaikum / Adabah🤝
| |-|
|
Hello friends, how are you all? Hope everybody is healthy. I am also very good in your prayers. I am rayhan111, from Rajshahi division of Bangladesh.
| |-|
![1000027433.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYzwmBP51gFFmDD7hZm1iWo8QB613KJifoVP84zU1JbH9/1000027433.jpg) Under the vast, open sky, the field becomes more than just a stretch of dry earth it transforms into a living portrait of community. These images capture a powerful moment where people of all ages gather, not for spectacle, but for participation. Children stand curious by the fence, elders watch with calm patience, and families arrive together, carrying conversations, expectations, and shared purpose. The simplicity of the place only amplifies the depth of the moment. The wide ground tells a quiet story of unity. There are no grand stages or loud banners, yet the presence of people fills the space with meaning. Lines form naturally, shaped by patience rather than force. Women dressed in colorful attire stand side by side, men wait thoughtfully, and children observe with wide eyes, learning what it means to belong to something larger than themselves. This is civic life in its most honest form rooted in people, not perfection. ![1000027432.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXmty5c7QtNmk8aiSNJtHrN45uWebdAzWKDCRpc6A1j2k/1000027432.jpg) What stands out most is the balance between movement and stillness. Some walk purposefully across the field, others pause to talk, and many simply wait. Motorbikes rest along the roadside, vendors linger with their carts, and neighbors greet one another with familiarity. It feels like a shared pause in the rhythm of daily life, where routine gives way to responsibility and presence. The fence separating the field from the road is symbolic. On one side, the orderly process unfolds; on the other, life continues steps, conversations, and quiet observation. Yet there is no real division. Everyone is connected by the same moment, the same space, and the same sense of participation. Even those watching are part of the story. These images remind us that community is not built in grand halls but in open fields, shared waits, and collective moments. Democracy, responsibility, and togetherness often appear ordinary but in their ordinariness lies strength. This field, filled with people and purpose, becomes a reminder that progress begins when individuals show up together, calmly and consciously, to shape their shared future. ![1000027431.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmecFSa7Kkk36jnDrtnHCryKyvQhCPevd7aQqh28sFT1ae/1000027431.jpg)

[Location](https://w3w.co/arise.pipeline.handed)

author: ngoenyi
title: I witnessed The Commissioning Of The Omenuko Bridge by The Governor Of Abia State, Dr Alex Chioma Oti
text:
https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXWGdcu735bcF34jZWSzYZ2hgkWWUQ1PUy5dH6g5DLGsk/1001068498.jpg
the New look of Omenuko Bridge. Picture from staff WhatsApp group
Omenuko Bridge has claimed so many lives in the past due to its dilapidated condition . It became a death trap that so many dreaded. All hope was lost as a result of incompetent construction companies or wil I say that the past administration were not ready and willing to construct the bridge. They kept giving excuses that the forces in the water didn't allow them to construct it. This particular bridge has finally been constructed after decades of sad news on it as it has claimed the lives of so many people in the community and beyond. All thanks to the Almighty who used the present governor of Abia State, Alex Chioma Oti to lift my people out of danger. .In my school where I am working, we had a congregation that was convened by our Rector on Wednesday, February 11, 2026 who used that avenue to invite everyone to his hometown because he is also from the same village with me. He also declared that Friday, a lecture free day and promised that there will be more than enough vehicles that will convey both students and lecturers to the community where this commissioning will take place. It was a joyful occasion and though I was having having double minds wether to attend or not due to my own lectures at the Akwaibom State University, I kept thinking about it until that very Friday morning, 13th day of February 2026 when the commissioning was to take place. I called my older brother who has been advertising this event as the President General of my own community to ask how the preparation was going, he told me that he was with my Rector the day before as they were making the arrangements and he was able to tell the Rector that I am the sister. Out of that excitement of the fact that the Rector has known me so well, I was moved to attend. I got ready and zoomed to my school where vehicles were already taking people to the venue. There were still enough vehicles to convey people. I got my t-shirt but it was too big and I didn't bother to wear it. ![1001063769.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXNsyybGzoGT1R6DnG2ByNEjEHBrQZbYEzgDenH335ivx/1001063769.jpg)|when I got to the school, going to enter vehicle |---|---| Inside the vehicle, ready to move|![1001063810.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXaguoDGxetaoLvrS6VzWykJ6Tdh2bb37cPKpfQcBDUMS/1001063810.jpg) ![1001064030.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUUqZVAN2R5Z1HMSTmdTxs6TatXTBtMj6ca4Gm7HbchQ1/1001064030.jpg)|official vehicle of the Governor close to the venue of the event As soon as we got there, the Governor was already there and the commissioning was ongoing. I quickly spotted my brother who was gorgeously dressed as one of the organisers. He was very happy to see me and we took pictures ![1001064032.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVSopaH4sjHnMec94G5SGcUSW689e1eDDALdbp9ukRCKM/1001064032.jpg)|with my older brother, the PG in my Village |--|--| ![1001064034.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmU6Yy6pSnjDauyvZuoHvYC1oFfyu8EnpqVaP3YsJd2yzF/1001064034.jpg)|with the PRO of my village union who joined us ![1001064040.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmb3yZCqMR3erv3TNeDhmGLZj7AD2CVLxUwJecjpY1sTi9/1001064040.jpg)|with my brother again ![1001064043.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTj41e5n14yRBYYz6rvVvj2dRdaPrNuYBNJnyxwYm7hv4/1001064043.jpg)|with people from my village I crossed the bridge following the old dilapidated bridge on foot since the old one was over crowded. I had to carry out my adventurous mission of going down to the river, washing my hands and drinking the water and also to capture the bridge from down there. One of the videos I have uploaded here will show that. Then, I quickly left that bridge to the Ozu Abam High School where my people are to host the Governor.. The music was on, the stage was set and everyone apart from those at the commissioning spot were seated including the traditional rollers from all the villages in Abam. My on Eze was there too. I reached out to him and exchanged pleasantry. As it's my custom, I took a picture of him as he was seated. The event began as soon as the commissioning was over and the Governor and his Entourage arrived . Dignitaries were introduced and recognised, cola nut was shared, speeches were given in turns from the sons of the soil who held one position of office or the other in the State. They were all overwhelmed by the show of love that Governor has shown to my people. There were a lot of cultural displays including the masquerades from various communities, traditional dance groups and all the rest of them. Then, the Executive Governor of Abia State, mounted the pulpit and gave his own speech as well as made promises of other commissionings that will take place in no distant time of which my own kindred will benefit from. It was really a joyful moment. I was so happy that after so many years, we are remembered. Come to think of it, it is through him that I am able to get a government work in my own state which has given me this opportunity to witness this grand event. I am so happy and I thank the Almighty for this opportunity. Yes, there are still good people who are doing good. While we await the time when all things will be made new everywhere and not just in some areas by our Dear God Jehovah through his Son Jesus Christ, we will make wise use of the opportunities we get now. ![1001064050.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPpgyE9aJEnPacGBEHU9A161mKksLBRpqRKwQ7zEV1N4Y/1001064050.jpg)| you can see the Eze or Chief of my own kindred at the middle there |---|---| ![1001064047.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdmUUoiPbXMM1Ty7z2zMquFMF1zjaMBZe1qUrpKH7Y5vt/1001064047.jpg)|the traditional dance group from my village ![1001064055.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdHYnnLCFt6ZFWg9JbkjCnmsJWg8jZ5pCpK5nNCMXdJVf/1001064055.jpg)|the Rector of my school walking to the stage to help organise things ![1001064053.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQaXSR9GzeyiahQt1ayc4mMvCRNr6aXsERTyvnyJTmPue/1001064053.jpg)|the Rector of my school who is the chairman of the organising committee giving directions of the order of events ![1001064058.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYQQb7ksGAbnoZJYFWdzrUhnLFfs7sfe7qvBiZ5hBpxPg/1001064058.jpg)|the place of the event with the crowd ![1001064060.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPwyFXZGNJVWKumPXi6rxqFtKQ6JMaj9dRBMJFuE3EBc4/1001064060.jpg)|the security personnels of the Governor trying to control the crowd ![1001064066.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcvoydg2nkuCVpCChJjxjJcFogsXpKNBDJwAVL9bFxG6e/1001064066.jpg)|The Governor's on the stage, giving his address ![1001064957.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTgRGvLtE7vuPwYQkCwsVp4Ktpxu6qBpb9LxLnDW62Yvc/1001064957.jpg)|the Rector of my school, addressing the students and staff and showing his gratitude ![1001064954.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUHh62nPACvv1zcUqPEhS6uEkqQWkLWCXxbgPX1sx8WpR/1001064954.jpg)|with my colleagues, waiting for our vehicle to move ![1001065018.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmY876xr6TVTJK9Yjeog6R65i3JLSdyDueQ7jYHc2FRwCE/1001065018.jpg)|on our way back inside the vehicle https://youtube.com/shorts/PbUeHKtf-fw?si=-8f1Ft5IKidHkrw7 The video link above shows when I went down to touch the water and get a clearer view of the Omenuko Bridge https://youtube.com/shorts/S8VxV0T_Guo?si=b4jqDc4wPLXLNlop The video above is when the Executive Governor of Abia State mounted the pulpit to give his address, I was so close as to get him https://youtube.com/shorts/6f3rZZ4z9aw?si=bqugivKgTPbDqvzi The video above shows when the Rector, who is also the chairman of the organising committee of this occasion and who invited the staff and students of the Ogbonnaya Onu Polytechnic showing his appreciation for our attendance https://youtube.com/shorts/UkuuQQMkSFk?si=7_2nRZgNebvATG39 The video above shows when we were going back in our vehicle, I had to video as we were going down to cross the Omenuko Bridge back to school The pictures below were taken from my school staff WhatsApp group for clearer view ![1001068496.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQme2eAg7t7mHEbRGxBFSyjTpjL7CoPmFsqpQ95zKbH1bq7/1001068496.jpg)|the traditional rullers on the march to the venue |---|---| ![1001068498.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXWGdcu735bcF34jZWSzYZ2hgkWWUQ1PUy5dH6g5DLGsk/1001068498.jpg)|the new bridge ![1001068501.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPp4aLcAUw4tLmYqHtBvp4JWcqZn1oGmLqwoNVGgytGAE/1001068501.jpg)|the line up of the staff from my school Thank you all for rejoicing with my people! author: nainaztengra
title: Beauty of Oman - Visual Story - Part 2
text:
I mentioned about the beauty of Oman and showcased some of the Sea life of the place. Today I will show you the beauty of the Mountains of this place. But before that let's get to know a little more of Oman. The country's rich history is reflected in its architecture. You will see some of the most magnificent structures here like the Grand Mosque, Royal Opera, The Alam Palace and more of such buildings which gives you a peak of traditional and modern influences.

It's a place which is yet to be discovered. Not many people are aware of this Country yet. It is a perfect destination for travelers who are seeking adventure, natural beauty, calmness and authenticity. Finding a reasonable hotel here is not a problem at all. There are all range of hotels available and I must tell you that even the cheap ones will look like a 4 to 5 star, that's the standard of this place. If you talk about food, you have variety of global cuisines that you can find. That's the beauty of living in a multi-cultural land.

author: maxinpower
title: Do you know Inari =/= Kennt ihr schon Inari? - Aizu Impressions 👹🍣🎎 Wonderful Japan ENG-GER
text: ![IMG_3094.JPG](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/23tbwZHuQ77ZMHD3yvkwVJGN3omaxw2d73TXhhCaiFS87eQS5w4buxmUAHnbcM7srLTHa.JPG) Once again, I look back on one of our recent trips, and once again we are in the Aizu region. Every time we visit, we go to Tsuruga Castle, where we walk a long round through the castle grounds. Although we have been here many times before, there is always something new to discover, and some details attract me again each time. For example, there is as small shrine that stands directly on the castle wall, and which is guarded by two stone figures. These are two statues depicting the Japanese deity Inari Ōkami, who represents rice, fertility, and prosperity and is also revered as a patron saint. Such fox figures are typically found at Inari shrines and are considered mediators between the spiritual world and humans. The statues here are dressed in red fabric, which is traditionally considered a protective color and is often used as an offering or a sign of worship. The large straw hat on their heads is probably more of a seasonal or ritual decoration, and may have been added as protection against ice and snow. Inari shrines always feature two fox figures, which together act as sacred messengers of the deity Inari. At first glance, they appear very similar, but there are a few differences between them that symbolize their respective roles. One of the foxes sometimes holds a key in its mouth, which opens the deity's rice storehouse and thus represents prosperity and food security. The other often carries a jewel or a scroll, which embodies spiritual wisdom or the fulfillment of wishes. They form a harmonious pair that guards and balances both the material and spiritual sides of the place. I have passed many Inari shrines, and somehow they always draw me back. I will probably continue to stop at such places in the future and will certainly take a few photos. I might share them here in another post... ![IMG_3091.JPG](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/23ynSjatMo6HPwCDd43ZxKv9kZe9v8GffrXysyf9ggpzPWJk8QRJskj2Z67yHy8uqyfpx.JPG) DEUTSCH Und wieder schaue ich zurück auf einen unserer letzten Trips und wieder sind wir in der Aizu-Region. Dort besuchen wir jedes Mal die Burg Tsuruga-jo, wo wir eine große Runde durch die Burganlage drehen. Obwohl wir schon des Öfteren hier waren, gibt es immer wieder etwas zu entdecken, und einige Details ziehen mich jedes Mal von Neuem an. So wie zum Beispiel der kleine Schrein, der direkt auf der Burgmauer steht, und vor dem zwei steinerne Figuren wachen halten. Es handelt sich um zwei Statuen, die die japanischen Gottheit Inari Ōkami zeigen, Diese steht für Reis, Fruchtbarkeit, Wohlstand und wird auch als Schutzheilige verehrt. Solche Fuchsfiguren stehen typischerweise an Inari‑Schreinen und gelten als Vermittler zwischen der spirituellen Welt und den Menschen. Die Statuen hier tragen roten Stoff, der traditionell als Schutzfarbe gilt und häufig als Opfergabe oder als Zeichen der Verehrung angebracht wird. Der große Strohhut auf dem Kopf ist wohl eher saisonaler oder ritueller Schmuck, und möglicherweise als Schutz vor Eis und Schnee angebracht. An Inari‑Schreinen stehen immer zwei Fuchsfiguren, die gemeinsam als heilige Boten der Gottheit Inari fungieren. Sie wirken auf den ersten Blick sehr ähnlich, haben aber doch einige Unterschiede, die ihre jeweilige Rolle symbolisieren sollen. Einer der Füchse hält manchmal einen Schlüssel im Maul, der das Reislager der Gottheit öffnet und damit Wohlstand und Nahrungssicherheit repräsentiert. Der andere trägt häufig ein Juwel oder eine Schriftrolle, die spirituelle Weisheit, göttliche Botschaften oder die Erfüllung von Wünschen verkörpert. Sie bilden ein harmonisches Paar, das sowohl die materielle als auch die spirituelle Seite des Inari‑Glaubens bewacht und ausbalanciert. Ich bin schon an vielen Inari-Schreinen vorbeigekommen, und irgendwie ziehen sie mich auch immer wieder an. Und auch in Zukunft werde ich wohl des Öfteren an solchen Orten stoppen und mit Sicherheit auch ein paar Fotos machen. Dieses gibt es dann vielleicht in einen weiteren Beitrag hier zu sehen... ![blog-ende.png](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/23tv6H18WKn98nBVkn2MwVhwWsuFRFgjBGwxLEMVhRZD7zRRbFokaNiAn39fUaB3TxVX6.png)

author: rayhan111
title: Where the Earth Breathes in Silence
text:
|
Assalamu Alaikum / Adabah🤝
| |-|
|
Hello friends, how are you all? Hope everybody is healthy. I am also very good in your prayers. I am rayhan111, from Rajshahi division of Bangladesh.
| |-|
![1000027216.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdjr2JycguGWbUNbtUcGTgnkkouCNGLLzQtBf3P9Dvcbb/1000027216.jpg) The beauty of rural life often reveals itself in quiet moments, far away from the noise of cities and the rush of modern living. These images capture such a moment where nature stands calm, honest, and deeply connected to human life. A narrow earthen path winds gently through green fields, inviting travelers to walk slowly, to feel the soil beneath their feet, and to listen to the soft rhythm of the countryside. The lone tree standing beside the path feels like a silent guardian of the land. Its branches, shaped by time and weather, reflect resilience and patience. Though not perfectly shaped, it carries a unique beauty reminding us that growth does not always follow symmetry, yet it always holds meaning. This tree has witnessed seasons change, crops grow and fade, and people pass by with stories in their hearts. ![1000027215.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmba4qQmGLtod92ebVAYLNwu5pvtYEjAVQFroXiLDZYuwB/1000027215.jpg) In the distance, the open fields stretch endlessly, painted in shades of green and gold. Farmers work quietly, their labor blending into the landscape as naturally as the river that flows beside the land. The water body nearby mirrors the sky above, creating a sense of peace that feels almost timeless. It nourishes the fields, supports life, and stands as a symbol of continuity and hope. Walking through such a place brings a sense of grounding. The air feels lighter, the mind clearer. There is no urgency here only the gentle reminder that life can be simple and meaningful at the same time. Every step along the path tells a story of hard work, harmony, and coexistence between humans and nature. These scenes teach us the value of slowing down and appreciating what often goes unnoticed. In a world driven by speed and ambition, the village landscape whispers a different truth: real wealth lies in peace, connection, and balance. The land does not ask for much only respect and care and in return, it offers nourishment for both body and soul.Such moments stay with us long after we leave, reminding us where we come from and what truly matters. ![1000027214.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZYMfrhKG2ZTQtx5JFu9bsaRzBj9D98jrZeLrdCH53SsS/1000027214.jpg)

[Location](https://w3w.co/arise.pipeline.handed)
## ***
Phone Details
***
Camera | Redmi Not 6 pro ------------ | ------------- Catagory | photography📸 C.MODEL | Not 6 Pro Capture | @rayhan111 Location | Sirajganj - BD[🇧🇩]

##
🌹💞Special Thanks to All Friends who are supporting This Post.🌺🌹

author: nainaztengra
title: Beauty of Oman - Visual story - Part 1
text:
I have always mentioned how beautiful the Country of Oman is. It is a country with a blend of tradition, culture and natural beauty. It's also called the hidden gem of Middle East. Unlike other Middle East countries where you see tall sky-scrappers here in Oman you will not find it. Which retains the beauty of this place, offering scenic views from any part of the city. Oman is also famous for its port businesses since it has a long coastline running through the country. Right from the 6th century Oman has been an important trading hub connecting between the East and West.

This Country welcomes people from all over the world with job opportunities to offer. Here it's like truly living multi-cultural.

Here are some pictures of Oman showcasing its beauty. I love the beach life of Oman and today I am going to focus on that.

author: live.log
title: **Winter Wonderland Dates: Skip the Snow Day Snooze-Fest and Spark Some Joy!**
text:
Brrr! Is it just me, or does winter sometimes feel like a giant blanket of "stay indoors and binge-watch everything"? Don't get me wrong, I love a good duvet day as much as the next person, but our love lives deserve more than just another night on the couch, right? Good news, my chilly companions! Winter is actually a secret goldmine for some seriously memorable, super-fun, and even wildly romantic date ideas. Forget the cold, let's turn up the heat on your winter outings!

### **Embrace the Flakes: Outdoor Adventures That Are Anything But Chilly!**

Who says winter means hibernation? Nope! It means strapping on some gear and getting out there!

*   **Shred the Gnar (or Just Wobble Downhill!):** Whether you're a pro skier or a snowboard-curious newbie, hitting the slopes is an absolute blast. Imagine the crisp air, the stunning views, and maybe a few adorable tumbles together. Afterward? Hot chocolate by a roaring fire. Pure bliss!
*   **Quiet Trails, Big Views:** Not into speed? Grab some snowshoes or cross-country skis and find a peaceful trail. It's like hiking, but with extra crunch and way more picturesque scenery. You'll get your steps in, breathe some fresh air, and maybe even spot some wildlife. Hello, nature therapy!
*   **Inner Child Unleashed: Sledding & Tubing!** This one's a no-brainer for guaranteed giggles. Find a local hill, grab a sled (or rent a giant tube!), and race each other down. It's pure, unadulterated fun, and honestly, nothing breaks the ice (pun intended!) like a good belly laugh.
*   **Twirl on Ice:** Classic. Romantic. And a little bit wobbly (which just means more hand-holding!). Find an outdoor ice rink, crank up some tunes, and pretend you're in a rom-com. Even if you spend more time holding onto the barrier, it's a super cute way to spend an afternoon.

### **Melt Away the Chill: Unique & Cozy Date Escapes**

After all that cold-weather fun, it’s time to warm up. But forget the same old coffee shop!

*   **Hot Springs Hideaway:** Seriously, what's better than soaking in warm, mineral-rich water when it's freezing outside? Picture steam rising around you as you relax and chat. It’s like a mini-vacation without leaving the state! Talk about ultimate relaxation goals.
*   **Culinary Comfort:** Elevate your cozy factor with a delicious meal. Maybe try that new restaurant you've been eyeing, or even better, find a cafe with a fireplace and indulge in gourmet hot cocoa and pastries. Good food, warm ambiance, and great company – recipe for a perfect date!
*   **Spa Day for Two:** If you're really looking to unwind and treat yourselves, a couples' spa day is the ultimate indulgence. Massages, warm pools, steam rooms... you'll emerge feeling refreshed, reconnected, and ready to conquer the rest of winter!

### **Inside Scoop: Fun That's Anything But Stagnant!**

When the weather really just isn't cooperating, or you're just craving some indoor excitement, these ideas are sure to keep the sparks flying.

*   **Get Creative in the Kitchen:** Sign up for a cooking class together! Learn to make pasta, sushi, or some exotic dessert. Not only is it a fun, hands-on experience, but you also get to eat your delicious creations afterward. Plus, teamwork makes the dream work (and the dinner!).
*   **Brainiac Adventures:** Hit up a local museum for a dose of culture, or challenge your minds with an escape room! Nothing brings people together like solving puzzles under pressure, or pondering ancient artifacts. Who knew learning could be so date-worthy?
*   **Unleash Your Inner Lumberjack (or Rock Star!):** Ever tried axe throwing? It's surprisingly therapeutic and a total blast. Or maybe indoor rock climbing? How about a classic bowling match or hitting up an arcade for some retro gaming? These active, slightly competitive dates are fantastic for laughs and breaking out of routine.
*   **Catch a Show:** From a live concert to a play, or even a local comedy night, experiencing live entertainment together is a fantastic way to bond and create shared memories. Dress up, grab a drink, and enjoy the show!

So there you have it! Winter is officially canceled as an excuse for boring dates. Grab your partner, pick an adventure (or two!), and make some amazing memories this season. Stay warm, stay happy, and keep those date nights sizzling!

**Original article inspiration:** [Utah Stories: Winter Date Ideas in Utah](https://utahstories.com/2026/02/winter-date-ideas-utah/)

author: imranhassan
title: The Diary Game || 04-02-26 ||  After working 8 hours on duty, I went to Gulistan Market in the afternoon to buy products.
text:
**Assalamu alaikum, everyone.** I am [@imranhassan](https://steemit.com/@imranhassan) from [#Bangladesh](https://steemit.com/trending/bangladesh)
How are you all? I hope you are well. By the grace of God, I am also well. Today I am sharing with you my daily activity on Wednesday, 04-02-26. ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZE4tfaa5eezGsD1gDTQExe5yJauGeuTHZt4Y6c5HuFcy/image.png) Edited with Canva. On Wednesday I was on duty from 6 am to 2 pm. In the morning I went on duty and did all the work like every day and came home at 8 am to have breakfast. ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmX2UGdZxjSJVD4NzqW43E64Y2VUyUGGfmSZi5TQM7CfT8/image.png)|![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmeW8cHNv6oTVxoqfRGJoTSoTmXErToaSv3hh7xPjprVqZ/image.png)| |-|-| ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdKsBvioA6xLmGkDmGdGcx1UVBPv2NvviUV9SwWJPdC9K/image.png)|![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfJ71gfvYVbKUqiceUifh4V1ZisA5Qh3qKk8jyWEUUqnm/image.png)| |-|-| When I came home in the morning, I first observed my pigeon babies and arranged to feed them for a while. Then I went to the roof of the building and observed the plants in my seven gardens, and the plants have now grown and started yielding. ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdniNSthTPU5LEwGTKzk7JdUL4fKkwsfwxEUbaFQyAn2y/image.png)|![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmW1EvLSNY8wP3R9oT6poxMVrPZ51VL1pBXFqg78m6wh5A/image.png)| |-|-| ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbsaGeqJZPuiq4xeaGb1XX53TMAC2MRHChqHMNSBTzCFQ/image.png)|![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSMvfKwz5RbisB3aVA89HVN1yGJxfcwnYRJtWu8rEWsSu/image.png)| |-|-| Green chillies, tomatoes, gourds, and black brinjals – four crops were planted; all of them have yielded crops, and the production capacity is good for eating. After observing them, I have breakfast at home in the morning and then go back to the factory and start my work. ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRx2UCF5tj6VGDajRBPpt1TLffUhp2G2ggL8YgRaFsVqN/image.png)|![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbLTUihYZR5tP6My2mnT54jBahZVxcvqdtRHxLaVrJxPM/image.png)|![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWkFwu7McnYnR2pmpdzwARuTgxU8gbCEVJdSFMpGydp1Y/image.png)| |-|-|-| Now our new section has been launched, which is selling soybean oil in the open market inside the village, which is called the drum filling section. There is now a lot of work pressure; there is more time than duty, and I work till 2 pm and finish my duty and come home. ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNtJk2uGu79RBTmiurZrSFQ8yHBDUiWjwi4qqpmYpGZEc/image.png)|![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmaSELqemku1TZhfBCQ7Nzqsj1G3pvahrcswFHuHLGP87R/image.png)| |-|-| ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZtCGmco2byqUUQJYfodtMhiqiRqeQhXxmwNYUm5Jg6Sb/image.png)|![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTbiUTtyfHADbsPQUm4xHJt1gzNGvNrG2EXgkUag4WNhV/image.png)| |-|-| Then in the afternoon after resting for a while, I went to Dhaka Gulistan Nawabpur Electronics Market to buy, but I did not know that it was closed on Shab-e-Barat that day. When I went there, I saw everything was closed; no shops were open. I just went there with difficulty, and no work was done. ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRBjmuVCbGXVryHoT96jtsEUDgWfFGapRNMDHc6nbJjaA/image.png)|![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRMZpJNpGnENnDaDAnv1ndeyjeYnYGxg1BW84m9BTK9Zj/image.png)| |-|-| ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYqKQkL2dnzp6cFpU8mgvbyoePci7ZpHcwGMrbnVeFXay/image.png)|![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbcVSgx5ZNDkY51s65juFyUDt64qoG9KPKcZ2FJnSvWJk/image.png)| |-|-| Then I paid 40 taka from there and got in the car from the counter and came to our area. It was night, but I spent money and wasted time. I could not do anything, you know. I gave a new electronics shop a visit, then came home and spent some time in that shop.
![imranhassan (1).gif](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmaYUmHW4x4zUkhT2U4sLmmTgrgVXwQodvWiTV3y7xtvHw/imranhassan%20(1).gif)
🟩 Thank you for reading my post and giving your valuable time. Stay well and stay healthy. God bless you. 🟩
| **Photography Details** | **📱 Device:** Walton Xanon90 | **📍 Location:** Narayanganj, Bangladesh | **📷 Captured By:** @imranhassan | |-------------------------|------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|----------------------------------|

author: dove11
title: Application for Steemit Challenge Season 30: Travel Videography Challenge
text:
Hi all, This is @dove11 on behalf speem.watch submitting our application for "The Steemit Challenge Season 30" along with aeljoseseven.
![DSC01651.png 111.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZxURJfCSUfUuhxdZij3KYgbX4EmaXUfncK82toxEYdeN/DSC01651.png 111.png)
In each Steemit Learning Challenge organized by the Steemitblog , we see different competitions and everyone joins these competitions with joy and full interest to show their best. Since photography and videography are one of the crucial parts of Steemit platform as we interestingly notice it with increasing number of videos we receive with meaningful content, we feel sure the contest will attract a large participation once again. * We have come up with a photography/videography idea for this season with videos published through speem.watch in any community. * We shall organize this contest under the name and style "Travel Photography & Videography Challenge"
![20251103_135642.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVBMfDmqg4KSssF7miohxwaTQKekQ7StxxVfofgh89KNj/20251103_135642.jpg)
@dove11
#### The Team | Name/Experience | SP, CLUB, REP | Discord/Telegram | | ------------- | --------------------------- |-----------| | @dove11 Ex. Steem representative, Video watcher Steam Curator, Moderation experience in Traveling Steem and Hindwhale communities, Several "Learning Challenges" independently. | 76313+, #75, 80.67 | Discord @dove11 #7685 |@alejos7ven Ex. Steem representative, Admin speem.video, Latina Community Manager/Dev also Seven Network mod and Curator | 57976+, #5050, 82.60 | @alejos7ven Our idea is to bring the best out of traveling Steemians from popular destinations with their personal shot photos and videos.​ I believe everyone usually has their own experiences for traveling therefore we suggest a contest that should bring best travel photos with a specific hashtag every week for six weeks from Steemians based on: #### How people can interact and get engaged with the project? As we are aware that there are many very good photographers and videographers who can share their creativity with everyone through their posts therefore, we propose this contest in line with this competition by combining both photography and videography. * We propose that every user shares both videos and photography through their posts and help enrich Steemit Platform. * We propose personal travel photographs and videos which is one of the most popular niche which will be acceptable and accessible to most users. * We are suggesting a contest that should bring best travel photos with a specific hashtag every week for six weeks from Steemians based on: * We have kept it simple so users can participate with their existing phones or cameras since almost all phones have good cameras and often have different modes which will help create them quality photos and videos without going for expensive equipment besides most users know how to edit their videos through free online Apps, I am sure this contest will be an instant hit. ***Now we give you an outline for contest topics:*** Our idea is to bring the best out of traveling Steemians from popular destinations with their peranal shot photo and videos tailored for Steemit platform.​ I believe everyone usually has their own experiences and wisdom. * Cultural Immersions Describe encountering a local tradition, e.g., "Festival Magic in My Hometown," outlining sensory experiences and lessons in the essay. Video shows rituals, dances, or food tasting through quick cuts, highlighting colors and sounds for engagement. * Adventure Challenges Narrate an adrenaline rush like "My Travel Dare," covering prep, thrill, and takeaways in 500 words. Short video uses action cams or edits to replay the peak moment, such as a cycle ride or hike, keeping viewers hooked. * Sustainable Escapes Essay on "Eco-Friendly Getaway Tips" explains low-impact choices and their impact. Video demonstrates practical demos, like zero-waste packing or park cleanups, with on-location shots to inspire green travel habits. * Foodie Discoveries Recount "Street Food Hunt Gone Wild" with flavors, vendors, and stories with 2-3 minute clip featuring tasting reactions, surrounding market idea, and recipes, tying into local favorites for relatable appeal. We intend to ask for their dream vacations, hotels, airlines, their foodie adventure, package and travel methods. >Ideal itineraries Travel preferences Beach or Mountains? Storytelling Prompts Encourage videos * We propose a travel contests limited to 500-1000 words with minimum 3 minute videos that blends best with topics that concise storytelling, visuals, and personal passion, fitting our Steemit platform. * These formats encourage quick, emotional narratives around journeys, discoveries, and tips, drawing from popular destination in user-generated content.​
#### We hereby provide: Schedule for six weeks https://youtube.com/shorts/OQ0xKRwbp2k?si=DqKXTMyV31BFGXRd
***Week-1 : Popular Spot*** Participants should share a 400-1000 word story plus a video of their visited spot, like a heritage site or offbeat trail. Videos capture the site's vibe in 2-3 minutes with narration on why it beats tourist traps. This sparks cultural pride and easy filming.​ Describe these 5 points for this "Popular Spot" contest, ensuring your post in 500-1000 word stories and 2-3 minute videos that highlight personal connections and unique vibes. * Entry Requirements Detailed Narrative: Write 500-1000 words describing your visit, including the spot's history, your journey there, and sensory experiences like sounds, atmosphere or whatever you felt best or even worst. * Personal Connection * Explain why this heritage site or offbeat trail resonates with you and your family's memories, cultural significance, or a transformative moment. * Explain in your story and video narration why it surpasses tourist traps, focusing on authenticity, accessibility, or hidden features. ***Video Guidelines*** * Film at least 3 minutes on-site showing panoramic views, close-ups of details, and ambient footage to immerse viewers in the atmosphere. * Your trip should spark cultural pride, engaging and easy filming with your phone or camera. * You should upload your video through speem. * You should add a viewable link of your video on speem on your post at the end.
#### Rules and guidelines > No use of AI in any form No plagiarism No copyrighted images Words minimum 400 Invite 3 friends Write in any language Write in any community of your choice #### Week-2: Travel Mishaps Entrants recount a funny or challenging travel blunder in 500-100 words, visualized in a short video. Topics like "My Worst Flight" or "Lost in Translation Moments" which could be anything highlighting a humorous situation. #### Week-3 : Dream Destination Steemians will describe a personalized dream trip in 500-1000 words, with a video tour using clips, of their trip. They should focus on budget to tell Steemians, emphasizing food stops or parks, etc. #### Week-4 : Local Food Spots Steemians will narrate a 500-100 word descripion for authentic street eats or hidden eateries, paired with a 2-3 minute tasting video. Themes like "My city's best -----secrets" tie into user interests, showcasing flavors, vendors, and tips for family/kid-friendly options.​ #### Week-5: Bucket List Moments Steemians will share one bucket list experience achieved in 500-1000 words, with video highlights of the emotion and . The idea is to bring their visits to their favorite religious spot, mall adventures, or their favorite tales, creating inspiration through their vivid, storytelling as well a video clip. #### Week-6 : Personal Journeys Share a transformative travel memory, such as "A Solo Trip That Changed Me," in 500-100 words detailing challenges and growth. Your video captures key moments with voiceover, like packing mishaps or scenic arrivals. #### Rewards We will publish a weekly report with top six to be rewarded by Steemcurtor01. #### Remarks & Verification Method: Criteria | Marks |Remarks | |--------| ------------|----------| |Plagiarism Free|✅ | |AI Free | ✅ | |Video quality | 2/2 | | |length and format |2/2 | | |Editing | 2/2 | | |Creativity | 2/2 | | |Presentation | 2/2 | |Total Score |0/10 | |Moderated By |
@x
| Regards, @alejos7ven @dove11

author: rayhan111
title: The Joyful Moment of Casting a Vote
text:
|
Assalamu Alaikum / Adabah🤝
| |-|
|
Hello friends, how are you all? Hope everybody is healthy. I am also very good in your prayers. I am rayhan111, from Rajshahi division of Bangladesh.
| |-|
![1000027211.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWPueDkQya9UaTY9sUkvF5TnHgPCzTuCzfuwAPSbzHam2/1000027211.jpg) The moment of casting a vote is more than a civic duty; it is an emotional experience filled with pride, hope, and responsibility. On election day, the air feels different. There is a quiet excitement in the streets as people from all walks of life walk toward polling centers with a shared purpose. Each step carries the weight of dreams, expectations, and the belief that individual voices truly matter. Standing in line among neighbors, elders, and first-time voters creates a powerful sense of unity. Differences in age, profession, or background fade away, replaced by a collective commitment to the future. Smiles, respectful conversations, and patient waiting reflect a deep understanding that democracy thrives on participation. For many, this moment is also a reminder of past struggles of how hard it was for previous generations to secure the right to vote. ![1000027206.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQcP5nCYcpHQMXbougmJnQB5ma7sbBqZuEU1mtryhqevC/1000027206.jpg) Entering the polling booth is a deeply personal experience. It is a quiet space where one reflects on values, hopes for progress, and the kind of society we wish to build. Pressing the button or marking the ballot may take only seconds, but emotionally it feels monumental. In that brief act lies the power to influence leadership, policies, and the direction of the nation. After casting the vote, there is a feeling of relief mixed with joy. The indelible ink on the finger becomes a symbol of participation and pride. It is a visible reminder that one has contributed to shaping the future. People often leave the polling station with brighter faces, knowing they have fulfilled a responsibility not just to themselves, but to the entire community. The joy of voting is also about hope. Hope that voices will be heard, that fairness will prevail, and that positive change is possible. Even when outcomes are uncertain, the act of voting itself strengthens democracy. It reminds us that change begins with participation and that every single vote carries meaning. In the end, the joyful moment of voting is a celebration of freedom, responsibility, and belief in a better tomorrow. It is a reminder that democracy lives through the people, and every vote is a step toward the future we imagine together. ![1000027201.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbeb4t8AoMyoXVb3r49rZRXQyZByAYJPgP9RzLZ37WLPx/1000027201.jpg)

author: live.log
title: Get Ready to Groove! Barranquilla Crowned World's Most Creative City!
text:
Guess what, culture lovers and adventure seekers? We've got some *seriously* exciting news from the sun-drenched shores of Colombia! If you haven't heard, Barranquilla, the vibrant heart of the Colombian Caribbean, just snatched a HUGE title: **"World's Best Creative Destination"** by The Global Creative Destination Awards! Talk about a mic drop!

Now, you might be thinking, "Creative Destination? What does that even mean?" Well, my friends, in Barranquilla, creativity isn't just a buzzword; it's the very heartbeat of the city! It's in the rhythm of their music, the swirl of their dances, the colors of their art, and the boundless energy of their people. This city isn't just thinking outside the box; it's dancing around it, painting it neon, and inviting everyone to join the party!

[SOURCE](https://colombiaone.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/barranquilla-colombia-credit-jdvillalobos-CC-BY-SA-4.0-wikimedia-commons.jpg)

Think about it: where else can you find a carnival so epic, so wild, so absolutely unforgettable that UNESCO declared it a Masterpiece of the Oral and Intangible Heritage of Humanity? Yep, the Barranquilla Carnival is not just a party; it's a four-day explosion of artistic expression, storytelling, and pure, unadulterated joy that takes over the entire city. It’s a living, breathing testament to their creative spirit!

But it's not just about Carnival. Barranquilla is a constant hub of innovation. The city is buzzing with artists, musicians, writers, and dancers who are constantly pushing boundaries. They're building new, incredible public spaces, like the Gran Malecón, which isn't just a beautiful waterfront promenade; it’s a stage, an art gallery, a community hub – a place where new ideas bloom and old traditions find fresh expression.

This award isn't just a fancy plaque; it's a shout-out to Barranquilla's dedication to sustainable tourism, its warm hospitality, and its mouth-watering gastronomy (seriously, the food alone is a creative masterpiece!). It's a recognition of a city that isn't afraid to innovate, to celebrate its roots, and to welcome the world with open arms and a whole lot of sabor!

So, if you're looking for a destination that will spark your imagination, make your feet tap, and fill your soul with vibrant energy, Barranquilla should be at the very top of your list. Go experience the magic for yourself – you won't regret it!

---
Original article inspiration: [https://colombiaone.com/2026/02/13/barranquilla-world-best-creative-destination/](https://colombiaone.com/2026/02/13/barranquilla-world-best-creative-destination/)

author: maxinpower
title: Let's visit Oyama Shrine 尾山神社 👹🍣🎎 Wonderful Japan
text: ![IMG_4694.JPG](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/Eou9CMGBCWNa31a7eC1Wj3GryN9ohTKyws1M8ftExD3MdGdeuz1so2cV1D1jUGYudTD.JPG) There are many reasons to stay a little longer in Japan. One is the food, which I have already reported on many times here, and another is the country's impressive variety of landscapes. From expansive coastlines to majestic mountains to endless forests and vast rice fields, each season has its own unique charm. And last but not least, there are the numerous temples and shrines that can be found in every corner of the country. And it is precisely these spiritual places that I find particularly appealing. Even though I am not religious myself, I cannot escape the quiet, almost tangible atmosphere that surrounds you there. And it is probably this subtle, mystical aura that tempts me time and again on my travels and excursions to visit temples and shrines that I don't yet know. And often enough, I find myself returning a second time, and in some cases even again and again. So it came to pass that during our last visit to Kanazawa, I decided to plan our trip so that we could visit a shrine that I had already seen on a previous occasion. Kanazawa itself is a really interesting city and definitely worth a visit. Located on the west coast of Japan in Ishikawa Prefecture, it was once the seat of the powerful Maeda clan. Over the centuries, they developed the city into a cultural center that rivaled even Kyoto. Kanazawa was once the center of the surrounding Kaga region, which during the Edo period was one of the richest principalities in the country. Luck, coincidence, and cleverness guided the city through the changing times without major destruction, allowing it to survive even the Boshin War and World War II without significant damage. Today, you will encounter a rare blend of samurai culture, craftsmanship, and Japanese elegance. And there, in the city center, stands the Oyama Shrine, from where I brought back today's pictures. ![IMG_4690.JPG](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/23zbMzKi3WfWxuoeYkSHytGjsk2BAzEnqAHCgzyrLJsrbtmd7SR2p26ibqnGuEcttwEbV.JPG) Oyama Jinja (尾山神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the heart of Kanazawa, dedicated to the famous warlord Maeda Toshiie, the first daimyo of the Kaga clan. Originally built in 1599 on Mount Utatsuyama, the shrine was moved to its current location in 1873 and given its current name. Oyama Jinja is particularly famous for its unusual main gate, which was designed by a Dutch architect in 1875. It combines Japanese, Chinese, and European architectural elements in an unusual way, even featuring an odd third floor that is said to have once served as a lighthouse and is decorated with colorful Dutch-style stained glass windows. Directly on top of the gate is one of the oldest registered lightning rods in Japan, bringing the gate to a total height of 25 meters. The sight is truly unusual, and when you stand directly in front of the gate, at first glance you probably don't think that you are about to walk through the entrance of a Shinto shrine. Were it not for the first stone torii gate that you passed earlier, which can be seen in the first photo. ![IMG_4689.JPG](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/23ynUc1L7H1v6t1ufv237WmQdGbKj1ZTwRgT8sKdw5cHiQNDJCboPAeNcmfZ9CWXJdgoU.JPG) Seen from the inside, the gate doesn't really look Japanese, which is what makes it so special. It reminds me a little of Nagasaki, because this city in the very south of Japan also has many European and Chinese architectural elements that create a very similar atmosphere. In any case, the entrance gate is an eye-catcher that has already made it into countless photos. I took a few pictures myself and added them to my own ever-growing collection. ![IMG_4684.JPG](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/EqNF9Gb4BvgmNk4ZEpS2VcqyK8AXqs4YAZBRUjDF9K8ppDGiiW6daymLnLEtjvLcL4G.JPG) Here we are standing in front of the actual shrine building, which immediately brings us back to Japanese reality. Yes, this is exactly what the main buildings in most large Shinto shrines look like. Directly under the roof hangs a large sign bearing the name of the place (尾山神社), whereby it is noticeable that the characters are arranged from right to left. Traditionally, words and sentences in Japan can be written in various ways, including from top to bottom. ![IMG_6702.JPG](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/EoK6JzCMvNt5vZV8k7gM1ivKhgNLg1mdj7BvEKYAVn1JvCQ1Zu87SW9Vx12DRagKtPk.JPG) The shrine grounds also feature a statue of Maeda Toshiie and a well-maintained small walking garden with a pond, which was probably once part of a villa belonging to the Maeda family. It is therefore worth taking a closer look around here. ![IMG_4677.JPG](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/EoK5mqiqxCVbBYu9ejJiTTXZVChjYq3ncEPp645qXQyoLRvJGB1M2scMr9y3cvPrFZL.JPG) Then you would also discover this small gate, which has a much longer history than the shrine in which it stands today. While the famous main gate with its stained glass windows is often the focus of most visitors, the east gate of the shrine is a completely different story: it is an original gate from Kanazawa-Jo, the city's former castle complex, which was moved here to the shrine after the Meiji Restoration. It is an original and authentic remnant of the castle complex and a piece of feudal history that has been transferred to a religious context here in this shrine. And that concludes our brief tour of Oyama Shrine. Directly behind is the site of the former castle complex, where various gates and buildings have been rebuilt over the past few decades, and which is another highlight of a visit to Kanazawa. So it's best to continue on and walk through the Nezumita-mon to explore all there is to see there.... ![IMG_6703.JPG](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/Eqqvs7oRZGM5iHvwygTbz4jMFxztjEAyFttsXGaExV4dh1jnVYHny7erQg2fw2gLaKd.JPG) ![blog-ende.png](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/maxinpower/23tv6H18WKn98nBVkn2MwVhwWsuFRFgjBGwxLEMVhRZD7zRRbFokaNiAn39fUaB3TxVX6.png)

author: marius19
title: ✨ A Place Where Light Speaks – A Journey Through Sacred Architecture
text:
There are places where silence feels alive, where every beam of light carries a message, and where art and faith meet in perfect harmony. Today, I want to share with you a visual story captured inside and outside a remarkable church – a place where history, devotion, and artistic expression blend into one breathtaking experience.

📸 Photo 1 – The Language of Light

![20250716_135320.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZk6dWAYnNMfJwArQtvpxtQAsWZ9fvHt8M7gpMFyZKix4/20250716_135320.jpg)

The first image captures a magnificent stained-glass window rising toward a wooden vaulted ceiling. The geometric explosion of colors — deep blues, radiant reds, and luminous yellows — creates a dynamic composition that feels almost celestial.

The circular form at the center symbolizes eternity and divine unity. As sunlight passes through the glass, it transforms the interior into a living canvas. The wooden beams above add warmth and grounding, creating a beautiful contrast between earthly craftsmanship and heavenly illumination.

Standing there, I felt as if the light itself was telling a story — not in words, but in color and movement.

📸 Photo 2 – The Sacred Facade

![20250730_185518.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRpM4G4yYg5KZSS6o6hzbuvotZzQJAHrPXs8qcQ7WpzLo/20250730_185518.jpg)

The exterior reveals a classical architectural structure crowned with a detailed mosaic. At the top, a powerful religious depiction shows angels surrounding a central sacred figure, radiating golden light against a blue sky.

Below the mosaic, the entrance staircase invites visitors upward — symbolically ascending toward reflection and peace. The symmetry of the windows and the stone pillars create a balanced and dignified presence.

What caught my attention most was how the building harmonizes with the sky behind it. The cross above the roof stands tall, reminding us of faith’s enduring presence through time.

📸 Photo 3 – The Emblem of Identity

![20250730_185525.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQY7qTMd5W37sT1uQSA4FhRmi385Hj4C6gazvNYdg9VPx/20250730_185525.jpg)

The third image focuses on a decorative crest embedded in the facade. This ornamental relief carries historical and spiritual symbolism, likely representing the church’s patronage or local heritage.

Weathered by time, the stone tells its own story — subtle stains and aging marks reminding us that beauty deepens with history. Just beneath it, another carved element above the entrance adds layers of identity and legacy.

Details like these often go unnoticed, but they hold the soul of the building. They connect the present moment to generations past.

🌿 Final Reflections

Architecture is more than structure — it is memory, belief, and artistry carved into space. This church stands not only as a place of worship but as a testament to human creativity inspired by faith.

Through these photographs, I wanted to capture more than images — I wanted to capture atmosphere. The play of light, the silent strength of stone, and the quiet power of sacred symbolism.

Have you ever visited a place where light and architecture made you pause and simply breathe? I would love to hear your experiences in the comments.

Thank you for reading and sharing this journey with me. 🙏✨

author: zainalbakri
title: Where Gratitude Walks the Hills of Gombak
text:
![gambar.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcY8P9XrAxmUibMdxu6gUvXU1qwCyuFUB3g1PCnzCe32s/gambar.png)


![gambar.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYC4sxe9jDYmSd24s9b17PRqSVsRb32LnhkQ449XRaYNH/gambar.png)


![IMG_2321.JPG](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdnvqJ1k5aWV8vpzcVsjLb9R3GEdF4PuHfoMoynN7m4nf/IMG_2321.JPG)


The last time I set foot on the campus of International Islamic University Malaysia in Gombak, Kuala Lumpur, was in 2022. When I returned in February 2026, the air felt different—warmer, almost intimate, as though the trees and quiet walkways remembered me. This was not merely a university to me; it was the place where my eldest son had grown into himself, where he earned his bachelor’s degree beneath the soft Malaysian sky. Every corner seemed to whisper fragments of his journey—late nights of study, earnest prayers before exams, and dreams that stretched far beyond the hills of Gombak.

From this very campus, a door to Europe opened. A lecturer here entrusted him with an opportunity to study for a semester at University of Limoges in France—an experience that broadened not only his academic horizons but his understanding of the world. What humbles me most is that from his first day at university to his sojourn in France, the path was cleared by the generosity of Khazanah Foundation, a scholarship foundation owned by the Government of Malaysia. Tuition, travel, living expenses—every necessity was quietly and graciously borne.

Gratitude, I have learned, is sometimes too vast for words. How does a father measure thanks for a future made possible without the burden of debt? Standing once more on that campus, I felt a silent prayer rising within me—for the lecturers who believed in him, for the institution that nurtured him, and for the foundation that carried his dreams across continents. Some places do not merely educate; they transform lives. And in that transformation, a parent’s heart finds both pride and profound humility.
