author: steem-for-future
title: Our morning trip to buy quail birds
text:
![20260212_124850.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTv5bFJ59yUuytoFPkkvUeaKFvVRhDUqmFMPpDs65TqTB/20260212_124850.jpg) > Cover image Assalamu Alaikum. Greetings Sri Lankan community. How are you all? I hope by the grace of Allah, you are all doing well. Alhamdulillah, by the grace of Allah, I am also doing well. I pray to Allah that we are always doing well. I had a beautiful travel moment yesterday. Basically yesterday morning, my friend and I went on a bike ride and went to a farm to buy birds. I really liked going to the quail farm and I saw the quail birds. Basically, a small entrepreneur has started farming quail birds in his house. From there, he has been successful and I am very happy to see his success. Finally, we reached there yesterday morning. ![PSX_20260212_123805.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbPCaZq2jisQarQymuwVCTELFQuefzSFZwMtmjWo1XhoG/PSX_20260212_123805.jpg) I was sitting in the sun early in the morning writing a post. At that time, my friend Joynal called me and asked me to stay outside. Since Bangladesh is currently holding elections, riding bikes is usually banned. In the morning, he came to our house with a van and we reached my friend's house in the van. After reaching my friend's house, we rested for a while and then we took out the bike. After taking out the bike, we were riding the bike on the road and were careful. Because at any time the army or police force could attack us. So, being careful, we rode the bike forward. ![PSX_20260212_123843.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWkdCPse6RAg1gnnY9PztESEK9DgzyaN2Zk3JnXWLZVx7/PSX_20260212_123843.jpg) We were standing on a big bridge. It was very nice to stand here. Because there are a lot of fish ponds around. I stood on the bridge and rested for a while and got to know about the livelihood of the people around. Some tribes have come out on the road to collect their food and earn a living. I was very happy. ![PSX_20260212_123915.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRfUfWidFiyVL26FExr3Z37csfNrWR7GdErHUw3yztidh/PSX_20260212_123915.jpg) We must use the bridge to maintain the bond between the two villages. The morning sunlight fell on the bridge and the entire area was illuminated. I saw a fish farmer feeding his pond. It was so much fun that words cannot express it. Ponds were dug nearby to cultivate a large number of fish. Then we took the car and left for the farm. ![PSX_20260212_124000.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmb4GDoahi3tdUgiMyDphyd3VVaqPf7nQa14u7F9QgAehf/PSX_20260212_124000.jpg) ![PSX_20260212_124051.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTLeE9RhKNUykkLzAzdBL7EB7ukkiu5dkCqZq2P6SgQaA/PSX_20260212_124051.jpg) We had already reached the farm. After reaching the farm, we talked to the farmer. We initially decided to buy a total of five quail birds. Finally, we bought five quail birds for only 200 taka. Then we left for our home. After leaving for home, we needed feed for the quail birds. We went to a zero size feed shop and bought 500 grams of feed from there for 35 taka. Since we have to keep the quail birds, we need to get a container to keep their food. So we bought two containers for the quail birds. One container will keep food and the other will provide water. Anyway, we finally completed our activities and returned home. How did you like the beautiful morning sun and the wonderful story of buying quail birds? I hope you enjoyed this little travel story. Thank you very much for being with the Sri Lankan community. Thank you very much for listening to the story of becoming a small entrepreneur.
# `ᏒᎬᎶᎪᏒᎠs ` ## [Achievement 1](https://steemit.com/hive-172186/@steem-for-future/achievement1-or-or-or-bangladesh-or-or-or-introduction) ![21PRtjKRXPQybj4WUXScWv5QPLninWRxfbcWNsx7SenD7FrhkCa1FNh95pCuKfX2zFADCawA8xHb1weasemMPCdBKdwpZjwnxZgGuS1bfpFGdAzMmJ7B4T7gHvKaPnXPkdS7x6z4uy79QYvGDMeBES9.gif](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXbhkt6S45FJ2EBoPRLrVYsoUMTnWHQ9UeUqEcvhxzYde/21PRtjKRXPQybj4WUXScWv5QPLninWRxfbcWNsx7SenD7FrhkCa1FNh95pCuKfX2zFADCawA8xHb1weasemMPCdBKdwpZjwnxZgGuS1bfpFGdAzMmJ7B4T7gHvKaPnXPkdS7x6z4uy79QYvGDMeBES9.gif) @STEEM-FOR-FUTURE

author: kiwiscanfly
title: Day 🔟Gold Coast Australia
text:
Today has been my last day of work here in Queensland Australia, it was short day at work but still a good one. I think i have said before this would have to be my fzvorite worksite to visit ue to the people that work there, so friendly and lots of Kiwi's work there as well. Plus the Gold Coast is my favorite spot to visit in Australia so it really is a holiday first with some work chucked in. Spent my last day lying ion the sun loungers by the pool before i had a lovely swim to cool off in the pool. ![20260204_122736.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmX1sGiQgR2EnCjA3yt3XGgQ8MRnmhTyPABaFEnMW1YLdo/20260204_122736.jpg) I have also done all of my laundry and folded it nicely, its good t be able to go home with clothes clean so i can just put them away when i get home.
![20260211_101600.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmW7YbqdWFXvxNfiwGR6e68jFXCHRUsAxjK2QqsACHi6Wq/20260211_101600.jpg)
![20260211_122417.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSXVeqf7hCYajcGjPZkXAicRakLn5a2Ypp5JQ6oc4wrdF/20260211_122417.jpg)
Today is also the last day i get to grab anything the Family like, all of this stuff is not available in New Zealand so why not.
![20260211_132808.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmejEAz1zhFn1HqFDXKsZEiFsNXvMRYWGfJ5PjDM5tT19n/20260211_132808.jpg)
![20260211_132831.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZuWJgEPyWLoSzLW1QjsuQ9EBAQFtgwuVBevcBagnR2ae/20260211_132831.jpg)
Everyone loves the Pods and these were on special for $5 AUS, actually i did see these again in NZ but they were $10 a bag! ![20260211_132755.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPdmXVcb5nREe2xi3xfXPBUG7rVeG7ApaRiiGYVM6Afh9/20260211_132755.jpg) When i was packing for this trip i packed very very lightly as i wouldn't need any warm clothing at all, plus having a washing machine in my room meant i could wash my clothes daily if i chose too. My plan also included being able to fit all of my clothes/laptop's etc into my backpack, this would leave my small suitcase just for stuff i brought. ![20260211_133018.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcqAeEP6eueqvFQgL36jcPrgTVNRwcmDcurT3dPvP57w3/20260211_133018.jpg) I am pretty much all packed and ready to go and have brought everything i wanted, now i just chill out and wait until tomorrow morning when i drive an hour's north to Brisbane Airport - then its back to Wellington. This Kiwi hopes you have enjoyed seeing a bit of the Gold Coast while i have been here. *all pics are mine* *smudge approves this post* # I am @kiwiscanfly 🥝🐔 ![20260204_124302.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQsqq18aKKHeZPWAG8WKBt88saFDWnkHks97SexcBvXiK/20260204_124302.jpg)

author: ssarrow
title: August Light at the Suzhou Museum
text:
In August, the Suzhou Museum appears especially luminous under the height of summer sun. White walls and gray tiles outline crisp architectural lines, while water courtyards mirror the sky and sweeping eaves. Sunlight filters through glass corridors, casting bright geometric shadows across the floor. Inside, the air feels calm and cool, and space unfolds with quiet restraint. Stepping outside and looking back, one realizes that even in midsummer, elegance can remain composed and refined.

author: live.log
title: Solo Travel is SO In: Why Everyone's Hitting the Road Alone!
text:
Ever dreamt of packing your bags and just... going? Anywhere? By yourself? Well, guess what – you're not alone in wanting to go solo! The travel world is absolutely buzzing, and it turns out, more and more people are ditching their travel buddies for the ultimate 'me time' adventure. We're talking about a massive 36% jump in solo travel by 2026! So, if you've been thinking about it, now's definitely the time to explore the world on your own terms.

### Who's Flying Solo & Why Are They Loving It?

Who's leading this awesome charge? Mostly the cool kids (Millennials and Gen Z, obvs!), but honestly, everyone from Gen X to Boomers is catching the solo bug. And ladies, we're totally owning this space! Why the sudden urge to hit the road alone? Imagine total freedom! No arguments over where to eat, no waiting for someone to get ready, no compromising on what to see. Just *your* schedule, *your* whims, *your* adventure. It’s all about finding yourself, pushing past comfort zones, meeting awesome new people, and seriously de-stressing. It's like therapy, but with way better views!

### Where Are These Brave Adventurers Heading?

So, where are these fearless explorers setting their sights? Japan is a massive hit, probably thanks to its incredible culture, delicious food, and super-safe environment. Europe classics like Paris, Rome, and London are always calling, offering endless charm and history. For a dose of wild nature and adventure, Costa Rica is a winner. And right here in the USA, NYC and Los Angeles are still sparkling bright for solo explorers looking for big-city thrills.

### Budget Hacks for Your Solo Journey (Because Freedom Shouldn't Break the Bank!)

Now, let's talk moolah. Solo travel *can* sometimes feel a bit pricier (hello, 'single supplement' struggle!). But don't you fret, savvy traveler! There are tons of ways to make your adventure affordable:

*   **Public transport is your new bestie:** Skip the expensive taxis and embrace local buses or trains.
*   **Off-peak is your peak:** Travel when everyone else isn't, and you'll find cheaper flights and stays.
*   **Hostels & guesthouses:** Meet cool people, soak up the vibe, and save serious cash.
*   **Eat like a local:** Street food, local markets, and grocery store picnics are delicious *and* cheap.
*   **Embrace free activities:** Parks, free museum days, and self-guided walking tours are your friends!

### Safety First (Always!)

Okay, real talk – safety is absolutely key when you're traveling solo. A little common sense goes a long, long way:

*   **Tell a pal your plan:** A quick text with your itinerary keeps someone in the loop.
*   **Stay alert:** Keep your head up, not just buried in your phone. Be aware of your surroundings.
*   **Trust your gut:** If a situation or person feels off, it probably is. Just walk away!
*   **Pack smart & light:** Less to worry about and easier to manage.
*   **Learn a few local words:** A simple 'hello' or 'thank you' can go a long way in connecting with locals.
*   **Travel insurance is your friend:** Just in case something unexpected happens.
*   **Reputable spots only:** Stick to well-reviewed transport and accommodations.
*   **Group up for some fun:** Consider joining a day tour or activity to meet others and see sights safely.

So, what are you waiting for? That solo adventure you've been dreaming of is totally within reach. It's time to embrace the freedom, the growth, and the sheer joy of exploring the world on your own terms. Go on, unleash your inner wanderer!

[https://www.yahoo.com/lifestyle/articles/solo-travel-surging-2026-budget-171933759.html](https://www.yahoo.com/lifestyle/articles/solo-travel-surging-2026-budget-171933759.html)

author: ssarrow
title: Wenfeng · A Gathering in Silken Lights
text:
Built beside the water, unfolding along the riverbanks,
it embraces a pool of shimmering jade-green waves.
In the misty drizzle, one pours a pot of Longjing in the marketplace,
while distant melodies of flutes and song drift softly through the air.

Taste a bite of street delicacies,
lift your eyes to the sunset over Wenfeng,
and in the vibrant glow of neon-lit streets and fluttering garments,
find yourself both detached from the world and immersed within it.

author: rayhan111
title: A Quiet Story of Everyday Life
text:
|
Assalamu Alaikum / Adabah🤝
| |-|
|
Hello friends, how are you all? Hope everybody is healthy. I am also very good in your prayers. I am rayhan111, from Rajshahi division of Bangladesh.
| |-|
![1000027176.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUwXAcoqrspAQxBBHG4AVyYTWeYtRHehtpKYfTprcKJSv/1000027176.jpg) These images capture a silent yet powerful narrative of everyday life unfolding on a familiar roadside. At first glance, it seems like an ordinary urban corner unfinished grounds, scattered dust, electric wires stretching across the sky, and residential buildings standing close to one another. But within this simplicity lies a deeper story of resilience, routine, and hope. The open space beside the road reflects a pause between development and daily survival. A lone man walking calmly across the dusty ground reminds us of countless people who move through life without noise or attention, carrying responsibilities that remain unseen. His slow steps echo determination, shaped by years of experience and struggle. Life here does not rush; it flows steadily, guided by necessity rather than luxury.Nearby, a rickshaw stands quietly, symbolizing hard-earned livelihood. For many, it is not just a vehicle but a source of dignity and survival. The presence of animals roaming freely adds a rural touch to the urban setting, blending village life with city expansion. This coexistence highlights how tradition and modernity walk side by side, neither fully replacing the other. ![1000027178.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSmNUg8pmxUmoxDJ39QcfCbtEzE282HXCPpRH8tG2JFh3/1000027178.jpg) The buildings in the background tell another story. Painted walls, balconies, and windows suggest growing neighborhoods and changing aspirations. Yet the tangled electric wires overhead reveal the complexity of progress development that often grows faster than planning. Posters and signboards on the roadside speak of local businesses, healthcare, and community services, reflecting how people rely on one another in close-knit surroundings.Scattered litter and uneven ground quietly point to challenges lack of infrastructure, neglect, and the need for collective responsibility. Still, the scene does not feel hopeless. Instead, it feels honest. It shows life as it truly is for many: imperfect, hardworking, and full of endurance. These photographs remind us that beauty does not always lie in grand landmarks or polished streets. Sometimes, it exists in ordinary moments in a man walking home, a rickshaw waiting for its next passenger, or a neighborhood breathing through dust and sunlight. This is the story of everyday people, living between dust and dreams, shaping their future one step at a time. ![1000027177.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmb7k73d6c4bw55oZNyXhknKsh92vNwirM7kMRjQkZ1hTe/1000027177.jpg)

[Location](https://w3w.co/arise.pipeline.handed)

text:

His Troubled Hedgehog found a block of cheese that they would sometimes put down their trousers. This is certainly a somewhat surprising operation, to me and my children, but not to the Hedgehog, who expected that this idea was entertaining. Honestly, a block of cheese was the item that was opted for.

His Troubled Hedgehog desperately desired a can of coke to put on his head. One might say this is a fairly weird undertaking, to my grandpa, but not to the Hedgehog, who had decided it would be miraculous. Who would have thought, a can of coke would be the item that was chosen.

His Troubled Hedgehog craved for a stretchman that they would occasionally throw. One might consider this to be a peculiar suggestion, to me and my parents, but not to the Hedgehog, who considered it would be fun. Remarkably, a stretchman was the item that was selected.

His Troubled Hedgehog wanted a porno magazine that they liked to sit and look at. It would have been a weird and wonderful idea, to some, but not to the Hedgehog, who had decided it was simply life. A porno magazine was the chosen thing.

His Troubled Hedgehog wanted a helmet that they would occasionally rub on his belly. This is certainly a fairly curious proposal, to most, but not to the Hedgehog, who expected that the idea was breathtaking. You wouldnt have thought, a helmet was the thing that was selected.

His Troubled Hedgehog searched for a radiator that they would break. One might find this to be an exciting exercise, to me and my parents, but not to the Hedgehog, who expected that this idea was exciting. Bizarrely, a radiator was the thing that was opted for.

author: evildeathcore
title: Shishkino
text:
The village of Shishkino is located in the Sokolsky district of the Vologda region on the banks of the Sukhona River. There are few houses in the village, as well as residents. The distance to Sokol is 13 km.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmajsXDSCiSbSbpxBB7cKqFpkwZjZ9HShtXUDy79Rj6buT/image.png)

I was attracted by a bridge over a stream flowing into the Sukhona, and old houses.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmYRSyLD5URsdk6XvXU1rs5GrmRPFxBCCE2129VMAsTss4/image.png)

After heavy snowfalls and snow accumulations on various surfaces, I began to constantly look for places to reflect the effects of the snowfall.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQMJd5977qMRVXLyspMJBiASaiYtSn9UwGHd6eL7XWxMu/image.png)

In simple words: I'm looking for a village with a height difference, a bridge, old houses, and it's all covered in snow.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdEDDcJggVBdntWaCgSgFVQyiEUQXxwVT4oxUxRosXKy1/image.png)

Sometimes it is possible to find something interesting, but very often unsuccessfully.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZ8u9GfXH4gTvuuJAiNTfpH2H3CdM95kZFeatyBQ7i1mk/image.png)

I am faced with the problem of remote villages – no, not the road and accessibility ...It's light!

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdkNPx6PrWDZHh9EaZG9tbfSTeGWfRuJrnMcMvQm7E4fS/image.png)

You arrive in a village, and there is only one lamp for the entire village...

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXdCWN9qrkDu7sG9WurvoCZ27eujEmPvvs9NWrAAbt6V6/image.png)

It's good if there's any lighting at all. It's just dark in places.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUrPxJ36oWs2E7SEUzoCSvnw6eHkPTs7UhnwUyWRsdWWM/image.png)

Once upon a time, the lack of light sources had a good effect on the picture, but to emphasize the texture and volume of snow, you need at least some kind of lighting.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSxR4UEAyo9Z9gi92RvmQsBoHAvVu2P42HSa8x6ZqCcQq/image.png)

Oh, and one more problem – solid metal fences.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmbt35swPvaoEEsCoLw8MUvMcc1KWTyx9UAGmKwRFDXK57/image.png)

But this is already a problem for our generation as a whole.

author: damithudaya
title: OWeekly CRICKET Review - CXVii | The T20 World Cup
text:
Hello, fellow Cricket Enthusiasts, You already know I share a weekly wrap-up of what's happening in the Cricket community. And it's the 117th edition now. The World of Cricket is very busy right now, with the T20I World Cup. Even after a few matches, the tournament has become so exciting and entertaining. So, let's see what's going on. ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWm7V2ZDBFYqyTHXjo3YRH6gQtrAo5Mi1Fi9xif4p5Qgt/image.png) [Leonardo AI](https://app.leonardo.ai/image-generation) ![asvcx1.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUicZZFrBwpSAekxGWTVteijTw67FCbm2mwQTjui2V6Vs/asvcx1.png * This is the 10th edition of the tournament, which began in 2007 in South Africa. India became the first champion, defeating Pakistan, and they are the defending champions as well. It will be played from 7 February to 8 March 2026, in India and Sri Lanka, with 20 teams. You can see the full details from [here](https://steemit.com/steem-sri-lanka/@damithudaya/icc-men-s-t20-world-cup-2026-drama-and-drama). -------------------- * Out of 55 matches, 25 matches have already been finished, and it was just awesome. Here you can see the points table up to now; all the teams have played two or more matches.
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTjaDveuQjGCBum6DdkxonJVSedJjEFTEMZLU3RBuFytx/image.png)
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXQca9yMUmhLvDYutZh1xNaAvj9y3ZHifv93kjfSHq7qt/image.png)
-----------
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTQRFHKNKwdGeTAHBMo89cSetm76KSDbDBFFYntnj5t9d/image.png)
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdonjsiY49Lxyyd42nTZj8seC9jkTtbc4CZ5TMZY5rEud/image.png)
----------- * All the groups have been pretty competitive, as associate teams are giving a huge fight, and the competition has become wide open. Right now, only the West Indies and South Africa have qualified for the next round, with three straight wins. Nepal and Oman have already been eliminated from the tournament. --------- * We saw very close matches from the start, like the USA challenging India, the Netherlands almost kicking out Pakistan, Nepal almost winning against England, etc. But Zimbabwe is winning against the Australians, and the double-super over match between South Africa and Afghanistan will be the best highlight so far. * Earlier I talked about [South Africa vs Afghanistan: A Magical Show!](https://steemit.com/steem-sri-lanka/@damithudaya/icc-men-s-t20-world-cup-2026-south-africa-vs-afghanistan-a-magical-show). The match got tied, the super over tied, and South Africa won the second super over in the ball. The match was spinning between the two teams until the very end, quality cricket from two quality teams. * The 19th match between Australia and Zimbabwe gave the biggest surprise. Anyway, it's not unheard of, as Zimbabwe did the same thing in the only other time they met, in 2007. The match was held in R.Premadasa Stadium, Colombo. Zimbabwe batted first and batted sensibly. From the start, they batted maturely, scoring runs without losing many wickets. Brian Bennett and Marumany scored 47/0 in the powerplay, with 61 runs partnership for the first wicket. Bennet scored an unbeaten fifty as well. With finishing touches from Burl and Raza, they managed 169/2 in their 20 overs. Australia really struggled with the unavailability of their pace trios. ----------- Australian battting line up, we were on the back foot from the start, and they lost their wicket in the second over. Inside 5 overs, they lost 4 wickets for just 29 runs, including two ducks. Matt Renshaw and Maxwell rebuild the inning, infact they needed 67 runs in the last 6 overs with 6 wickets. But once the partnership is broken, the next few wickets also followed simultanuously. Australia only managed 146/10 with a 23-run loss. Blessing Muzarabani was the hero, with 4 wickets for 17 runs. Brad Evans also took 3 for 23. 4/17 by Muzarabani is the best bowling figures for Zimbabwe in a T20 WC match. A famous and memorable day in Zimbabwe cricket. #### Most Runs -------------- ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWg3vnzs3cPFh8Cn1KjUHdKw7uamNMrJbBLexCFytDeTr/image.png) [stats](https://www.cricbuzz.com/cricket-series/11253/icc-mens-t20-world-cup-2026/stats) * Tim Seifert from New Zealand is leading the list along with Markam, Lorcan Tucker from Ireland, Shubham Ranjana from the USA, and Shimron Hetmyer. There are no hundreds so far, and 94 runs from Tucker against OMAN is the highest score. #### Most Wickets -------------- ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmc1pXVFrf2dvEY94PBiZb599gpBLXhW7kEizbKPPfkmF7/image.png) [stats](https://www.cricbuzz.com/cricket-series/11253/icc-mens-t20-world-cup-2026/stats) * Shadley van Schalkwyk from the USA leads the chart with Lungi Ngidi, Blessing Muzarabani, Harmeet Singh from the USA, and Marco Jansen. So, that's it for today!! Until next time! Enjoy! Thank you. Best Regards!

author: frafiomatale
title: Don't call it shame, just call it Inter
text:
![HBJm1v-bQAA7l4D.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmd1YrcDJtPrxx5RxYtjBw6QgFmi9WeWq1PNc79dS9w4Ay/HBJm1v-bQAA7l4D.jpg) [Image source](https://x.com/MemoriesMilan/status/2022792263332507923/photo/1) #### #### _This is the English version of the post [Non chiamatela vergogna, chiamatela semplicemente "Inter"](https://steemit.com/italy/@frafiomatale/non-chiamatela-vergogna-chiamatela-semplicemente-inter), originally published in Italian in the ITALY community._ ####
![#LIFE.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPuAbxsvCJFEcTAJr6BMtCoRdaNC5fsERb5nq4JhMXjRT/#LIFE.png)
I find it seriously difficult to contain my words, which almost seem to want to fly off the keyboard I am using to write this piece, commenting on what I saw at San Siro last night, in the match between **Inter** and **Juventus**. I would like to describe the behaviour of **bastoni** (deliberately written in lower case) and the refereeing of **La Penna** as “shameful”, but shame is a feeling that has its own dignity, even noble dignity, when felt sincerely as a result of one's own mistakes. Infinitely more noble, and therefore unknown, to the individuals mentioned. Here we are beyond simple shame. What we witnessed yesterday is probably the biggest refereeing scandal ever seen in a century of football: it is intentional malfeasance, a sick, obsessive, embarrassing and disgusting way of living and thinking about football. It is an inferiority complex that has been brewing for a century and has turned into pure hatred, so strong that no means are spared in order to overwhelm the enemy. A “war”, no longer a sport. This is Italian football today, encapsulated in last night's **Inter-Juve** match, in the horrendous simulation by the Inter defender and in the referee's haste to punish the team hated by the puppet masters. A match between a team that has been targeted beyond imagination and dignity on and off the pitch for twenty years now, but which the media and “popular sentiment” continue absurdly to describe as “thieving”, and another that has turned football into “its own thing”, continuing to flaunt a supposed moral superiority despite being constantly contradicted by the facts. In Italy, football has now become “unrefereable”. The close ties between **Inter** managers and players and organised crime were officially dismissed as “childish behaviour” (resulting in a ridiculous one-match ban), and these are the results: who would dare to whistle at their “friends's friends”, knowing that they would be putting at least their career, if not their life, at risk? The feeling, ever since the strange appointment of **La Penna** (18 wins and two draws with him for the Nerazzurri, between the pitch and VAR), was that **Inter** had to win last night, one way or another. And so it went. A victory that was directed, distorted, and constructed on paper, just like the championship they boast about the most and which is referred to in those parts as the “most beautiful”. The joy of having deceived the referee, the wild celebrations at the end of the match, and **Chivu**'s statements speak volumes about how **Inter** understands football. They would have acted in the same way even if a goal had been scored with their hands, if they had been caught with 13 players on the pitch, or if at every corner kick the ball had ended up in the opponent's goal after punching the goalkeeper in the face. That is, pretending nothing had happened. Pretending nothing happened, as in their dealings with the 'Ndrangheta, as with the financial default, as with Facchetti's phone calls, as with the **Palazzi** report, as with twenty cross-capital gains with Milan, as with **Acerbi**'s racist insults or **Lautaro**'s profanity. The important thing, as in war, is to defeat the enemy by any means necessary. But this is no longer football, it is no longer shameful, it is simply **Inter**. ####
## I'm part of ITALYGAME Team **together with: @girolamomarotta, @sardrt, @mikitaly, @mad-runner, @famigliacurione**

👉 VOTA PER NOI COME WITNESS👈 ![Progetto senza titolo (10).jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmaaNuF8gaoBCnFXZ2atqX8RbaceziqKXatioiUEQZHMMm/Progetto%20senza%20titolo%20(10).jpg)

author: jahangeerkhanday
title: When Rivalry Becomes One-Sided: India vs Pakistan in Modern Cricket
text: If there are any rivalries in the sports world, they are the ones that go beyond the field. In football, we have Barcelona versus Real Madrid, Brazil versus Argentina, and Manchester United versus Liverpool. In cricket, we also have great rivalries like Australia versus England, but the biggest of them all is India versus Pakistan on the cricket field. ![IMG_7612.jpeg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRhmv1LH72VChXZhBFoWUusqFegC6CXXJkDUE5q6ZWuk3/IMG_7612.jpeg) It is one of the greatest rivalries ever in the sports world, involving the emotions of more than 150 crore people across the two nations, along with millions of viewers from different countries around the world. It is always one of the most awaited matches in every tournament. There is no doubt that ticket prices go very high for this game. The ICC, which is the controlling body of cricket worldwide, recognized this opportunity long ago and has been arranging matches between these two nations in almost every tournament in which both teams participate, making it one of the biggest revenue-generating events in cricket. ![IMG_7613.jpeg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSdXrjg1eVPRGaGxKCYVHKMRSBobvBMEeU3omHAeemaw4/IMG_7613.jpeg) Yesterday, we had yet another match between India and Pakistan. People had been waiting for it right from the start of the tournament, and even before that. No doubt, India is a much stronger team compared to Pakistan. India has created a huge infrastructure for cricket, far greater in comparison to Pakistan. The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has become the richest cricket board in the world. It puts in a lot of effort, infrastructure, and investment to groom cricketers. At this stage, there is hardly any comparison with Indian cricket. For me, the hype around this game now feels somewhat exaggerated. Pakistan no doubt plays good cricket, but the team is quite mercurial and can fail in the most unexpected situations. Yesterday’s game was yet another example of this. I feel that this match should not be regarded as highly as it once was, because Pakistan has not been able to compete consistently with India in recent times. On the world stage, especially in the World Cup, India has dominated Pakistan with an 8–1 record so far. For that reason, the rivalry now seems somewhat one-sided, and the excitement has gone down considerably. ![IMG_7614.jpeg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfV8wVDXVju53mxaATgVnkAJ6ZgY3gkxLgGioc56Xducz/IMG_7614.jpeg) Whenever there is such a match, India always seems to have the upper hand because of its high standard of batting and bowling. Again and again, India has shown that no matter what the circumstances are, it is a resilient team that can recover from any situation and remain competitive. I hope we get to see good cricket in the coming World Cup matches. For me, India is one of the favourites—indeed, the number one favourite—especially with the advantage of home conditions. It is high time that India puts in its best effort to bring home yet another laurel and achieve another great feat. Thank you for going through my post. Have a great time ahead.

author: welshstacker
title: Still life in this old dog yet...
text:
<center>![Screenshot_20260214_221036_Instagram.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmW3XhiwRS2FDgtiR2QNRr16MMgth4SgzdnJk3xSmekcoq/Screenshot_20260214_221036_Instagram.jpg)</center>


While most of you, i assume, were spending today celebrating Valentines day with your loved ones, i was doing something else i love.

With the 6 nations rugby tournament underway, and Wales due to play France tomorrow, my local village side hosted a touring "veterans" rugby team, for those of us 45yrs and older.

Was a great day playing with some of my old team mates, some i haven't seen or played with innover 15 years. With me being 45, i was onenof the younger men playing today. So you can imagine the state we were all in by the end...

We managed to pull off a 25 - 12 victory, and as i left the rugby club to head home, the French tourist were well into the drinking games. It looked to be heading to be a great night.

author: frafiomatale
title: The popcorn are ready
text:
![lucid-origin_A_man_in_his_thirties_with_a_short_black_beard_and_a_cowboy_hat_on_his_head_laug-0.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVoXvEYSfh7f3wAcxdWgH9dA6wx2hcMLZiN2WBWppKMSL/lucid-origin_A_man_in_his_thirties_with_a_short_black_beard_and_a_cowboy_hat_on_his_head_laug-0.jpg) [Immagine creata con Leonardo.ai](https://app.leonardo.ai/image-generation) #### #### _This is the English version of the post [I popcorn sono già pronti](https://steemit.com/italy/@frafiomatale/i-popcorn-sono-gia-promti), originally published in Italian in the ITALY community._
![#LIFE.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPuAbxsvCJFEcTAJr6BMtCoRdaNC5fsERb5nq4JhMXjRT/#LIFE.png)
Have you prepared the popcorn? Two or three events that could soon occur on the international stage — and I’m talking about different fields, from geopolitics to sports — promise to be truly “tasty” and unmissable, especially for the reactions and the hasty bandwagon jumping that will inevitably follow. However, for those who don’t feel like waiting the months that separate us from those events, the present already seems more than enough to entertain us, especially if we focus on the fantastic world of the “Gravina Circus,” the theater of the absurd into which the entire Italian football system has turned. A world in which, for incidents occurring outside the stadium, certain groups of supporters rightly experience the “iron fist,” paying with away-match bans until the end of the season for the misconduct of troublemakers, yet where, if you belong to the “state faction” and throw a firecracker at the head of an opposing team’s player, you get away with little more than a slap on the wrist. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6-KFyyOjHVo ####
In the “Gravina Circus,” if, as a victim, you report organized crime interference in your supporters’ section, you are suspended for twelve months (with an initial request of thirty), but if you wear the right-colored tuxedo and maintain regular relations with said “gentlemen,” even facilitating their shady dealings, you get away with a single match ban, to be served in a meaningless home game. In the masterpiece by **George Orwell**, _Animal Farm_, the phrase “The law is equal for all” is at one point interpreted with a sensational addition: “but some are more equal than others.” And here one recalls the words of good old **Giraudo**, the day after the Calciopoli verdict: “You’ll see what kind of bandits will come after us...” At the circus, by definition, you certainly cannot expect reality. Animals are forced to perform movements that, if they lived in nature, they would never even dream of doing; clowns dress in ways they never would in real life; and even the presenters speak in an affected voice completely different from the one they use when ordering coffee at the bar in the morning. #### [WATCH HERE THE DOUBLE INTERPRETATION BY MARELLI (DAZN) ON IDENTICAL INCIDENTS INVOLVING INTER AND JUVENTUS](https://x.com/ladolcejuve/status/2021845864415719568) #### And in the one directed by the president of the FIGC, things run almost the same way, like a gigantic, millionaire show of the absurd. Listen to this: if a player shoots and scores from outside the box while a teammate stands beyond the defense (offside) and in the ball’s path, without touching it, the goal is allowed, provided the offside player is at a reasonable distance from the goalkeeper (not interfering, to be clear). This is the rule. But listen to this as well: if a player shoots and scores from outside the box while a teammate stands beyond the defense (offside) and in the ball’s path, without touching it, the goal must be disallowed because he interferes with the goalkeeper, even if the offside player is at a reasonable distance from the goalkeeper (not interfering, to be clear). And no, this is not a joke, nor another of Orwell’s dramatic hyperboles, but what happens every Sunday in refereeing interpretations, now no longer tied to the rulebook but exclusively to the color of the jersey. A kind of wrestling, where the “villain,” often in agreement with the referee or with those running the whole show, wins by committing every possible foul. And the “good guy” (or if you prefer, “the fool”) must resign himself to losing, even when he doesn’t deserve it, because that’s the script. [WATCH HERE IDENTICAL HANDBALLS INTERPRETED IN TWO OPPOSITE WAYS](https://x.com/EpicureoUgo85/status/2020899779371520441) The big difference, however, is that in wrestling everything is openly staged for the audience’s entertainment, even through moments designed to make them angry, whereas football, in theory, should be a sport where the best team wins. Want another one? If a player deliberately kicks the ball against a defender’s arm inside the box, it is a handball and a penalty. But if the exact same thing happens three days later, the ball is deemed unexpected and can never be a penalty. Ladies and gentlemen, you can watch this mess yourselves — a system that, in order to protect the state-backed team and sink its rival, is driving Italian football deeper and deeper into the abyss. Much better other sports and, speaking of which, applause to the great **Francesca Lollobrigida**, two gold medals as a mother, complete with a final sign of the Cross. Now, let’s get back to the popcorn... https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1TYXbOFZdy8 ####
## I'm part of ITALYGAME Team **together with: @girolamomarotta, @sardrt, @mikitaly, @mad-runner, @famigliacurione**

👉 VOTA PER NOI COME WITNESS👈 ![Progetto senza titolo (10).jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmaaNuF8gaoBCnFXZ2atqX8RbaceziqKXatioiUEQZHMMm/Progetto%20senza%20titolo%20(10).jpg)

author: vik24
title: Plot Twist! Pep's Sweating Over Haaland Before the Arsenal Showdown!
text:
Alright, football fanatics, gather 'round! The Premier League title race is heating up faster than a forgotten hot dog on the grill, and guess what? We've got ourselves a major plot twist brewing in Manchester!

Imagine this: The biggest game of the season is just around the corner, Man City vs. Arsenal, a true winner-takes-all (or at least, winner-takes-a-massive-advantage) kind of match. The tension is thicker than a triple-chocolate milkshake, and then, *BAM!* Enter Pep Guardiola, looking a little… well, *sweaty*.

No, he hasn't just finished a marathon! Our beloved City boss has reportedly been "sweating" over the fitness of none other than goal-machine Erling Haaland. Yes, you read that right. The man who scores goals for fun, the Viking cyborg, the human cheat code – he's apparently been battling some muscle problems and playing with a bit of discomfort.

[SOURCE](https://assets-cms.goal.com/images/v2/blt56f707f15c7e8499/haaland-celebrates-man-city-v-burnley-fa-cup-2022-23.jpg?auto=webp&format=pjpg&width=3840&quality=60)

Now, for Arsenal fans, this news might feel like finding an extra tenner in your old jeans – a small, unexpected boost! Haaland, with his uncanny ability to find the back of the net from seemingly anywhere, is *the* guy who keeps opposition defenders up at night. The thought of him not being at 100%, or even worse, missing the clash, is like music to Mikel Arteta's ears (and probably a collective sigh of relief from the Arsenal backline).

Pep admitted Haaland has been having "problems" recently. While he didn't exactly rule him out, the mere mention of it has sent shockwaves through the football world. Could this be the slight crack in City's armour that Arsenal needs to finally leapfrog them?

Of course, City's squad is packed with superstars, and they're hardly a one-man team. But let's be real, taking Haaland out of the equation is like taking the main ingredient out of a perfect recipe. It just changes *everything*.

So, as we count down to the colossal clash, keep your eyes peeled for any updates on Haaland. This little fitness drama has just added a whole new layer of spice to what was already a mouth-watering encounter. Will Pep's sweat turn into tears, or will Haaland miraculously find his stride? Only time will tell, but one thing's for sure: the title race just got even more unpredictable!

---
Inspired by: [Little boost for Arsenal? Pep Guardiola reveals Man City sweating over Erling Haaland fitness](https://www.goal.com/en-us/lists/little-boost-arsenal-pep-guardiola-reveals-man-city-sweating-erling-haaland-fitness/blt590b541edc5ef460)

author: vik24
title: Get Ready for the Future: It's Going to Be Quantum and Nano-tastic!
text:
Ever feel like the future is just around the corner, waiting for us to catch up? Well, buckle up, buttercups, because the National Science Foundation (NSF) is hitting the accelerator, and they're doing it with some seriously tiny tech! We're talking about a future powered by quantum leaps and nanotechnology, and trust me, it’s way cooler than it sounds.

Think of it like this: Imagine having LEGOs so small, you can build with individual atoms. Or unlocking secret superpowers hidden within the very fabric of reality. That’s kind of what quantum and nanotechnology are all about! They're not just buzzwords; they’re the keys to unlocking breakthroughs that will change everything from how we get our energy to how we treat diseases.

[SOURCE](https://www.lawbc.com/wp-content/uploads/iStock-2206773425.jpg)

So, what's the big news? The NSF is creating something called the National Quantum and Nanotechnology Research Infrastructure (NQNRI). Don't let the mouthful of a name scare you! Basically, it's like building the ultimate national playground for super-smart scientists and innovators across the country. We’re talking about a network of top-tier facilities, packed with the most advanced tools and tech, all designed to help our brightest minds push the boundaries of what’s possible.

Why now? Because these fields are exploding! We're on the cusp of some truly mind-blowing discoveries, and the NSF wants to make sure America is leading the charge. This new infrastructure isn't just about cool gadgets; it's about making sure our scientists have open access to the best equipment, fostering teamwork across different fields, and making sure we're training the next generation of genius thinkers.

What could this mean for *you*? Picture this: cleaner, more efficient energy that powers our homes without a fuss. New medical treatments that can target diseases with unbelievable precision. Materials that are stronger, lighter, and smarter than anything we have today. And yes, probably even cooler tech for your phone (you're welcome!). This initiative is all about boosting our national competitiveness, creating high-tech jobs, and solving some of the world's toughest problems.

Big universities and research hubs are already circling their calendars, because the NSF is making a huge investment – up to $100 million over five years for each lucky awardee! They’re looking for bold ideas and brilliant teams to make this vision a reality, with initial proposals due in the summer of 2026.

So, while the scientists are busy building the future, we can all get excited about the quantum-powered, nano-infused world that’s heading our way. It's going to be smaller, smarter, and seriously spectacular!

---
*Inspired by: [https://www.lawbc.com/nsf-will-establish-national-quantum-and-nanotechnology-research-infrastructure-letters-of-intent-are-due-march-16-2026/](https://www.lawbc.com/nsf-will-establish-national-quantum-and-nanotechnology-research-infrastructure-letters-of-intent-are-due-march-16-2026/)*

author: damithudaya
title: ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 - South Africa vs Afghanistan : A Magical Show!
text: Hello, fellow Sports Enthusiasts, Today, we witnessed one of the greatest and thrilling Cricket encounters of all time. Two of the competitive Cricket nation on a must-win match; the match was tied, and went to a double super over. It's not just the fact that the control of the match has been changing from one another until the last ball of the second Super over. It's just absolute cinema, and what more could a fan ask for? Well played, Afghanistan, but tough luck once again! It was your game to lose! ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZXTegMoAkN6b6nWXz4XyPGL7mzGf3MFJWpqLghpCfWUK/image.png) #### 1st inning from South Africa -------------- * Well, it was the 13th match of the tournament, and one of the most important matches in Group D. As only two teams could advance to the next round, the winner of this match would go through it most probably along with New Zealand. * Anyhow, with all the pressure and excitement, South Africa started batting first at Narendra Modi Stadium, Ahmedabad. Markram didn't get his rhythm, and in the third over, Farroqi took him. Anyhow, from there, Quinton de Kock and Ryan Rickelton took control. * SA just scored 43/1 at 7.16 RPO in the power play. But then accelerated, adding 81/0 at 13.5 RPO in the next 5 overs. De Kock scored 59 runs off 41 balls with 5 fours and 3 sixes. Iickelton was the monster, adding 61 runs in 28 balls with 5 fours and 4 sixes. * Anyhow, Rashid Khan got both the set batters in the same over, and Afghanistan came back once again. They just gave 35/4 at 5.83 RPO in the next 5 overs. But with a good finish, SA managed 187/6 in their 20 overs. #### Chase from Afghanistan -------------- * Afghanistan got a positive start, especially from Gurbaz's bat. In fact, they scored 50 runs in the first four overs of the powerplay, but then lost three quick wickets in the next overs for just 6 runs. Anyhow, Gurbaz carried the inning even with a little support from the other end, and he scoreda 26-ball fifty. * They reached 100 in 11 overs, and with 7 wickets remaining, they were on top. Anyway, in the 13th over, another 2 wickets fell, including Gurbaz. (84 off 42 balls, 4 fours, and 7 sixes.) * Rashid and Azmatullah Omarzai once again built the confidence, and they had just 30 runs in the last 3 overs with 4 wickets remaining. But once again match turned to SA as Lungi Ngidi balled a stunner in the 18th over, taking a wicket for 6 runs. In the next over, Marco got Rashid as well. When everyone thought the match was over, Noor Ahmad smashed a six and showed some hope. * 13 runs off the last over from Rabada with just a wicket remaining. Kagiso Rabada vs Noor Ahmad🔥 19.1- Nb, 19.1 - Wd, 19.1 - 0, 19.2 - 6, 19.3 - 0, 19.3 - N2, 19.4 - W1 -------- * Balling wides, and no balls, Rabada tried his best to throw away the match, and Afghanistan almost grabbed it. But with a sloppy run from the Farooqi, the match was tied. #### 1st Super Over -------------- * Azmat and Gurbaz came to the middle with Nabi to follow. Ngidi got the ball. Azmat just did what they needed most, smashing two fours and a six. Afghanistan scored 17 runs. Ball 1 – 4 runs, Ball 2 – 6 runs, Ball 3 – 1 run, Ball 4 – 1 run, Ball 5 – 4 runs, Ball 6 – 1 run * Miller and Brevis came up against Farooqi. Ball 1 – 1 run, Ball 2 – 6 runs, Ball 3 – wicket, Ball 4 – 4 runs, Ball 5 – dot ball, Ball 6 – 6 runs * Bethel got out after hitting a six in the third ball. 11 needed off 3 balls. Then Stubbs scored a four on the fourth ball, missed out on the next. 7 needed of the last ball, and it wasn't over. Stubbs hit a six, and SA finished on 17/1, level with Afghanistan's score. #### 2nd Super Over -------------- * Miller and Stubbs were the batters, with the ball on Azmat. Ball 1 – 6 runs, Ball 2 – 1 run, Ball 3 – 2 runs, Ball 4 – 6 runs, Ball 5 – 6 runs, Ball 6 – 2 runs South Africa finished with 23 runs, becoming the favorite. * Nabi and Azmat came to bat against Mahraj. Ball 1 – 0, Ball 2 – wicket, Ball 3 – 6 runs, Ball 4 – 6 runs, Ball 5 – 6 runs, Ball 6 – wide, Ball 7 – wicket * After the first 2 balls, everyone thought it was over as 4 sixes were needed in the last 4 balls with one wicket. Anyway, Gurbaz showed his class and smashed three sixes in a row. But unfortunately, he couldn't finish it. AFG 19/1, and SA won it. This was one hell of a match, with the excitement until the end, with the chance of winning shared to open another. Two quality performances from two quality teams. ----------- https://www.cricbuzz.com/live-cricket-scorecard/139084/rsa-vs-afg-13th-match-group-d-icc-mens-t20-world-cup-2026 Thank you. Best Regards!

title: Your Chase Sapphire Just Got a Workout Buddy (and a Discount!)
text:
Hey fitness fanatics and savvy savers! Ever wonder if your credit card could do more than just rack up points for that dream vacation? What if it could also help you get in shape *and* save you some serious dough? Well, buckle up, buttercups, because Chase Sapphire is doing just that!

We're talking about **WHOOP**, the super-smart wearable that’s basically a tiny, all-knowing health guru strapped to your wrist. Forget just counting steps; WHOOP dives deep into your sleep, recovery, and daily strain, giving you personalized insights to optimize your well-being. It's like having a crystal ball for your body!

Now, for the exciting part! If you're rocking a Chase Sapphire card, you're in for a treat.

[SOURCE](https://thriftytraveler.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Sapphire-cards-with-a-cup-of-coffee-e1761578176976.jpg)

*   **Got the Chase Sapphire Preferred?** You can snag a sweet **$60 statement credit** when you sign up for an annual WHOOP membership. That takes the usual $239 down to a much friendlier $179. Not too shabby for getting smarter about your health!
*   **Rolling with the Chase Sapphire Reserve?** Oh, you fancy! You'll pocket an even bigger **$90 statement credit** on that annual WHOOP membership. Your price drops from $239 to a super-cool $149. That's practically a steal for all that health intel!

The best part? This isn't just for newbies! Whether you're a long-time WHOOP devotee or you've been curious to try it out, this offer is for *you*. Just link your eligible Chase Sapphire card, and boom – instant savings on your annual membership.

Think about it: WHOOP helps you understand when to push harder and, more importantly, when to rest and recover. It's about optimizing your life, not just tracking it. And with your Chase Sapphire card stepping in to make it more affordable, there's never been a better time to invest in your health. It's like your credit card is giving you a high-five for being smart with your money *and* your body.

So, if you've been eyeing a WHOOP or are an existing member looking to renew, dust off that Sapphire card and get ready to elevate your health game without draining your wallet. Smart spending meets smart living – now that's a combination we can all get behind!

author: vik24
title: Daily Grind or Daily Gainz? Can You REALLY Lift Every Single Day?
text:
Alright, fitness fanatics and couch potatoes alike, gather 'round! We've all seen that one gym rat who practically lives under the squat rack, right? Or maybe you've had that thought cross your mind: "If I lifted *every single day*, I'd be shredded in no time!" Sounds tempting, doesn't it? But is it actually a smart move, or a one-way ticket to Snap City?

Let's cut to the chase, because I know your time is precious (especially if you're planning your next workout!). The answer, according to the wise sages of strength, is a resounding… **YES! You *can* lift every day. BUT – and this is a huge, capital letters, flashing neon sign BUT – it’s all about how you play the game.**

[SOURCE](https://hips.hearstapps.com/hmg-prod/images/mhstrongtalkpod-liftingeveryday-1-698cbfffec0e0.jpg?crop=1xw:1xh;center,top&resize=1200:*)

You see, "lifting every day" doesn't mean you go full beast mode, maxing out your deadlifts, bench presses, and every other heavy lift until your muscles scream for mercy, 7 days a week. Nope, that's a fast track to injury, burnout, and probably a serious case of grumpiness. Your body needs to recover, people! That's when the magic (a.k.a. muscle growth) actually happens.

**So, what's the secret sauce for daily lifting success?**

Think of it like a smart chef planning their menu. They don't make a Michelin-star, five-course meal every single night. Sometimes it's a light salad, sometimes a hearty stew, sometimes just toast! Your daily "lift" should be just as varied:

1.  **Mix Up the Menu:** Don't hit the same muscle group hard every day. If Monday was heavy chest, Tuesday could be light legs, Wednesday mobility work, Thursday back and biceps, Friday full body, and maybe Saturday a long walk with some bodyweight squats thrown in. Spread the love!
2.  **Intensity Control is Key:** Not every day needs to be a PR attempt. Some days can be about lighter weights and higher reps, focusing on form, or even just bodyweight exercises. Think of it as active recovery rather than pure demolition.
3.  **Listen to Your Body (Seriously!):** This is non-negotiable. If something hurts, stop. If you're feeling unusually fatigued, take a lighter day or an actual rest day. Your body is smart; it sends signals. Don't ignore them!
4.  **Define "Lifting":** It doesn't always have to be barbells and dumbbells. "Lifting" can include resistance bands, kettlebells, calisthenics, or even just really focused stretching and mobility drills that challenge your strength in different ways.

**Who is this daily dedication for?** Generally, it's best for folks who are already pretty familiar with gym etiquette, understand their body's limits, and have their nutrition and sleep game on point. If you're new to the fitness world, starting with 3-4 full-body workouts a week is a fantastic way to build a solid foundation without overdoing it.

Ultimately, lifting every day isn't about proving how tough you are. It's about consistency, smart programming, and building a sustainable routine that makes you feel strong, energized, and ready to tackle whatever life throws at you. So go ahead, chase those daily gains – just make sure you're doing it the smart way!

---

author: sports.guy55
title: What winter Olympic sports are you watching?
text:
I've never been one to get all that excited about the Olympics.   To me they are one of those things that if it is on I will turn to the channel every now and then and see what is going on, but I don't really know much about the various games and I certainly don't plan my day around what event is going to be on.   

Normally, when I do find something that I am interested in it is just because it happens to be on at the time and I don't really know enough about it to really get too committed to any of it. 

<center> https://www.rollingstone.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/GettyImages-2259436736.jpg
[src](https://www.rollingstone.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/GettyImages-2259436736.jpg)</center>

one sport I never really saw myself becoming a fan of is curling and I want to say that I am not alone in this.   I wouldn't say that I really have any idea what the strategy could possibly be in this game, I do find it rather fun to watch.   It is also very amazing to me that these guys and girls seem to get the giant rock thing to land exactly where they want it to be every single time.   It also seems to me that the person who goes 2nd has a huge advantage as far as the overall score is concerned.   

I'm not super patriotic, but I do root for "my team" whenever they are rolling or sliding or whatever it is called.   Since these games take a really long time it seems to be on basically at all hours too.  Who is the best at it?  I have no idea.  All the people I have seen play it seem to be extremely good and I guess that makes the events even more exciting despite the rather slow pace of it.   It's fun to have a commentary with me and my friends who clearly now next to nothing about the sport but still make up our own terms for what is about to go down.  

<center> https://res.cloudinary.com/usopc-prod/image/upload/v1744241042/TeamUSA%20Assets/News/Germain110125vt5551.jpg
[src](https://res.cloudinary.com/usopc-prod/image/upload/v1744241042/TeamUSA%20Assets/News/Germain110125vt5551.jpg)</center>

Another sport that I really enjoy watching is the biathlon.   I saw a funny joke the other day about how since a triathlon is running, cycling and swimming so a biathlon must be two of those things right?  Well, not exactly. 

Whoever decided that cross-country skiing should be mixed with firing a rifle is an absolute genius.   Going fast is always a big part of it, but if you don't hit the targets well enough at the shooting stages now you have to do punishment laps that are going to put you behind in your skiing.  Being able to do one of the two better than anyone else but being average in the other is NOT going to win you the event.   What a crazy duo of skills to have huh? 

I really enjoy watching this although I have to say that I rarely know what the hell is going on.  It's just fun to me.  Regular shooting and regular cross-country skiing is quite boring to me, but somehow the two together are just absolutely awesome.  Unfortunately, I don't get to see this very often because they have to do it during the early day in Italy and this time zone doesn't match up with my life.   We always have highlights I guess.

<center> https://images.immediate.co.uk/production/volatile/sites/3/2026/02/GettyImages-2260044445-b85cdb3.jpg?quality=90&resize=980,654
[src](https://images.immediate.co.uk/production/volatile/sites/3/2026/02/GettyImages-2260044445-b85cdb3.jpg?quality=90&resize=980,654)</center>
There is one aspect of the Winter Olympics that I am "meh" about though and that is almost all of the snowboarding events.  I believe that snowboarding being in the Olympics at all is the result of the popularity of the X-games in the past (I don't think those still exist do they) and this pressured the Olympic committee to take it on board (pun intended.)   But a great many of the snowboarding events to me seem either very subjective (who did a twist flip the best?) or they seem like something that you can't possibly do any better than the person before and it all just becomes a question of who weighs the perfect amount? 

I don't know what the event is called but it looked like the pic above.  There was a track of easily accomplished bumps going down a hill and there wasn't any way for the participants to move any faster than they were.  It also didn't seem to be particularly challenging to any of the people involved so what he ended up with was times that were within a second or two for each and every person that did it.  

When we compare this to the ski slalom for example, there are people that realize they have to push the limits in order to have a chance at getting in the top 3 so they really do crazy things to move perhaps a lot faster than they should.  This results in about 20% of the racers crashing and burning.  In the aforementioned snowboarding event there was no chance for the participants to even take a big chance and no one came even close to falling down.   It was just really boring and didn't make a lot of sense to me.   

If I see a snowboarding event is on I will search for something else to watch or to just not watch the Olympics at all.   

They will never take these events out though because there is too much money to be made with sponsorship.   

Well that is two events I love and 1 that I could do without? You have anything to add?

author: frafiomatale
title: Referees eliminated
text:
![lucid-origin_A_referee_during_a_soccer_match_leaves_the_field_throwing_yellow_and_red_cards_i-0.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXbFzmXJu5FDzZNPrJCRJMULxkfEBGVjZDB4S5Vetvqk5/lucid-origin_A_referee_during_a_soccer_match_leaves_the_field_throwing_yellow_and_red_cards_i-0.jpg) [Image created with Leonardo.ai](https://app.leonardo.ai/image-generation) #### #### _This is the English version of the post [Arbitri eliminati](https://steemit.com/italy/@frafiomatale/arbitri-eliminati), originally published in Italian in the ITALY community._ ####
![#LIFE.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPuAbxsvCJFEcTAJr6BMtCoRdaNC5fsERb5nq4JhMXjRT/#LIFE.png)
The introduction of VAR into the world of football was intended, by those who manage the game, to reduce controversy to zero, giving match officials a tool capable of transforming on-field decisions from subjective to as objective as possible. "No one will be able to favor one team over another anymore," parroted the supporters of the so-called "On-field replay," along with the other _evergreen_ of "psychological submissiveness" towards big clubs, which seemed destined to disappear. However, after almost a decade of use, it has been discovered that not only has VAR failed to eliminate refereeing controversies, but it has actually increased them. Every week, on almost every pitch in Serie A and the minor leagues, complaints and accusations are recorded from this or that coach. ![lucid-origin_A_Serie_A_soccer_coach_similar_to_Antonio_Conte_complains_to_journalists_about_t-0.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWYwAWrNvPrVMpLwYNRVxJJKSsuRvxQn9r3umP93R2VeN/lucid-origin_A_Serie_A_soccer_coach_similar_to_Antonio_Conte_complains_to_journalists_about_t-0.jpg) [Image created with Leonardo.ai](https://app.leonardo.ai/image-generation) It doesn't matter whether the reason for the protest is a card given or not given, a disallowed goal, a millimetric offside, a foul missed by the cameras, or any other questionable case: no one is happy with VAR, its protocol, its practical application, and those who manage it. In other words, the experiment (that's how they sold it to us at the beginning) can safely be considered a failure. And indeed, beyond _Goal Line technology_, a system that nonetheless exists independently of the electronic eye, the system has proven to have enormous flaws. The worst refereeing "atrocities" have been seen in recent years, when referees delegated much of their decision-making autonomy to the eyes of colleagues in front of the TV. And so goals have been disallowed for offside by a piece of a jersey or a boot stud, or even because defenders disappeared from the camera frames. ![lucid-origin_Soccer_referees_with_diverse_facial_features_and_varying_skin_tones_wearing_stan-0.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmRiRbYVtKKfjifYsPyQYiBWM6HyfeTpfCkgMV8cDjhWh4/lucid-origin_Soccer_referees_with_diverse_facial_features_and_varying_skin_tones_wearing_stan-0.jpg) [Image created with Leonardo.ai](https://app.leonardo.ai/image-generation) Players have learned that the "on-field" foul, where the referee directly assesses the intensity of the contact, has been replaced by the VAR foul, where every "touch" on the opponent, seen in slow motion, can become a brutal foul, especially if enriched by the classic performance of those who have learned to roll on the ground. Almost all teams have started to include the "strategic roll" into their game tactics, the performance to be done for the camera at the slightest contact during a potentially dangerous opponent's chance, so they can cling to an alleged foul in case a goal is conceded. Celebrating scored goals has now become risky, given the high possibility of a VAR review, whether for a contact that occurred thirty seconds earlier, for an offside by a shoelace, or for an accidental handball, effectively irrelevant to the play. ![lucid-origin_Soccer_players_complaining_to_a_referee_about_a_disallowed_goal-0.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWH1XRfHxs22pD1xvoGq3MLziGSbPLw56EVnPYXNYUBon/lucid-origin_Soccer_players_complaining_to_a_referee_about_a_disallowed_goal-0.jpg) [Image created with Leonardo.ai](https://app.leonardo.ai/image-generation) So here is the question: what are referees on the field really for, if decisions are then mostly made by those in front of the monitor? The revolution I hope to see soon is the following: get rid of all referees, get rid of the linesmen, the fourth official, and VAR, and have decisions entrusted solely to an AI, connected to all the cameras filming the match. If we must eliminate human decision-making capabilities, we might as well do it properly. ####
## I'm part of ITALYGAME Team **together with: @girolamomarotta, @sardrt, @mikitaly, @mad-runner, @famigliacurione**

👉 VOTE FOR US AS WITNESS👈 ![Progetto senza titolo (10).jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmaaNuF8gaoBCnFXZ2atqX8RbaceziqKXatioiUEQZHMMm/Progetto%20senza%20titolo%20(10).jpg)

author: vik24
title: Koda's Guide to Pure Joy: Unleashing Your Inner Puppy!
text:
Ever watch a dog and just wish you had their zest for life? You know, that boundless energy, that pure, unadulterated happiness over... well, anything? I've been getting my daily dose of inspiration lately from an absolute legend named Koda, and let me tell you, this pup has life figured out!

Koda's daily routine looks something like this: WAKE UP! EXPLORE! SNIFF EVERYTHING! DIG A HOLE (or two)! RUN LIKE THE WIND! Repeat. It's truly a masterclass in living in the moment. Forget your to-do lists and your worries for a sec; Koda reminds us that sometimes, the best thing you can do is just *go*.

[SOURCE](https://scontent-ord5-2.cdninstagram.com/v/t51.82787-15/632082719_18425839582138768_6013905878355598536_n.jpg?stp=c240.0.720.720a_dst-jpg_e35_s640x640_tt6&_nc_cat=1&ccb=7-5&_nc_sid=18de74&efg=eyJlZmdfdGFnIjoiQ0FST1VTRUxfSVRFTS5iZXN0X2ltYWdlX3VybGdlbi5DMyJ9&_nc_ohc=x4d-53VEYtwQ7kNvwGn5b1l&_nc_oc=AdnJ1BzvJrj1wcNHsSPSEDnYiYYrdYuXVUg-6Ff1ZvhyR-Po7jYBQ834f_1GHI4tBA&_nc_zt=23&_nc_ht=scontent-ord5-2.cdninstagram.com&_nc_gid=wRj6cU10auZVdpCgBAQQFQ&oh=00_AftSjZydkc-Cd_8Ch1SWp31a10cIaBhqYVAtNWTf-SC1Ng&oe=6991E211)

Seriously, when was the last time you dug a hole with such enthusiasm? Or ran through a field with absolutely no destination in mind, just for the sheer joy of it? Koda dives headfirst into every sniff, every sprint, every muddy patch, showing us how to truly soak up the simple pleasures. He doesn't care about being graceful, or what anyone thinks of his digging technique. He's just *happy*.

So, next time you feel a bit bogged down, take a page from Koda's book. Find your own personal "field" (even if it's just a park bench or a quiet room), sniff out something interesting (a good book, a new hobby), and maybe, just maybe, unleash your inner Koda for a bit. You might be surprised how much joy you find!

Original Article: [https://www.instagram.com/p/DUl7TGFEhOu/](https://www.instagram.com/p/DUl7TGFEhOu/)

author: sports.guy55
title: The Super Bowl was a massive letdown this year
text:
I suppose you could say the same about last year and if you are not a fan of NFL, you could say it about every year I guess.   This year saw the Seattle Seahawks absolutely dominate the field in a very one-sided affair that was basically determined after the first quarter.   

<center> 
![image.png](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/sports.guy55/23u5vfQb4ZhbrZwhyHwhPUXJH5fReMkZzCS7VxTRitXWeTWrfQcvcTxzFfEvnwwfsEtf1.png)
[src](https://dam.mediacorp.sg/image/upload/s--gLqP4X4m--/c_fill,g_auto,h_468,w_830/fl_relative,g_south_east,l_mediacorp:cna:watermark:2024-04:reuters_1,w_0.1/f_auto,q_auto/v1/one-cms/core/2026-02-09T034914Z_3_LYNXMPEM18066_RTROPTP_3_FOOTBALL-NFL-SUPERBOWL.JPG?itok=1Kas7Fg5)</center>

I arrived late to the game where my friends had saved me a seat at a packed bar of people that were mostly cheering for the Eagles.  Things were noisy at the beginning but then the crowd in the place I was at started to quiet down as it became more and more evident that the Patriots just didn't seem to have much going for them and that Seattle was definitely going to win. 

<center> 
![image.png](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/sports.guy55/23xAVYMwM3HAZLqFnd3GAzwUtsXM6tEvAwHWUyQz3tmYeEHv5ey5CHQGK4YYP4h75GYED.png)
[src](https://a57.foxnews.com/static.foxnews.com/foxnews.com/content/uploads/2026/02/1200/675/nfl-patriots-kyle-williams-020826-2.jpg?ve=1&tl=1)</center>

one of the best tackles the Patriots had on the night was when a streaker rushed the field and Kyle Williams helped to catch him.  Imagine spending thousands upon thousands of dollars it takes to go the the Super Bowl and then rushing the field, getting fined and then kicked out.   They won't even show this sort of thing on the air in order to discourage people from doing it but yet, here I am posting it.  

Watching the game on TV is an exercise in extreme frustration due to the prolific amounts of commercials that they have during this game.   The game itself as far as the clock is concerned is only 1 hour long, yet the game takes over 4 hours to complete and while they will claim this is because of various aspects of the game where the game starts and stops that is BS and they know it.   How else can they get over 3 hours of commercials at us.

As for the halftime show:  I know that this is a point of controversy but I personally don't give a shit.   It doesn't make much sense to me that we would have a show that is almost entirely in Spanish in a game that is watched almost exclusively by Americans who are overwhelmingly English-speaking but if I had to guess I would say that it is rooted in politics and that makes it even stupider.

The problem with this game was that it was not competitive.  At no point in time did it appear as though the Patriots were remotely a threat and Seattle wasn't even being all that creative with their play choices.  The amount of times they handed the ball over the Kennety Walker III was just nuts and what is more nuts is the Pats had to have known this was going to happen but were just completely unable to stop it. 

<center> https://www.wlns.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/50/2026/02/AP26040023701098.jpg?w=2560&h=1440&crop=1
[src](https://www.wlns.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/50/2026/02/AP26040023701098.jpg?w=2560&h=1440&crop=1)</center>

When they would hand him the ball on every other snap and he would rush for a gain of 4-10 yards every single time I would groan but at the same time if I am the coach of Seattle and this is working time after time after time after time, why would you change the strategy?   The Patriots are helpless to stop it so it's guaranteed victory.  K. Walker won the MVP of the match and it's deserved I guess but seeing as how he had the ball 50% of the time this was always going to happen.  

The Patriots were just awful on offense as well, allowing 6 sacks and throwing 2 interceptions.   I think there was a fumble in there as well but at that point I don't think I was really even paying attention anymore.  

When the Patriots got their one and only impressive passing TD I kind of felt like the Seahawks weren't 100% trying to prevent it anymore.   The game was already won at this point and all of us in the audience were bored.   

Last year when the Chiefs put up a similar level of non-impressive I was disappointed as well.  I was supporting the Chiefs but I wasn't really upset that they lost, I was upset that the game was friggin boring.   These are supposed to be the best of the best and when it doesn't end up that way it kind of makes me ask myself why the hell am I spending nearly 5 hours of my life on this crap.  

I think that next year I am not going to bother watching even though everyone else is.   Just like with MMA cards that bore me I sometimes feel better about not watching it live and just catching the score after the fact.

author: sports.guy55
title: The UFC heavyweight division is beyond repair
text:
It's hard to imagine that it wasn't even that long ago that the heavyweight division was one of, if the not most, sought after division in UFC or perhaps even MMA as a whole.  The idea of juggernauts that weight 220 lbs (100kg) or more getting in a ring for a scrap with almost no holds barred is something that brings about a level of excitement that the little guys simply cannot bring out.  I remember really dreading the flyweight and lightweight categories as we made our way to the "big guys" but now it is the other way around.

<center> https://www.worldmmaawards.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/brock-lesnar.jpg
[src](https://www.worldmmaawards.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/brock-lesnar.jpg)</center>

It certainly wasn't entirely because of him, but Brock Lesnar being an absolute beast certainly helped a lot and the fact that they didn't pave the way to the top for him was a big plus as well.  This addition, and the genuine nature of his abilities, perhaps lead to UFC and WWE becoming "pals" and that is why they fly under one banner these days.   

One thing I liked about Brock was that once he started to flounder, UFC didn't tailor-make fights for him that they knew he could win just to finagle his way back to the top.  They put him in real fights and he lost a lot of them.  Then he left the sport on his own to return to WWE where it was a lot less likely that you were going to get put to sleep in front of millions of people.   

The heavyweight division these days is just a joke.   There is nobody worth watching in there and the "champion" is out indefinitely due to an eye gouge and the necessary surgery that has come along with it.  

<center> 
![image.png](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/sports.guy55/EoeMC94XsFy5KVTokhZF198FtfMLXnPAAvhC6wCbTBmjpJURs3fae9GhwtuJ4hoFaZr.png)
[src](https://www.ufc.com/rankings)</center>

Ciryl Gane was the new "golden boy" for UFC and the hope was that he was going to breathe new life into the foundering division.  The main problem with him was that he was extremely boring and people therefore couldn't wait for someone to come along and knock his block off.   

Jon Jones came back out of retirement to fight in the heavyweight division, defeated Gane, then dodged a fight with Tom Aspinall for ages citing various excuses, then retired again, then unretired but is unconfirmed about which division he wants to fight in, and now the latest news from him is him hanging out in IG videos and complaining about his arthritic hip.  Not exactly the sort of talk and actions of someone that is dedicated to a sport.  

Number 2 ranked Volkov has two split decision in a row and very boring 15-minute dance contests that had boos erupting from the crowd even when he was declared the winner.   Pavlovich's last 3 fights have also been decisions and very boring ones at that. 

<center> 
![image.png](https://files.peakd.com/file/peakd-hive/sports.guy55/23tcPAu8nFsRg869LmJ5EbGsPCRYcLCL2stnHsqHnDP6YeJqetd4nuZmBkNGtdP2CAPkh.png)
</center>

If you venture further down the list you have a group of people that most in the world can't probably even imagine who they are and this is because they are so boring that if they do have a fight at all they are on some minor event, or so far down the fight card that it is unlikely that they are going to be seen at all.  Tai Tuivasa, deservedly, has been removed from the top 15 while his opponent in the last fight, Tallison Teixeira has gone DOWN a spot despite "winning" his last fight.   It seems as though they just don't know what to do with this division and well, I wouldn't either.

There just isn't any really good talent left in the big boys and I really don't have any idea how they could change this.  They keep trying to push Derrick Lewis because he is funny on the microphone and has a fun little thing he does when he wins but that is the problem... the dude just doesn't win very much unless he is fighting against a "nobody" that the UFC was probably hoping he would lose to.   Derrick has been around so long that UFC is in the phase now where they are using him as a springboard for new talent, but the new talent just isn't there.

When we look at other forms of combat sports, the heavyweight division is normally the one that people look forward to the most, but here we are with UFC and it is one that people don't really even think about at all.   

There was a time when we could fill up almost an entire card with heavyweight fights but these days if there even is one, it is normally relegated to the background because so much can go wrong.  Tai Tuivasa and  Teixeira was supposed to be a slugfest that reignited interest in the division but the opposite happened and the fans were booing and just wanted the two fatties to get out of the octagon and get out of there so they could see a good fight.  

<center> https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/gcdn/authoring/authoring-images/2026/02/01/SMMA/88462886007-getty-images-2258756420.jpg?width=700&height=467&fit=crop&format=pjpg&auto=webp
[src](https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/gcdn/authoring/authoring-images/2026/02/01/SMMA/88462886007-getty-images-2258756420.jpg?width=700&height=467&fit=crop&format=pjpg&auto=webp)</center>

I get that nobody wants to get caught with a tree-trunk sized arm catching them on the face but the alternative is to win on points for being the best dance-hugger isn't going to get you future fights.  

At this point in time I really feel as though the best thing that UFC could do for the heavyweight division is to take a play from the film "Aliens."  Take off, nuke it from orbit...it's the only way to be sure.   

Let's stop thinking that we can tweak this division back out from obscurity.  We can't.  It is too far broken to repair.  Nobody cares about anyone in this division and even if you did manage to bring back Jon Jones for a fight the people would care for just that one fight and then go back to groaning whenever there was a heavyweight fight on the card.  It really shouldn't be that way.
