author: vik24
title: Rise and Shine: Unleashing Your Inner Phoenix (Fitness Style!)
text:
Mondays, right? They can feel like a mountain to climb even *before* you've had your first coffee. But what if I told you that starting your week strong could be way more fun, less intimidating, and actually make you feel like a superhero?

That's exactly what I stumbled upon thanks to the awesome folks at Phoenix Fitness of GVL, who recently showcased their magic on WSPA's "Your Carolina" for their "Move It Monday" segment. And trust me, it wasn't about fancy gadgets or confusing routines. It was about getting back to basics, having a blast, and feeling good in your own skin!

[SOURCE](https://www.wspa.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/112/2024/05/Screenshot_20240506_105234_YouTube.jpg?w=1280)

*Okay, so I don't have a direct photo link from the original article, but imagine a group of super happy, strong people getting their sweat on! This is my best attempt to represent the vibe!*

So, what's their secret sauce? It's all about **functional fitness**. Sounds super technical, but it's really just a fancy way of saying: "Let's train our bodies to be awesome at all the stuff we do every single day!" Think lifting groceries, playing with your kids, rocking that hike, or just feeling zippy walking up the stairs. It's about building real-world strength and muscle that makes life easier and more enjoyable.

They showed off a killer full-body circuit that hit all the right notes. We're talking movements like:
*   **Squats:** Because who doesn't need a stronger booty and legs for, well, living?
*   **Push-ups:** Get ready to feel like a powerhouse pushing the world away (or just opening that heavy door!).
*   **Kettlebell Swings:** These are like a magic wand for your whole body, building power and getting your heart pumping.
*   **Battle Ropes:** Ever wanted to feel like a Viking? This is your chance to unleash some awesome energy!

The best part? It's not about being perfect from day one. It's about **consistency** and **starting somewhere**. The trainers at Phoenix Fitness emphasize that showing up is half the battle, and they'll help you with the other half – making sure your form is on point so you're safe and getting the most out of every single movement. No more guesswork, just great results!

So, if you're in the Greenville area and looking for a fitness spot that's welcoming, fun, and focused on making you stronger for life, check out Phoenix Fitness. They'll help you rise up and conquer your fitness goals, one awesome workout at a time. And who knows, maybe your next Monday won't feel so mountainous after all!

***
*Inspired by: [Move It Monday: Phoenix Fitness of GVL](https://www.wspa.com/your-carolina/move-it-monday-phoenix-fitness-of-gvl/)*

author: infogalaxy
title: The Scientific Formula: How Experts Quantify Sporting Difficulty.
text:
Determining the hardest sport is not merely a matter of opinion. Sports scientists, journalists, and athletic organizations have developed methodologies to quantify difficulty using objective metrics. These studies provide fascinating insights into what makes certain sports extraordinarily demanding. This article examines the scientific approaches to measuring sporting difficulty and what the data reveals.

ESPN's Degree of Difficulty Project

One of the most comprehensive attempts to rank sports difficulty came from ESPN in 2004 and again in 2019. Their panel of experts evaluated sports across ten key athletic skills:

The Ten Metrics:

• Endurance

• Power

• Speed

• Agility

• Flexibility

• Nerve

• Durability

• Hand-eye coordination

• Analytic aptitude

• Physical strength

Each sport received scores from 1 to 10 in each category, creating a composite difficulty ranking. Boxing consistently topped these lists, followed by ice hockey and football (American). The methodology recognized that no single attribute defines difficulty-sports demand different combinations of skills.

The SportSpedia Difficulty Index

SportSpedia developed a more mathematically rigorous approach, weighting factors based on their importance to athletic performance. Their index considers:

Physiological Demands:

• Maximum oxygen consumption (VO2 max) required

• Muscle fiber type composition needed

• Energy system utilization (aerobic vs. anaerobic)

Biomechanical Complexity:

• Number of discrete motor skills required

• Coordination demands across multiple limbs

• Precision requirements under fatigue

Psychological Factors:

• Decision-making speed under pressure

• Attention span and focus duration

• Resilience to performance failure

Physiological Measurements in Sports Science

Laboratory testing provides objective data on what sports demand from the body:

Heart Rate Monitoring: During competition, elite soccer players average 85–90% of their maximum heart rate for 90 minutes. Basketball players experience similar cardiovascular strain with explosive bursts.

Lactate Threshold Testing: Sports requiring sustained high-intensity output-like rowing, cycling, and swimming-push athletes to process lactic acid at extraordinary rates. Rowers often have among the highest lactate tolerance of any athletes.

Muscle Fiber Analysis: Sprinters and weightlifters require predominance of fast-twitch fibers. Marathon runners need slow-twitch dominance. Sports like hockey and basketball demand balanced fiber type distribution.

Injury Data as a Difficulty Metric

Some researchers use injury rates to gauge physical difficulty:

High-Risk Sports:

• Rugby: 90 injuries per 1,000 player-hours

• Ice hockey: 80 injuries per 1,000 player-hours

• Boxing: 70 injuries per 1,000 player-hours

Chronic Injury Sports:

• Gymnastics: 80% of elite gymnasts report chronic back pain

• Swimming: Shoulder injuries affect nearly 50% of competitive swimmers

• Running: 50-80% of runners experience annual injuries

Training Load Quantification

Modern sports science tracks training volume as a difficulty proxy:

Weekly Training Hours:

• Gymnastics: 30-40 hours (elite level)

• Swimming: 30-35 hours (20-25 miles weekly)

• Rowing: 25-30 hours on water plus cross-training

• Boxing: 25-30 hours including sparring and conditioning

Popularity vs. Difficulty

Interestingly, the world's most popular sports do not necessarily rank highest on scientific difficulty indices. As 7 Facts About The Most Popular Sport In The World illustrates, soccer dominates with 3.5 billion fans. Yet on physiological metrics, it ranks behind sports like boxing and ice hockey in overall difficulty. This disconnect highlights an important truth: popularity measures entertainment value and accessibility, not athletic demands.

The Limitations of Quantification

While scientific approaches provide valuable data, they cannot fully capture certain dimensions of difficulty:

Fear Factor: How do you quantify the courage required to descend an Olympic ski jump or face a 100 mph hockey slapshot?

Pressure: The psychological weight of performing in front of billions during a World Cup final defies measurement.

Longevity: Sports like golf and tennis require decades of sustained excellence, a different kind of difficulty than explosive, short careers.

Scientific difficulty rankings offer objective frameworks for debate, but they cannot deliver a definitive answer. The hardest sport depends on which metrics you prioritize and which athletic qualities you value most. As we explore individual sports throughout this series, we will apply these scientific lenses while acknowledging that some aspects of sporting greatness remain beyond measurement.

author: alrikorion
title: Understanding NEW88 and the Role of new88thai
text:
The world of online betting and digital gaming has expanded significantly in recent years, with platforms like NEW88 becoming widely known among users in Asia and beyond. When researching this topic, terms like ลิขสิทธิ์ NEW88 (the licensing of NEW88) and new88thai.com often appear. These relate to the legal and operational aspects of the platform, including how it claims to be regulated, what licensing credentials it presents, and what users should consider before engaging with any online betting site.

What Is NEW88?

NEW88 is a brand name associated with online betting services that include a variety of gambling activities such as sports wagering, live casino games, slot machines, and virtual competitions. The name itself is used by various sites that claim to offer gaming services under the NEW88 brand identity. Many of these sites describe themselves as international platforms for digital entertainment, attracting users with a range of betting options and interactive games.

The central concept behind the platform is to provide a comprehensive entertainment hub where players can log in, make deposits, place bets, and monitor gaming outcomes through a centralized interface designed for both desktop and mobile access.

What Is new88thai.com?

The domain new88thai.com is one of the web addresses associated with the NEW88 brand and is commonly referenced in online discussions about NEW88 in Thailand. According to information linked to this domain, the site presents itself with contact details such as a business address in Bangkok and claims to operate under gaming licenses, including one from PAGCOR (Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation).

PAGCOR is a gaming authority in the Philippines that issues licenses to online gaming operators. A license from such a regulator generally indicates that the operator is subject to certain legal requirements, financial oversight, and consumer protection standards. For users, this means that the platform claiming this license should adhere to rules about fair play, financial transparency, and data security.

However, it is also important to independently verify licensing claims directly on the regulator’s official website rather than relying solely on what a third‑party domain displays.

Understanding the Concept of ลิขสิทธิ์ NEW88

The term ลิขสิทธิ์ NEW88 refers broadly to the licensing, authorization, and legal credentials that a platform claims to hold to operate as an online betting service. Licensing in online gaming is crucial because it determines whether a platform is operating under regulated conditions or not. Legitimate licenses usually come from recognized authorities such as PAGCOR, the Isle of Man Gambling Supervision Commission, or the Cagayan Economic Zone and Freeport in the Philippines.

Licensing from a reputable regulator typically means that a platform must follow strict standards to protect players, ensure fair game outcomes, and safeguard financial transactions. Regulators often require operators to maintain separate accounts for player funds, implement anti‑money‑laundering procedures, and submit to periodic audits.

On the other hand, claims of licensing on a commercial website should be interpreted carefully. Users should verify licensing directly through the issuing authority’s official registry when possible to confirm whether the license is valid, active, and applicable to the region from which they intend to play.

Why Licensing Matters in Online Betting

Licensing is one of the most important aspects of safety and trust in the online betting industry. A platform operating with a valid license from a recognized authority demonstrates a commitment to compliance with legal and ethical standards. Licensed operators are usually required to protect user data, segregate player funds, and publish transparent terms and conditions.

When a platform like NEW88 presents licensing information, users should consider what regulator has issued the license and whether that regulator is reputable. International authorities such as PAGCOR are well‑known in the gaming industry for overseeing casino and sports betting operations. This does not guarantee perfection, but it does generally indicate that the operator is subject to external oversight.

Users should also be aware that licensing by an offshore authority may not automatically make a platform legal in every country. The legal status of online betting varies by jurisdiction, and a license that is valid in one country might not confer legality in another. Independent verification of the licensing and compliance status of any online betting platform is a recommended step before engaging in any financial transactions or gameplay.

What to Look For When Verifying Licenses

When researching the legitimacy of a platform like NEW88 and its associated domain new88thai.com, several factors should be considered:

First, check the licensing claims against the official registry of the stated authority. For example, PAGCOR has an online listing of companies it has licensed, and many reputable regulators offer searchable databases for this purpose.

Second, look at whether the platform clearly displays terms and conditions, privacy policies, and responsible gambling information. This transparency typically indicates a more legitimate operation.

Third, evaluate user feedback and independent reviews from trusted sources to understand whether others have encountered issues with withdrawals, account access, or game fairness.

Finally, consider whether the platform’s operations are compatible with the legal requirements of your own jurisdiction. Even if a platform holds a foreign license, it may not be legal for users in certain countries to engage with its services.

Conclusion

Understanding ลิขสิทธิ์ NEW88 and the context surrounding new88thai.com involves more than just reading the claims on a website. Licensing in the online betting industry is a critical component of trust, fairness, and legal compliance. Platforms that claim to operate under licenses from recognized authorities such as PAGCOR or offshore regulators are generally more credible than those that do not provide verifiable credentials.

However, verifying these claims through official regulatory websites and understanding the local legal environment where you live remains essential. This helps ensure that you engage with online betting platforms in a manner that is both safe and compliant with applicable laws.

author: tara55
title: Ice Cold Revenge: USA Delivers Historic 5-0 Silence to Canada at Milano Cortina
text:
The USA women‘s ice hockey team produced a historic performance at the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics,  as they shut out arch, rival Canada 5, 0 on their final preliminary game on February 10.  This game, played at the Milano Santagiulia  Ice Hockey Arena,  not only marked the first time in Olympic history that the USA has defeated Canada in a shutout but also set the record for the largest margin of victory between the two nations at the Olympics.


 The result completed a 4, 0 record in preliminary round play for Team USA, who dominated the group stage with a 25, 1 goal differential. The win stretched the Americans’ winning streak over Canada to seven games, including a pre,  Olympic Rivalry Series series late last year,  and represented only their second loss in Olympic preliminary round play (their first was against the USA at Nagano 1998). Despite the setback, Canada faced a few obstacles to overcome,  such as the absence of long, time captain Marie,  Philip Poulin due to injury.
From the start of the game,  it was all Team USA, as they dictated the pace with pressure,  precision passing, and physicality, while Aerin Frankel made save after save to earn the shutout. The defense was disciplined,  clogging shooting lanes and frustrating the Canadian attack with size and skill.
Hannah Bilka scored twice,  demonstrating her speed and finishing touch. Caroline Harvey scored on an individual effort, while Kirsten Simms and Laila Edwards added single markers.  Long, time captain Hilary Knight,  likely in her final Olympic tournament,  had a hand in the attack, scoring a goal and tying a U.S. Olympic record.
The result demonstrated the depth and confidence within the American program.  While world, class players like Bilka, Edwards, Harvey,  and the legendary Knight combined effectively with veteran leaders,  Team USA suffocated the Canadians and prevented them from building any substantial pressure.
This win secured top seeding for the Americans in the medal rounds and bolstered their confidence heading into the elimination games.  They are set to face host nation Italy in the quarterfinals on February 13 and hope to continue their journey to another potential gold,  medal showdown against Canada or another country.
 While Canadian fans will dearly feel the blow of the shutout, the women‘s tournament remains wide open for the medals. Both Canada and the USA are still medal contenders, whether they will be able to counter the American momentum remains to be seen.  Women‘s hockey at these Games has been a display of elite skill and fierce rivalry, and the USA‘s current domination will be closely examined as the medal rounds near.
![gm.jpeg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfEjoZ3BNPVKPvspbjaXja8dMpsoiyGxCzQgCPiEVfi1V/gm.jpeg)

author: prathamsharma
title: Ball Sports Luggage Market Expansion Trends and Forecast to 2034
text:
Through comprehensive analysis, the global ball sports luggage market report evaluates overall industry development and future growth potential. It outlines evolving consumer preferences, distribution expansion, and company strategies contributing to market progress. The study supports informed decision making by presenting insights into regional trends and competitive dynamics influencing the industry outlook.

𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗸𝗲𝘁 𝗦𝗶𝘇𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝗰𝗮𝘀𝘁
The Ball Sports Luggage Market was valued at USD 4.93 Billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 19.38 Billion by 2034. The market is expected to grow at a CAGR of 16.44% during the forecast period. Growth is supported by rising participation in ball sports, increasing awareness about proper equipment storage, and expanding retail and online sales channels.

The report provides detailed insights into revenue trends, growth patterns, and competitive strategies shaping the industry. It explains regional performance, investment movements, and evolving consumer preferences. The study helps stakeholders understand market direction and long term development opportunities through 2034.

𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗸𝗲𝘁 𝗦𝗲𝗴𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻
The market is segmented based on type and distribution channel. These categories help explain demand across different sports activities and purchasing platforms.

𝗕𝘆 𝗧𝘆𝗽𝗲
This segment focuses on luggage designed for specific ball sports. Demand depends on participation levels, professional leagues, and organized sports activities.
• Football : Football luggage supports safe transport and organized storage of balls and gear, driven by strong participation in professional and recreational football activities.
• Basketball : Basketball luggage is designed for durability and easy handling, meeting demand from schools, clubs, and sports academies.
• Table Tennis : Table tennis luggage ensures compact and protective storage, reflecting steady interest in indoor sports activities.
• Others : This category includes luggage for additional ball sports, contributing to broader product diversification in the market.

𝗕𝘆 𝗗𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗯𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗵𝗮𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗹
Distribution channels explain how products reach consumers and institutions. Retail presence and digital platforms both contribute to overall sales performance.
• Offline : Offline sales include specialty sports stores and retail outlets where customers can physically check quality, material, and design before purchasing.
• Online : Online platforms offer convenience, wider product comparison, and competitive pricing, supporting rapid digital sales growth.

𝗞𝗲𝘆 𝗣𝗹𝗮𝘆𝗲𝗿𝘀
The market is competitive with strong international brands expanding product portfolios and strengthening distribution networks.
• adidas AG : Focuses on innovative sports gear solutions supported by strong branding and global retail presence.
• Nike Inc. : Offers performance focused sports products with extensive global distribution networks.
• PUMA SE : Expands sports accessories portfolio with emphasis on design, quality, and durability.
• Under Armour Inc. : Develops athlete focused products designed for performance and functionality.
• Amer Sports : Provides diversified sports equipment and accessories through multiple brand offerings.

Companies are investing in product innovation, brand strengthening, and retail expansion to improve competitive positioning.

𝗥𝗲𝗴𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗜𝗻𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁𝘀
The market shows steady growth across major regions supported by sports culture and retail development.
• North America : Dominated the market, supported by strong sports participation, professional leagues, and well established retail infrastructure.
• Europe : Growth is driven by increasing engagement in football and basketball along with expanding sports facilities.
• Asia-Pacific : Rising youth participation in sports and growing awareness about equipment safety are supporting demand.
• Latin America : Expanding interest in organized sports and improving retail access contribute to regional growth.
• Middle East & Africa : Increasing investments in sports programs and infrastructure encourage market expansion.

Information Source: https://www.valuemarketresearch.com/report/ball-sports-luggage-market

author: ridley-321
title: How Daniel Guest Built Imagen Golf into a Leader in Modern Golf Instruction
text:
![daniel guest6.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmatZqdTLkdBZ7wKuZMEbFqarwfPaSnn7LB3Cvgqw5BAu5/daniel guest6.jpg)




Daniel Guest, born on June 10, 1965, in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, is an American golf instructor, coach, entrepreneur, author, and podcast host whose name has become synonymous with innovation and excellence in golf education. As the founder and chief executive officer of Imagen Golf, he has built one of the most respected and forward thinking golf instruction companies in the country. Guest’s philosophy centers on the belief that golf instruction should be as unique as the person receiving it, a mindset that has reshaped how players of all skill levels learn and master the game.

Guest’s connection to golf began early in life. He was born into a family with a deep legacy in the sport. His great grandfather played professionally in Scotland, the birthplace of golf, while his great uncle, Thomas Fischer, made history as the first golf professional at the DuPont Country Club in Wilmington, Delaware. Surrounded by this heritage, Guest developed an early passion for the game. During his youth in South Florida, he refined his skills on some of the region’s most prestigious courses, achieving a single digit handicap and gaining a strong understanding of the game’s physical and mental demands. These formative experiences laid the foundation for his lifelong mission to simplify and humanize golf instruction.

In 2012, Guest founded Imagen Golf with the vision of creating a smarter and more personal approach to coaching. His method focuses on understanding each player’s natural movement, personality, and mindset rather than forcing a universal model of swing mechanics. He believes that every golfer has an ideal swing already within them, and it is the coach’s job to bring it out. This approach, emphasizing individuality over imitation, quickly set Imagen Golf apart from traditional golf schools and earned Guest recognition from both amateurs and professionals.

Guest’s teaching philosophy draws inspiration from world renowned coaches such as David Leadbetter, Butch Harmon, Mike Bender, Hank Haney, David Edel, and Fred Shoemaker. However, he has built his own identity around the principle of “coaching the person, not the swing.” His lessons integrate biomechanics, psychology, and performance strategy to create lasting improvement rather than quick fixes. Over the years, Guest has taught more than thirty-nine thousand lessons to over five thousand students worldwide, from complete beginners to tour level professionals.

To deliver precise, effective, and engaging lessons, Guest incorporates advanced technology into his teaching. He uses Boditrak pressure mats, Blast Motion Golf, Sportsbox AI, V1 video swing analysis, and the GolfLive app for virtual sessions, providing his students with real time feedback that helps them track and measure progress. His ability to combine cutting edge tools with personal connection has made Imagen Golf one of the most modernized and effective instructional programs in the industry.

Guest is also one of only twenty-five golf professionals worldwide to serve as a PXG Staff Pro and ambassador for Parsons Extreme Golf, representing excellence both on and off the course. In 2023, he became a certified World Long Drive coach and began training long drive competitors including Jacob Molnar, who ranks number twenty-five in the world. His dedication to continuous learning has led him to earn an impressive number of certifications, among them Titleist TPI Certified, K Vest Certified, SAM Putting Lab Certified, Golf Digest Certified, Golfzon Certified, Operation 36 Certified, AIMPoint Certified, Dr. Kwon’s Golf Biomechanics Certified, GolfPsych Instructor Certified, Flatstick Putting Certified, Plane Truth Certified Level 1 Instructor, US Kids Golf Certified Coach, Birdie Buddies Certified Coach, Trackman Certified Master, SuperSpeed Certified Level 3, FlightScope Certified Professional, Callaway Certified Product Specialist, Titleist Golf Ball Certified, and Taylormade Certified Club Fitter.

In addition to leading Imagen Golf, Guest serves as Director for Nike Junior Golf Camps operated by Imagen Golf, where he oversees programs at the Four Seasons Golf Center in Fountainville, Pennsylvania, and the kids only driving range at Camp Curiosity in Doylestown. The camps serve golfers aged eight to seventeen, offering a structured yet enjoyable environment focused on skill building, discipline, and confidence. Guest’s philosophy extends beyond physical instruction, emphasizing sportsmanship, focus, and resilience. These programs have helped develop countless young golfers while promoting positive values that go beyond the game itself.

Guest’s work as an author has also contributed significantly to his influence. His first book, Unleash the Golfer Within, published in 2020, focuses on the mental side of the game, exploring strategies for building confidence and breaking through mental barriers. In 2025, he released Why Traditional Golf Instruction Just Doesn’t Work, a critical examination of old teaching models that rely on mechanical swing breakdowns. In this book, Guest argues that such models cause confusion and overthinking, and he instead promotes a science based, feel driven approach that reflects how people naturally learn motion.

His reach extends into media as the host of The Imagen Golf Podcast, where he shares his unfiltered thoughts on golf instruction, equipment, and the evolving state of the game. His podcast includes interviews with leading instructors, sports psychologists, and professional athletes, offering listeners a mix of practical advice and industry insight. Guest’s engaging communication style has made the show popular among golfers seeking honest and useful discussion about improvement.

Guest’s innovative spirit is also reflected in his invention of the Pure Putt Grip, a patented training aid designed to improve putting mechanics through tactile feedback and visual alignment. It encourages relaxed, consistent grip pressure and hand placement, making it especially effective for junior and amateur players. This simple yet effective invention showcases Guest’s commitment to creating tools that make learning more intuitive.

His achievements have earned him numerous honors in recent years. He was named Best 
Golf Instructor in Pennsylvania by Best of Best Review in 2024 and recognized as the Number One Golf Instructor in Pennsylvania in both the first and second quarters of 2025. He received the Best Golf Instruction Service in Pennsylvania award from Evergreen Awards and was featured in the Best of Bucks County list by the Bucks County Herald. Guest is also a frequent guest speaker at industry events such as the PGA Merchandise Show in Orlando, where he has led live demonstrations and discussions on golf technology and youth development.

Among his contributions to the sport is the creation of the Seven Seven Seven Drill Protocol, a unique system that combines neuroscience and physical training principles to help golfers retain lessons more effectively and correct common swing issues. This drill has become a key component of Imagen Golf’s teaching system and another example of Guest’s commitment to evidence based learning.

Guest’s influence extends beyond instruction through his charitable work. He founded The Imagen Golf Foundation, which operates under the motto “Fore Good.” The organization donates full sets of golf clubs to individuals who cannot afford them and supports initiatives that increase access to the sport for young people and underserved communities. He regularly collaborates with local schools and community programs to organize clinics and equipment drives, believing that golf should be a game for everyone.

Away from his professional life, Guest lives in Pennsylvania with his family, balancing his time between work, community service, and personal hobbies. A lifelong athlete, he continues to play ice hockey with a group of longtime friends and often draws parallels between hockey and golf in his coaching, using the same principles of teamwork, rhythm, and focus. He is married and has two children, one of whom works alongside him at Imagen Golf, reflecting his dedication to family and mentorship.

Through his passion, innovation, and commitment to making golf accessible and enjoyable, Daniel Guest has built Imagen Golf into a leader in modern golf instruction. His legacy continues to grow as he shapes the next generation of golfers and redefines what it means to teach and learn the game.

author: nainaztengra
title: Saving Children from Predators
text:
Sometimes the world feels very unreal, for all that keeps happening around and you wonder is this really life. Yesterday I binged watched the Jeffery Epstein files on Netflix, I felt very angry at him and at the same time I also felt angry at those girls who could not draw a line and willingly went into him. But then later on I thought, is my anger towards them justified? Sometimes the situation can become so helpless for some people. And then there are predators like him in the society who do not leave any chance to take advantage of other people's vulnerability.

What type of a human he was. I believe that people like him have lost their souls, because if they would have, they would not do such inhuman things. His death may always remain a mystery. After watching the documentary in the end, I felt that possibly he may have not died and staged it all. For people like him, it may be easy to do such things. After all money power speaks and he had contacts of all the possible influential people of the world. Or it is also very much possible that the same influential people may have got him killed. God only knows what is the truth behind all of this.

In a way good that a person like him is no more around else he would have continued doing his atrocious acts with underage children. In a society where the children must be protected, he left no chance to exploit them. And to think of it he was only full of lust who must have only kept thinking of sex all the time. Quiet a devil like trait I can say. We say that in the end justice is always served, but I feel that in that whole process the amount of damage that happens may sometimes become irreparable and even with the justice it may not help heal. It can become a life long scar.

![image.png](https://img.blurt.world/blurtimage/nainaztengra/648fe82afed67f3c767df6110ba3924e08e01809.png)
[Source](https://pixabay.com/vectors/harassment-workplace-incident-man-4499303/)

Pedophile racket is big beyond imagination in every society. Even in common households. In our remand home there are so many underage girls who have been physically abused by their own family members and their parents even after having knowledge of it would not want to do anything because of the fear of shame that they may have to go through in the society. It does not only happen in backward class, rather I have seen it happening in rich people's families also. As a teenager once, one of my relative tried to abuse me, but my dad had taught me about these things and I could save myself from him. He did not have too much guts so he made a try and when I tried to make a scene he immediately distanced out, and acted innocent, as if he was only trying to cuddle me in a warm family way.

A pervert is always around in the society. With him, it does not end.  There are so many Jeffery Epstein's in the world, it's just that majority of them are operating under cover. Possibly this documentary will give courage to a lot of underage girls if they are facing any such situation to come out and speak and get such people behind bars.

 ***Thank you for visiting my blog.*** 👼🏻👼🏻💖💖🌹🌺🌸

![](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmexfm7Y3xHUSDtYLX2gywbcyG8mt4FdQ6Uop8pxAcKw5L/image.png)
***<center>"Unlimited Abundance, Blissful Happiness and Unconditional Love"***</center>

author: kiwiscanfly
title: Big Storm⛈️💨 Grid outage 🪫🔋
text:
We have had a major storm for the past 18 odd hours with 100 kilometer winds and plenty of rain, last night as the winds got up i checked the Powerwall battery level only to see it was at 5%🪫.

That was after a cloudy rainy day yesterday, so i decided to charge it up while i could nd by 2am we were back to 100%🔋.
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcxZf3r8eujCN6nnpmwtvM3c99CZrYv8ToroX87GXzdbJ/image.png)

We lost power around 4am this morning and the battery has kept us going nicely, now that the storm has passed mostly we have a grey cloudy day but we are producing power from the solar panels.
![Screenshot_20260216_131234_Tesla.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXNBzTf1t4D3Uvdk4wKFttFPViuNNWZQ7BHi6KKQfQAUf/Screenshot_20260216_131234_Tesla.jpg)
The weather forecast for the upcoming week is looking far nicer.


-----------

In other event i brought a couple of new Mountain bike tires for my ride, the current tire's are almost bald and needing replacing.


I decided to go top of the line and pay $230 each for a couple of MAXXIS 26x4.0 Minion FBF 26x4.0 / FBR 26x4.0.
![s-l1600.webp](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWX73Z17zJbmYHU8jPKay1te5E7sRvhZSKUwmsWRcEN6P/s-l1600.webp)
These tires are similar but one is designed for the front tyre to help with steering etc, the one for the back has a grid pattern that focuses on traction.

These should be here within a week then they will be replacing the current ones.


Right now its stopped raining i feel a walk is in order.


Have a good Monday :)

*all pics are mine*
*smudge approves this post*

comments.author: ubongudofot
comments.body:
>....i checked the Powerwall battery level only to see it was at 5%🪫.

That's poor! 

As at this 2am that the battery was fully charged..., were you using a public utility to charge it...?


Also, if the power was 100% full at 2am and by 4am it discharges at that rate....it could be that  your loads were much heavier or perhaps, the battery is getting weak. Do you experience this often even on days with much sun?

I am doing a research/program on **Tesla** product

replies.author: kiwiscanfly
replies.body:
Maybe i didn't explain it properly LOL

> As at this 2am that the battery was fully charged..., were you using a public utility to charge it...?

yep just the normal power grid to charge it


Powerwall was FULL by 2am and would have sat idle until the grid went down at 4am, then the 100% battery took over and ran the house for the next 15 hours.

> ....i checked the Powerwall battery level only to see it was at 5%🪫.

That's poor!

If we had a bad day for solar generation then the powerwall level will suffer, we had a bad day yesterday for generation

author: nainaztengra
title: Beauty of Oman - Visual story - Part 1
text:
I have always mentioned how beautiful the Country of Oman is. It is a country with a blend of tradition, culture and natural beauty. It's also called the hidden gem of Middle East. Unlike other Middle East countries where you see tall sky-scrappers here in Oman you will not find it. Which retains the beauty of this place, offering scenic views from any part of the city. Oman is also famous for its port businesses since it has a long coastline running through the country. Right from the 6th century Oman has been an important trading hub connecting between the East and West.

This Country welcomes people from all over the world with job opportunities to offer. Here it's like truly living multi-cultural.

Here are some pictures of Oman showcasing its beauty. I love the beach life of Oman and today I am going to focus on that.

author: kiwiscanfly
title: Day 🔟Gold Coast Australia
text:
Today has been my last day of work here in Queensland Australia, it was short day at work but still a good one. I think i have said before this would have to be my fzvorite worksite to visit ue to the people that work there, so friendly and lots of Kiwi's work there as well. Plus the Gold Coast is my favorite spot to visit in Australia so it really is a holiday first with some work chucked in. Spent my last day lying ion the sun loungers by the pool before i had a lovely swim to cool off in the pool. ![20260204_122736.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmX1sGiQgR2EnCjA3yt3XGgQ8MRnmhTyPABaFEnMW1YLdo/20260204_122736.jpg) I have also done all of my laundry and folded it nicely, its good t be able to go home with clothes clean so i can just put them away when i get home.
![20260211_101600.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmW7YbqdWFXvxNfiwGR6e68jFXCHRUsAxjK2QqsACHi6Wq/20260211_101600.jpg)
![20260211_122417.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmSXVeqf7hCYajcGjPZkXAicRakLn5a2Ypp5JQ6oc4wrdF/20260211_122417.jpg)
Today is also the last day i get to grab anything the Family like, all of this stuff is not available in New Zealand so why not.
![20260211_132808.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmejEAz1zhFn1HqFDXKsZEiFsNXvMRYWGfJ5PjDM5tT19n/20260211_132808.jpg)
![20260211_132831.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmZuWJgEPyWLoSzLW1QjsuQ9EBAQFtgwuVBevcBagnR2ae/20260211_132831.jpg)
Everyone loves the Pods and these were on special for $5 AUS, actually i did see these again in NZ but they were $10 a bag! ![20260211_132755.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmPdmXVcb5nREe2xi3xfXPBUG7rVeG7ApaRiiGYVM6Afh9/20260211_132755.jpg) When i was packing for this trip i packed very very lightly as i wouldn't need any warm clothing at all, plus having a washing machine in my room meant i could wash my clothes daily if i chose too. My plan also included being able to fit all of my clothes/laptop's etc into my backpack, this would leave my small suitcase just for stuff i brought. ![20260211_133018.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcqAeEP6eueqvFQgL36jcPrgTVNRwcmDcurT3dPvP57w3/20260211_133018.jpg) I am pretty much all packed and ready to go and have brought everything i wanted, now i just chill out and wait until tomorrow morning when i drive an hour's north to Brisbane Airport - then its back to Wellington. This Kiwi hopes you have enjoyed seeing a bit of the Gold Coast while i have been here. *all pics are mine* *smudge approves this post* # I am @kiwiscanfly 🥝🐔 ![20260204_124302.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQsqq18aKKHeZPWAG8WKBt88saFDWnkHks97SexcBvXiK/20260204_124302.jpg)

author: nainaztengra
title: 10 years of expat life
text:
It's been 10 years living as an expat in Oman. I always wanted to live out of Country for a few years and hence mean hubby were looking out for opportunities and then this came. But our target was never this long. We thought we would stay for 6 to 8 years and then move back to India. This was always the thought process. But then as time went by, we started settling very comfortably in Oman and made a good life here. Though I miss India a lot and always want to keep going back to my land, but then I must agree that I have got equally fond of Muscat also.

At this point of time, it is confusing for me whether to go back permanently or not, because I like this mix of life between Mumbai and Muscat. It is giving me the best of both worlds. I have a good social life in both the places. Both the places offer me work opportunities. Quality of life is better here in Muscat in terms of pollution and health, this is also one reason why I like here. Air is clean and infrastructure is super. Population is less here so there are open and clean places to move around. I love the mountain and sea life of Muscat. There are lot of benefits, so I think why not continue till I can. But that's for sure I am not going to settle here. May be a couple of more years and then I will take a decision to move back to Mumbai, because the most important, family is there, which is above everything for me.

![20171118_140811.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQma38d2zHApTiScFqq5z9x9iS9RuQWCwpdAS2FMXxNAWgT/20171118_140811.jpg)

While it all looks good, still at the end of the day expat life is not very colorful also, there are pros and cons of everything. There is always a threat of job security and instability. And too many rules to follow. That time I feel living in our own Country is better, we can live freely and not under constant monitoring. Anyways let's see how many more years I can spend here in Muscat.

***Thank you for visiting my blog.*** 👼🏻👼🏻💖💖🌹🌺🌸

author: nainaztengra
title: Adjustments become a habit
text:
This happens very commonly in a family, where sometimes one person keeps adjusting to the needs of other people in the home. And these adjustments are majorly done by the women of the home. But what happens eventually is that these adjustments are taken for granted and they become a habit. Adjustments are also another name for sacrifices that the person makes. If they happen too regularly they will go unnoticed and not be valued also.

It becomes unfair for one person to keep adjusting in the family. The women will make adjustments for her husband, her children, her in-laws, parents, everyone and she is expected to do this without ever complaining. Is this really fair. In Indian households it is very common. No matter how much ever we talk about empowerment and freedom, still in some areas of life it has become an unsaid rule that the woman has to adjust.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdU6g73ahtgkytbZBs3uYNUZeU4qC66A9D6rXY3FYPiBZ/image.png)
[Source](https://pixabay.com/vectors/plant-leaves-green-resistance-148601/)

And when it happens very regularly, it just becomes a habit even without realization and the person who keeps making adjustments sets into this mode of sacrificing all the time. There are so many women who I hear saying, So what. But then I tell them, why it's you all the time. Sometimes even a leisure thing to do is a necessity and to give up on that for adjusting other things may not be right, even if it is for leisure. Important things will keep coming up daily in a family and every single person of the family need to make adjustments and just not one person.

Understanding other people's need and taking care of them is good but it should not come at the cost of your mental health where you feel disheartened and unhappy. One should not forget to nurture their own needs while taking care of others.

***Thank you for visiting my blog.*** 👼🏻👼🏻💖💖🌹🌺🌸

![](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmexfm7Y3xHUSDtYLX2gywbcyG8mt4FdQ6Uop8pxAcKw5L/image.png)

author: kiwiscanfly
title: Day's 8️⃣& 9️⃣Gold Coast Australia
text:
The last 2 days here on the Gold Coast just been more of the same, work eat and go for a walk or 2. On the work front i am so far ahead of schedule my boss said yesterday "see if you can get a flight home on the Wednesday a day earlier than scheduled", i tried but there were no direct flights to Wellington - well there was but that was with Air NZ at a cost of $679 and that would mean forgoing my existing ticket with Qantas on Thursday. When i mentioned i could get a change of flight he said have an early day on Wednesday, so tomorrow will most probably be 5-6 hours max. ![20260206_102755.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmd9mmur5CFCoGSfdca9SFeL8MC5q1WGxpeoEzT4t7LSkE/20260206_102755.jpg) Last night for dinner i got a Roast Chicken and some Potato salad which i demolished the lot, it makes a nice change to what i have been having a no cooked required.
![20260209_151000.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQLxez83jMZsyc6Y7udN96DwLGP6nfekzv9B7ZtTZ8Ass/20260209_151000.jpg)
![20260209_151333.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmcTLKzfNtfKQK7AWeTKPjR25vDLt1cbxrsdKEZN9rXHe7/20260209_151333.jpg)
The guy at the Worksite who is effectively my minder got me a can of Root beer yesterday, we had been talking about a few days ago and i had it with my Chicken and salad last night. It's got a unique smell and tastes pretty nice, sort of tastes like sarsaparilla with a combination of an earthy taste. At least i can say i have tried it now. ![20260209_152959.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTfuRniTpScGVpydagDrhVeusHc3gThr6JxkaSoVdJ6Xq/20260209_152959.jpg) Tonight for dinner i am having some Cracked pepper Salmon with an avocado and some eggs, i have 9 eggs left so i may have 3 or 4 for dinner. ![20260210_133118.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmVkAEa1Y5RCnbZwprorJm1puL6DTmjAyTE8y1E4UtwfkE/20260210_133118.jpg) 🛫✈️🛬🛄🌏🛫✈️🛬🛄🌏 My Flight on Thursday leaves Brisbane at 8.50am and gets back to Wellington around 3.20pm, remembering i gain 3 hours time so you can say i am a time traveler LOL. I get to fly on a brand new Qantas A220 which they are using on this route, i don't really care what plane it is as long as it gets me there. ![Screenshot_20260210_140148_Qantas.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmP4wcfVXnKmYFFKA7CsDccDA8Quh6Hw7iBK67AosUhV1n/Screenshot_20260210_140148_Qantas.jpg) I have really enjoyed this trip and i really like doing the worksite as everyone is friendly and its not a huge site, plus i get to stay in my favorite place the Gold Coast. I am off to relax now and enjoy my 2nd to last day here, thanks for reading my post. *all pics are mine* *smudge approves this post* # I am @kiwiscanfly 🥝🐔 ![20260205_165454.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmXWQFmm9AFXwuNacupx8kyWY9LqEdssGuotLvwnXYkR7T/20260205_165454.jpg)

author: reetuahlawat
title: Sunflower is a symbol of beauty
text:
<center>mobile  Photography</center>


![IMG_20250831_105045400.jpg](https://img.blurt.world/blurtimage/reetuahlawat/b0960ae537ffe2e56110490064c446ef920cca39.jpg)

The sunflower is a beautiful, vibrant, and energetic flower, known worldwide for its medicinal properties, beauty, and function. Scientifically, it is called Helianthus annuus. This flower is famous for its unique characteristic—as its name suggests, it follows the sun.

The sunflower plant is tall and sturdy. Its stem is long and rough, bearing large, dark green leaves. The flower's most distinctive features are its center and petals. The center of the flower is flat and dark brown or black, containing thousands of tiny flowers (florets) that later develop into seeds. Surrounding it are long, golden-yellow petals, giving it a miniature sun-like appearance. Its most distinctive feature is heliotropism. When young, the flower faces east in the morning and rotates westward with the sun's movement throughout the day. At night, it turns back eastward.
![IMG_20250831_105034655.jpg](https://img.blurt.world/blurtimage/reetuahlawat/2745bf3cad9be9498371fe4adf8083ffeedda7ff.jpg)


![IMG_20250831_105024962.jpg](https://img.blurt.world/blurtimage/reetuahlawat/783e4b687471ba80b6b5826982ae3351f2cca2bb.jpg)

author: davidchristain
title: What Planning Steps Reduce Cracking in Outdoor Hardscapes?
text: Thorough planning significantly reduces cracking risks. [Masonry contractors in New Jersey](https://njmasonrypavers.com/) assess ground movement and moisture exposure before construction begins. Base layers must be compacted evenly to support stone or pavers. Concrete pool deck contractors near me install control joints that allow slight expansion without surface damage. Reinforcement grids and proper curing techniques also limit shrinkage cracks. Careful scheduling during moderate weather conditions ensures materials set correctly. Professional oversight during every phase keeps outdoor hardscapes smooth, level, and resilient.

author: lixue
title: The Unstoppable Blaze: Why Every Gardener Needs Tithonia
text:
If you’re searching for a flower that delivers pure, unadulterated drama with the ease of a favorite old sweater, look no further than Tithonia. Commonly known as the Mexican Sunflower, this radiant annual is not a delicate daisy but a bold, blazing comet in the garden. 

With large, single or double blooms in shades of fiery orange, deep red, and brilliant yellow, Tithonia doesn’t whisper—it sings a vibrant, sun-drenched song from midsummer until the first frost.

Native to Mexico and Central America, Tithonia rotundifolia is the star of the show. It’s an unfussy, heat-loving powerhouse that thrives in the very conditions that wilt more refined blooms. Full sun, poor to average soil, and drought are not insults to Tithonia; they are invitations.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQEN2m2vqJdq7XxiApmoMNfVtEjCLocbqjsisgFt8Xkk4/image.png)


 This is the plant you tuck into that dry, baked patch by the mailbox or the back of a border where nothing else seems to compete. It grows tall and sturdy, often reaching 5-6 feet, with a somewhat shrubby, coarse texture that adds wonderful architectural contrast to finer-leaved plants like cosmos or cleome.

But Tithonia’s greatest magic is its irresistible magnetism for pollinators. The large, shallow flowers are perfectly designed for butterflies, and you’ll often find a kaleidoscope of monarchs, swallowtails, and painted ladies flocking to its nectar-rich discs. Bees adore it too, making it a cornerstone for any wildlife or pollinator garden. To have Tithonia blooming is to host a continuous, fluttery celebration of life.

Growing Tithonia is wonderfully simple:

* Sow seeds directly in the ground after the last frost. They germinate readily.
* Give them space. Plant 18-24 inches apart for good air circulation.
* Water deeply but infrequently. They are incredibly drought-tolerant once established.
* Deadhead spent blooms to encourage a constant flush of new flowers.
* Stake the tallest varieties if they’re in a windy spot.

In a world of pastel perfection, Tithonia is a courageous splash of joy. It represents resilience, abundance, and raw, sunny optimism. It’s the flower that proves the most spectacular show can come from the least demanding performer. Plant a packet of seeds this spring, and let your garden be lit with its unforgettable, fiery glow.

***

author: thevpnboss
title: Standing Accountable for Your Decisions and Goals
text:
There is something strangely powerful about saying: *“This is on me.”*

Not in a self-blaming, heavy, shame-filled way. But in a grounded, calm, grown-up way. The kind of ownership that doesn’t look for excuses, doesn’t point fingers, and doesn’t wait for perfect circumstances. Just a quiet decision: **I choose this. I’m responsible for this.**

Accountability is uncomfortable because it removes our favorite escape routes. If your goals aren’t moving forward, you can’t blame timing, the market, your childhood, your boss, your spouse, or Mercury being in retrograde. You have to look in the mirror and ask: *What did I actually do? What did I avoid?*

That’s not punishment. That’s power.

When you take ownership of your decisions, something shifts. You stop being a passenger in your own life. You stop reacting. You start steering.

## Goals Without Accountability Are Just Wishes

Everyone has goals. Fewer people have systems. Even fewer have accountability.

Saying “I want to get fit” is easy. Showing up when it’s raining and you’re tired is different.  
Saying “I want to grow my business” is easy. Doing the uncomfortable calls and the boring backend work is different.  
Saying “I want a better relationship” is easy. Admitting your own mistakes and changing behavior? That’s real work.

Accountability means tracking what you said you would do — and whether you actually did it. It means reviewing your week and asking uncomfortable but honest questions:

- Did I prioritize what I claim is important?
- Where did I procrastinate?
- What patterns are repeating?
- What excuse did I use this time?

The answers might sting a little. Good. That’s growth knocking.

## The Freedom of Ownership

Here’s the paradox: accountability feels heavy at first, but it creates freedom.

If your results are someone else’s fault, you’re stuck waiting for them to change.  
If your results are your responsibility, you can change them.

That’s liberating.

When you fully own your decisions, you also own the possibility of improvement. You stop waiting for permission. You stop waiting for motivation. You start building discipline.

And discipline isn’t harsh. It’s alignment between what you say matters and what you actually do.

## Practical Ways to Stay Accountable

You don’t need a dramatic life overhaul. Start simple.

1. **Write down your goals.** Vague intentions disappear. Written goals stare back at you.
2. **Break them into weekly actions.** Goals live in daily habits.
3. **Track your actions, not just outcomes.** Did you show up?
4. **Review weekly.** No drama. Just data.
5. **Own your misses.** No excuses. Just adjustments.

If you failed this week, good. That means you’re trying. Now adjust.

Accountability isn’t about being perfect. It’s about being honest. And honesty builds momentum.

At the end of the day, you are living the consequences of your decisions — good or bad. That’s not a threat. It’s an invitation.

Stand tall. Own it. Adjust when needed. Move forward.

Your future self will thank you.

author: nainaztengra
title: Saving Children from Predators
text:
Sometimes the world feels very unreal, for all that keeps happening around and you wonder is this really life. Yesterday I binged watched the Jeffery Epstein files on Netflix, I felt very angry at him and at the same time I also felt angry at those girls who could not draw a line and willingly went into him. But then later on I thought, is my anger towards them justified? Sometimes the situation can become so helpless for some people. And then there are predators like him in the society who do not leave any chance to take advantage of other people's vulnerability.

What type of a human he was. I believe that people like him have lost their souls, because if they would have, they would not do such inhuman things. His death may always remain a mystery. After watching the documentary in the end, I felt that possibly he may have not died and staged it all. For people like him, it may be easy to do such things. After all money power speaks and he had contacts of all the possible influential people of the world. Or it is also very much possible that the same influential people may have got him killed. God only knows what is the truth behind all of this.

In a way good that a person like him is no more around else he would have continued doing his atrocious acts with underage children. In a society where the children must be protected, he left no chance to exploit them. And to think of it he was only full of lust who must have only kept thinking of sex all the time. Quiet a devil like trait I can say. We say that in the end justice is always served, but I feel that in that whole process the amount of damage that happens may sometimes become irreparable and even with the justice it may not help heal. It can become a life long scar.

![image.png](https://img.blurt.world/blurtimage/nainaztengra/648fe82afed67f3c767df6110ba3924e08e01809.png)
[Source](https://pixabay.com/vectors/harassment-workplace-incident-man-4499303/)

Pedophile racket is big beyond imagination in every society. Even in common households. In our remand home there are so many underage girls who have been physically abused by their own family members and their parents even after having knowledge of it would not want to do anything because of the fear of shame that they may have to go through in the society. It does not only happen in backward class, rather I have seen it happening in rich people's families also. As a teenager once, one of my relative tried to abuse me, but my dad had taught me about these things and I could save myself from him. He did not have too much guts so he made a try and when I tried to make a scene he immediately distanced out, and acted innocent, as if he was only trying to cuddle me in a warm family way.

A pervert is always around in the society. With him, it does not end.  There are so many Jeffery Epstein's in the world, it's just that majority of them are operating under cover. Possibly this documentary will give courage to a lot of underage girls if they are facing any such situation to come out and speak and get such people behind bars.

 ***Thank you for visiting my blog.*** 👼🏻👼🏻💖💖🌹🌺🌸

author: mike15
title: Ice Plant Flowers: A Chilly‑Cool Addition to Your Garden
text:
![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmP3Cs7q5NFCRzP71ygabqiXCzJZVeHCV8i3Sw53MzLhRV/image.png)

When it comes to low‑maintenance, eye‑catching groundcovers, the ice plant (genus Delosperma) is hard to beat. Native to the arid regions of South Africa, these succulent perennials thrive on sunshine, drought, and a touch of neglect—making them perfect for both seasoned gardeners and busy beginners. 

Yet beyond their rugged reputation, ice plants deliver a spectacular floral show that can transform any garden, balcony, or container into a bright, almost other‑worldly space.

What Makes Ice Plants Special?

Ice plants are best known for their crystallized, glistening foliage. Tiny, water‑filled cells called papillae cover each leaf, reflecting light much like a layer of frost. In full sun, the leaves sparkle, earning the plant its whimsical name. 

When the blossoms appear—typically in late spring through early autumn—the contrast is striking. Flowers burst in vivid shades of pink, orange, yellow, and even deep magenta, each opening in the morning and fading by evening, only to be replaced by a fresh set the next day.

Choosing the Right Variety

There are hundreds of Delosperma cultivars, but a few stand out for garden use:

Variety	Flower |Color	|Height	|Notable Feature
-|-|-|-
‘Fireworks’	|Fiery orange‑red	|6–8 in (15–20 cm)	|Compact, ideal for rock gardens
‘Little Miss Sunshine’	|Bright yellow	|4–5 in (10–12 cm)	|Small leaves, perfect for containers
‘Ruby Frost’	|Deep magenta	|8–10 in (20–25 cm)	|Drought‑tolerant, spreads quickly
‘Ice Star’	|White with pink edges	|6–9 in (15–23 cm)	|Soft pastel hue, great for edging

When selecting a cultivar, consider the scale of your planting area, the color palette of surrounding flora, and how aggressively you want the plant to spread.

Planting & Care Basics

Sunlight: Ice plants crave full sun (at least 6 hours daily). Insufficient light leads to leggier growth and fewer blooms.
Soil: Well‑draining soil is non‑negotiable. A mix of sand, perlite, and a modest amount of compost works wonders. In heavy clay, add coarse grit or plant in raised beds.

Water: Water deeply but sparingly. Once established (usually after the first growing season), they survive on rainfall alone in most climates. Over‑watering is the single biggest cause of root rot.

Spacing: Plant 8–12 in (20–30 cm) apart to give each clump room to expand. They spread by both runners and leaf cuttings, creating a dense mat that suppresses weeds.

Winter Protection: In USDA zones 8‑9 and warmer, ice plants are evergreen. In colder zones (≤ 7), treat them as annuals or mulch heavily and move containers indoors.

Propagation Made Easy

Ice plants are a propagation dream. In early spring, gently pull a healthy leaf, let the cut end dry for a day (to form a callus), then place it on a moist, well‑draining substrate. Within a few weeks, tiny rosettes emerge, ready for transplanting. Alternatively, divide mature clumps in the fall and re‑plant the sections.

Design Ideas

Rock Gardens: Their low, spreading habit fills gaps between stones, while the bright flowers provide seasonal fireworks.
Container Displays: Pair a deep‑pot orange ‘Fireworks’ with silver foliage succulents for a bold contrast.
Pathway Edging: Use the compact ‘Little Miss Sunshine’ along walkways for a cheery, low‑maintenance border.
Living Walls: Their shallow root system makes them suitable for vertical planters, turning bare fences into living art.

A Final Word

Ice plant flowers bring more than just color—they offer a resilient, low‑effort solution to those sunny spots that other plants often ignore. Their glossy leaves, endless bloom cycles, and effortless propagation make them a garden staple for anyone seeking a splash of summer vibrancy year after year. 

So, grab a tray of seedlings, find a sun‑kissed corner, and let the frost‑kissed foliage do the talking. Your garden (and your neighbors) will thank you.

***

author: thevpnboss
title: Drawing Strength from God – When He Is Your Source
text:
There are seasons in life when your own strength simply isn’t enough. You can drink the coffee, make the plans, organize the calendar, smile for people… and still feel empty inside. The Bible never denies that weakness is real. In fact, it leans into it.

“God is our refuge and strength, an ever-present help in trouble.” (Psalm 46:1)

Notice something important: it doesn’t say God *gives* strength occasionally. It says He *is* our strength. That changes everything. If He is the source, then you are not required to manufacture power from within yourself.

### 1. Admit Your Weakness

The first step to drawing strength from God is honesty. The Apostle Paul writes:

“My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.” (2 Corinthians 12:9)

Weakness is not a disqualification. It is an entry point. When you admit, “I cannot carry this alone,” you create space for God’s power to work.

### 2. Stay Connected to the Source

Jesus says in John 15:5:

“I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, you will bear much fruit; apart from me you can do nothing.”

Branches don’t strain to produce fruit. They simply stay connected. Drawing strength from God is not about trying harder; it’s about staying close. Prayer, Scripture, worship, silence — these are not religious duties. They are lifelines.

### 3. Exchange Your Burdens

Isaiah 40:31 offers one of the most powerful promises in Scripture:

“But those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles.”

Renewal implies exchange. You bring Him your anxiety, fear, exhaustion. He gives you peace, courage, endurance. It may not always feel dramatic. Sometimes strength comes quietly — the ability to take one more step, make one more loving choice, endure one more day.

### 4. Remember Who Fights for You

Exodus 14:14 says, “The Lord will fight for you; you need only to be still.”

Strength in God does not always look like action. Sometimes it looks like trust. Sometimes it looks like resting when you would normally panic.

When God is your source, you are no longer a battery that must constantly recharge yourself. You are connected to an endless current. And that current does not run dry.

Draw near. Ask honestly. Stay connected. Let Him be your strength.

author: companions
title: My Foody Day at Home:  Coconut Rice and delicious Soup
text:
There’s a quiet magic to the rhythm of a day spent in the kitchen, especially when the menu is a comforting bowl of coconut rice paired with a hearty pot of Afang soup. For me, this duo isn’t just a meal; it’s a ritual that ties together family memories, cultural pride, and the simple joy of creating something delicious from scratch.

5:30 am – The First Light

The day begins before the sun has fully risen. I love this early hour because the house is still, the only sound being the soft hum of the fridge. I head straight to the pantry, pull out the bag of long‑grain rice, and give it a quick rinse. As the water runs clear, I can already smell the faint, nutty aroma of the grains—a promise of the creamy coconut flavor that’s about to unfold.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmTMmuf3fZyEhDapaqRu4VqyNtJwEMUBj2WGLxyW5kP2vC/image.png)

6:00 am – Coconut Prep

A fresh coconut is the star of today’s rice. I crack it open with a sturdy hammer, careful not to waste any of the tender white flesh. Once the meat is scraped out, I blend it with a splash of warm water, then strain it through a fine muslin cloth. The resulting milky liquid pools into my pot, where I’ll toast the rice.

I heat a generous knob of palm oil, letting it shimmer before adding the rinsed rice. The grains sizzle, coating themselves in oil, and the kitchen fills with a faint, caramelized scent. I pour the coconut milk in, add a pinch of salt, and let the mixture come to a gentle boil. Then the lid goes on, and the rice steams away, turning fluffy and fragrant.

7:30 am – The Soul of Afang Soup

While the rice is doing its quiet work, I turn my attention to Afang soup—a West African classic that never fails to warm both body and spirit. The base is a blend of palm oil, smoked fish, and assorted meats (usually beef and shaki). I start by sautéing diced onions and minced garlic in a generous splash of palm oil until they turn translucent and sweet.

Next, I add the smoked fish and meat, letting them brown and release their smoky essence. A spoonful of ground crayfish follows, intensifying the umami depth. Then comes the star ingredient: Afang leaves (also known as Okazi). I wash them meticulously, chop them roughly, and stir them into the pot, watching the bright green mingle with the rich, reddish broth.

To balance the flavors, I toss in diced tomatoes, a splash of fermented locust beans (iru), and a pinch of ground pepper. The soup simmers gently for about twenty minutes, allowing the leaves to soften and the flavors to meld. A final sprinkle of chopped garden eggs and a dash of salt bring everything into harmony.

8:15 am – The Grand Finale

The rice is perfectly cooked—each grain separated, glossy with coconut milk, and subtly sweet. I fluff it with a fork, then spoon a generous serving onto a plate, nestling a ladleful of Afang soup beside it. The steam rises, carrying the tropical scent of coconut and the earthy fragrance of the leaves.

I sit at my kitchen table, a steaming bowl before me, and take a moment to appreciate the simple pleasure of a homemade meal. The coconut rice provides a creamy, slightly sweet base that tempers the robust, peppery notes of the soup. Together, they tell a story of tradition, love, and the quiet satisfaction of a day well spent.

Why This Meal Matters

Cooking coconut rice and Afang soup isn’t just about feeding the body; it’s a daily affirmation of my roots and the people who taught me these recipes. Each stir, each pinch of spice, is a conversation with the past, a celebration of the present, and a hope for future generations to enjoy the same comforting flavors.

So, the next time you’re looking for a “normal” day that feels a little extraordinary, try your hand at this duo. Let the aroma fill your home, and let the rhythm of the kitchen remind you that sometimes the simplest meals are the most profound. Bon appétit!

***

author: soulsdetour
title: The Heritage
text:
I remember when I was little, my grandparents, due to some peculiar, local, probably communist tradition, were "related" to a local communist family, I mean, they were godparents to their child or something like that, I don't remember what their relationship was exactly, nor do I know why this was inflicted on everyone from both sides.
Because every time these families had to get together for some occasion, that gathering ended in arguments, swearing, and insults, I mean - my grandfather to the man from that family, and this happened every single time, despite warnings to my grandfather from everyone in the family before. He just couldn't help it. And every time the evening ended in this ugly way.

At the time I thought that was very stupid. That one had to bury one's political beliefs for at least one evening in order to have peace and understanding for at least one evening. But I don't think so anymore either. I mean, now I know that it is just not possible. Because of a simple incident in my life in recent months. 


![IMG_20260215_131426.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdq1ZNGftdtA38dXvKz5eoi2tRG1fJTsS79qEr71hwTA3/IMG_20260215_131426.jpg)


You know, I was admitted to the hospital last year to have tests done. Before I went there, there was also this worry about what kind of person I would be in the same room with. Rather, how many and what kind of women I would be in the same room with.

In Bulgaria, there is always a chance that you will come across a person who does not observe even basic hygiene and smells. This is more common in men, but my God, I have encountered it in women too. In addition, it is very possible that the person could also turn out to be a thief and what not. 
While I was preparing for my admission to the hospital and searching the internet for information on what I should take with me, I came across a hospital website that insisted that no valuables be brought into the hospital, and that you should always carry your money, phone, and documents with you everywhere. All of this, on the hospital's official website, suggests that thefts often occur at this hospital, either by patients or by staff. And this is probably not an isolated case only there. 
So I was genuinely relieved when, in the room they put me in, I saw a rather good-looking, even still pretty, clean and tidy 70-year-old woman who wore light makeup, was well-dressed and, in short, completely different from at least 80% of women her age, the so-called typical retirees in the country. 
Why this was so, I would find out later.


![IMG_20260215_125216.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdGka5WpAb4zZDY3tdV1RFW61kGHjeRmGHeDFpiT4FTvD/IMG_20260215_125216.jpg)

 
But our first contact, our initial, quite ordinary conversation, ended in tension. 
So I, maybe 15 minutes into our conversation, I was kind of forced to ask her what her political beliefs were - left or right, without even mentioning names of parties, in order to be as tactful as possible. And when I got the obvious answer straight out - leftist beliefs, I simply changed the direction of our conversations, or simply paid more attention to what I said, how I said it, and how I expressed myself. So that there would be no more tension. Because I had to spend three whole days with this woman. Without tension.

This woman, so good-looking and obviously well-off, unlike the average Bulgarian pensioner, had been the director of the local branch of an important government agency in the city she lived in, for many years. She, as she later told me, came from an uneducated working-class family from some remote village, as one would expect the communist electorate to be and the origin of the communist party supporters who later rose to power. Her husband, also from uneducated peasants, was also some kind of head of a unit in the local police department. As are also expected to be the employees of the police authority in the country, and even more so - the directors. Before or now, it does not matter, because everything is the same today, just as it was before. 


![IMG_20260215_125228.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdBLYdhscx7N9XmcsmYpvjThj31a1MCumK22QKw3txR9r/IMG_20260215_125228.jpg)


And don't think that we talked about politics - during that first moment of our meeting. It is wrong to think that in this truly polarized country, where people have always been and continue to be, so extremely divided from each other, people are always talking about politics. And with this sole purpose - to quarrel, to have a scandal. 

We talked about culture, about poetry, about history, about architecture. 
It all started with the question - where do you live? And then we talked about the poets inevitably associated with these cities. Then about architecture and history. And all of this, of course, was intertwined with the miserable communist past of our country. In which one part, these educated, intelligent, moral and reasonable people were trampled and repressed, imprisoned and killed, while the uneducated representatives of the working class, the immoral but obedient supporters of the Party, flourished, were placed in leadership positions, looked after, received a lot of money and so on.


![IMG_20260215_125156.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUWV5mNv2K7bKiL74aSyu6AcU7b7T6TTZMeZ9DzXqYgaf/IMG_20260215_125156.jpg)


My grandfather was an educated man, he was a luminary in medicine in his time, but he had a terribly unhappy life, made deliberately unhappy by the authorities who hindered him at every step of his life. Denunciations were written about him until the last day of his life by an incompetent doctor, a member of the communist party, who lived in the same village. 
The man with whom he was supposed to spend traditional family dinners was also an uneducated member of the communist party with an easy life. I don't know much about him. But I know that other members of the Party in this village, uneducated, who had only finished the third grade of school, were carefully looked after, placed, if not in leadership positions, then in very peaceful positions in which they could spend their days carefree until retirement. 


![IMG_20260215_131407.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmU27rHaCFdXV7WZ3suDCGkpjGmpfLUc4rfYtmQnGMWP1Q/IMG_20260215_131407.jpg)


My other grandfather, as an educated and moral man (he was a teacher), was even sent to a concentration camp because he refused to cooperate with the authorities in vile deeds. His life later also went from difficulty to difficulty, from obstacle to obstacle. (Why did I think that my life would be easy then after all this, with such an inheritance?) 

And both of my grandfathers didn't live long. One died at 77, the other didn't even make it to 70. And it wasn't because they were sick. I mean, they were sick because they were unhappy, because their lives had gone by so unhappily. Both of them seemed to have passed away with "broken hearts," the illnesses they died from showed....


And since this post has become so long again, I think I will finish my thought in the next one and tell you exactly what I mean by writing all this.

|Thank you for your time! Copyright:|@soulsdetour|
|-|-|
|![steem.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNoCcaa3Q9eA2LbZYyWJtaZjdT7trhQqHhDDck3tMBH8X/Steem.jpg) |Soul's Detour is a project started by me years ago when I had a blog about historical and not so popular tourist destinations in Eastern Belgium, West Germany and Luxembourg. Nowadays, this blog no longer exists, but I'm still here - passionate about architecture, art and mysteries and eager to share my discoveries and point of view with you. 
Personally, I am a sensitive soul with a strong sense of justice. 
Traveling and photography are my greatest passions. 
Sounds trivial to you? 
No, it's not trivial. Because I still love to travel to not so famous destinations.🗺️
Of course, the current situation does not allow me to do this, but I still find a way to satisfy my hunger for knowledge, new places, beauty and art.
Sometimes you can find the most amazing things even in the backyard of your house.😊🧐🧭|

author: meta-verse
title: A sack of lentils
text:
Greetings everyone! Hope everybody is doing well. I'm much better. Today I bring you another new blog. I always share good content with you and I like these a lot. Photography can be my hobby or profession. Every now and then I do a lot of photography. In between, I share with you some photography. I hope you and all of you like that photography. Anyway, let me know in the comments.
![1000062210.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWXzz2bwyCtTLUK5VwEnskp1UMBciqTgnm2L2aENh3WTB/1000062210.jpg) ![1000062211.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNxeZ3kxGDnYfwv1W4z3ib3frfSrbYx8sXmer1QiRWXci/1000062211.jpg) ![1000062213.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmfY5PMxfiLuicvcZjWiGXFupL9Ec1hLszi37n5edjJZsH/1000062213.jpg) ![1000062212.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUF57y51G9nmMyENde2EGzeJoryCHnpCu36dKLZkWDRxj/1000062212.jpg) A sack of lentils is kept here. To cook the lentils, they are soaked nicely. In the morning, these lentils are cooked. And to cook these lentils, you have to keep them properly in the morning, because these lentils take quite some time to soak in water and need time to be cooked. The lentils here are a very preferred kind of lentil that is quite delicious to eat. It can be cooked in several ways. It can be cooked as dal, and it can be cooked as bharta. However someone finds this lentil tasty, they can cook it that way. It is a very nice food. If you want, you can also cook it nicely and eat it. I took some pictures, and now I have shared those pictures with you.

text:

author: denver23
title: Khichuri
text:
**Hello steemit family,** how are you all, I am also fine with your prayers, Alhamdulillah, I wish peace to all.  In daily life we ​​do different things and also we do photography and memories. That's why I share with you some photography everyday.  Hope you like these photographs.


![1000062292.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmUUrEQfm4cFPt3d8CokHa18WdNkTsR5KbNZCLvBePZmRB/1000062292.jpg)

![1000062293.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmV6rAajh4amhW22cM9JYH91hp21AaWCrddPkK36Rj7Exe/1000062293.jpg)

![1000062294.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmWnCsR8dgDzTfrLBCSh9ueATEQfTx1oUkFc1drDQCkjyj/1000062294.jpg)

![1000062295.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmdZTmA1rDDocffDDdXwqMVdB1abvYgpCXLfankNydrQcy/1000062295.jpg)





Khichuri has been cooked in a large pot for eating. Here, a lot of khichuri has been prepared, arranged for about 150 people. Vegetable khichuri has been cooked so that everyone can enjoy eating it. The khichuri has been cooked nicely, then taken down and kept properly to make it ready for eating. The khichuri is very tasty and delicious, and the vegetables inside are enjoyable to eat. 


Meat has not been added here because many women do not like meat, which is why only vegetable khichuri has been cooked. Everyone likes vegetable khichuri very much, which is why it has been served. The vegetable khichuri with eggs is delicious and tasty. I have taken some pictures of the vegetable khichuri, which I am now sharing with you.



## <center>TAKE LOVE @DENVER23</center>

title: Pupha Mahanatee Garden at Queen Sirikit Park, Bangkok
text:
https://i.postimg.cc/85cQLYqN/Pupha-Mahanatee-Garden18.jpg

Queen Sirikit Park has a very large area which is divided into many sections.  One of the interesting sections is “Pupha Mahanatee Garden”.  (Pupha means mountain or hill, Mahanatee means river or lake or pond)  

Pupha Mahanatee Garden provides a beautiful landscape with a relaxing spot for walking, jogging or admiring gorgeous flowers with various species.
