author: welshstacker
title: Still life in this old dog yet...
text:
<center>![Screenshot_20260214_221036_Instagram.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmW3XhiwRS2FDgtiR2QNRr16MMgth4SgzdnJk3xSmekcoq/Screenshot_20260214_221036_Instagram.jpg)</center>


While most of you, i assume, were spending today celebrating Valentines day with your loved ones, i was doing something else i love.

With the 6 nations rugby tournament underway, and Wales due to play France tomorrow, my local village side hosted a touring "veterans" rugby team, for those of us 45yrs and older.

Was a great day playing with some of my old team mates, some i haven't seen or played with innover 15 years. With me being 45, i was onenof the younger men playing today. So you can imagine the state we were all in by the end...

We managed to pull off a 25 - 12 victory, and as i left the rugby club to head home, the French tourist were well into the drinking games. It looked to be heading to be a great night.

author: capetown01
title: A cruel picture of the neglect of skinny street dogs.
text: ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmNrxgjiwauRHbwWvuHoxNaVR5NkRcjgEYP1NqbGnDomxy/image.png) ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmepKiFeHJx5QKAjWQXaXsrePrSJgeHy4P3B3wEYRMvvY6/image.png) The skinny street dog is a silent reflection of our society. It can be seen wandering around in search of food on the dusty side of the road, next to the dustbin or on the corner of a busy road. Its rib bones are clearly visible, its eyes are tired and its body is malnourished - every step it takes tells the story of its struggle for survival. Such puppies do not get a safe shelter from birth. Limited mother's milk, lack of adequate food and various diseases hinder their physical growth. Many times people chase them away, and if someone kindly gives them a little food, they look at it with grateful eyes. But due to irregular food and lack of treatment, they quickly weaken. We need a collective effort to solve this problem. It is very important to provide regular food and clean water to the street dogs in the neighborhood, and to provide vaccinations and treatment through local animal welfare organizations. Implementing a sterilization program will also reduce uncontrolled breeding. A little compassion and responsible behavior can change the life of a skinny puppy. Only a humane approach can ensure a safe and healthy future for these helpless animals.

author: saleh-sarker
title: Teddy bears are tender childhood companions.
text: ![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQ8yqJ3EkN3uBys2U8ABCCqVcYaLARHojZfCw2zeqBaGk/image.png) Teddy bears are not just toys, they are symbols of childhood joy, love and security. A round face covered in soft fur, small ears and a sweet smile - a teddy bear instantly wins the hearts of children. This yellow teddy bear in the picture is particularly attractive. It has a pink ribbon around its neck, a small heart design on its chest and a gentle gaze that seems to hide infinite love. Children usually love their teddy bears like friends. They hug them while sleeping, talk to them when they are sad, and make them their companions in games. This harmless toy enriches the world of children's imagination and plays a positive role in mental development. Even adults often keep their beloved teddy bears as souvenirs, which remind them of their happy childhood memories. Teddy bears of different shapes, colors and designs are available in the market. They are popular as gifts for people of all ages. A teddy bear can be a beautiful expression of feelings on birthdays, Valentine's Day or any special occasion. So a teddy bear is not just a toy, it is a silent language of love and affection.

author: nainaztengra
title: The Audit is finally over
text:
It was a stressful week mentally. All I had on my mind was the Audit, which finally got over yesterday. We had a few improvement suggestions but overall, it went very nice and the auditors left happy and on a positive note. It always feels good when a job which has taken up so much of your time, energy and attention gets closed successfully. Now I am going to relax mentally and ease out. This whole week I did not even step out in the evening for my walk. Today I plan to go for a nice long walk by the beach and soak up myself in to the sea air.

Team work is very important for these things, and our team did a fantastic job in putting up the show. Everyone was committed except the MD of the Company who was supposed to be the most committed person. But then he is the MD, we cannot even tell him anything. It was not good on his part that being a senior person he did not take up the responsibility. I did lose my mind on him, but can't do anything much about it. At least the team effort paid off well and I am happy for that. We all have been working on preparing for it since the past 2 weeks and a lot of time and effort has gone in it. If it would have not been successful it would have felt very disappointing.

![image.png](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmQeHLGw37afhM9Qcm1M1x2sPJAvLw2xmZTEUq71jK6Gg7/image.png)
[Source](https://pixabay.com/vectors/employees-workmates-team-colleagues-5577754/)

There were some important points that they highlighted to us which will be beneficial for our own functioning and growth. There was no negative feedback , there were some constructive suggestions which would be good for improvement and growth if implemented.  They were very happy that we were  organized and whatever they asked for was presented efficiently. Our reporting and documents were all in order.   Now we need to work on the suggestion points and in a month's, time again get back to them with our inputs of whether it is feasible for us or not to implement, which is fair enough. It's all going to be over emails and no personal visit, so that can be managed without any stress. 

All is well that ends well.

***Thank you for visiting my blog.*** 👼🏻👼🏻💖💖🌹🌺🌸

![](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmexfm7Y3xHUSDtYLX2gywbcyG8mt4FdQ6Uop8pxAcKw5L/image.png)
***<center>"Unlimited Abundance, Blissful Happiness and Unconditional Love"***</center>

author: nainaztengra
title: 10 years of expat life
text:
It's been 10 years living as an expat in Oman. I always wanted to live out of Country for a few years and hence mean hubby were looking out for opportunities and then this came. But our target was never this long. We thought we would stay for 6 to 8 years and then move back to India. This was always the thought process. But then as time went by, we started settling very comfortably in Oman and made a good life here. Though I miss India a lot and always want to keep going back to my land, but then I must agree that I have got equally fond of Muscat also.

At this point of time, it is confusing for me whether to go back permanently or not, because I like this mix of life between Mumbai and Muscat. It is giving me the best of both worlds. I have a good social life in both the places. Both the places offer me work opportunities. Quality of life is better here in Muscat in terms of pollution and health, this is also one reason why I like here. Air is clean and infrastructure is super. Population is less here so there are open and clean places to move around. I love the mountain and sea life of Muscat. There are lot of benefits, so I think why not continue till I can. But that's for sure I am not going to settle here. May be a couple of more years and then I will take a decision to move back to Mumbai, because the most important, family is there, which is above everything for me.

![20171118_140811.jpg](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQma38d2zHApTiScFqq5z9x9iS9RuQWCwpdAS2FMXxNAWgT/20171118_140811.jpg)

While it all looks good, still at the end of the day expat life is not very colorful also, there are pros and cons of everything. There is always a threat of job security and instability. And too many rules to follow. That time I feel living in our own Country is better, we can live freely and not under constant monitoring. Anyways let's see how many more years I can spend here in Muscat.

***Thank you for visiting my blog.*** 👼🏻👼🏻💖💖🌹🌺🌸

![](https://cdn.steemitimages.com/DQmexfm7Y3xHUSDtYLX2gywbcyG8mt4FdQ6Uop8pxAcKw5L/image.png)
